data.title: Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description:
Sleep comfortably--even when the temperature dips below freezing--with the Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag. Long enough for people up to 6 feet 5 inches in height, this adult sleeping bag is designed to keep you warm in temperatures as low as 0 degrees Fahrenheit. The cotton canvas cover and cozy cotton flannel liner retain heat, so you feel warm from head to toe. For additional comfort, a Thermolock draft tube helps prevent heat from escaping through the zipper, and a ComfortCuff surrounds your face in soft, warm fabric. Fiberlock construction keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A two-way patented ZipPlow zipper system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. And the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N Roll integrated packing system make this backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. The Big Game Sleeping Bag is machine washable and comes with a matching flannel pillow. Stay snugly warm as you slumber on camping and hunting excursions with the Coleman Big Game sleeping bag, which is rated to temperatures as low as -5 degrees Fahrenheit. It's filled with five pounds of Holofill 808 insulation, which offers high-loft, hollow-core fibers that prevent body heat from escaping, thus holding more air inside the bag for good warmth. This super king-sized, rectangular sleeping bag measures 40 by 85 inches, and will fit most sleepers up to 6 foot, 5 inches. Other features include a heavy duty cotton cover material, cotton flannel liner, an included pillow, and Coleman's exclusive set of ComfortSmart sleeping bag technologies, including:
  • ZipPlow: Prevents zipper snags by plowing fabric away from zipper
  • FiberLock: Prevents insulation from shifting, increasing durability
  • ThermoLock: Reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer
  • Zipper GlideTailoring: Allows smooth zipper operation around the corner
  • Comfort Cuff: Surrounds your face with softness
  • Roll Control: Locks bag in place for easier rolling
About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: I would say I love this except for the color and the weight of the outer fabric. It looks like something I've "borrowed" from the military - there is no reason camping can't be colorful and fun. We had nights where the temperature fell to 24 deg F with pouring rain. Even though the sleeping bag got wet due to a leaky tent I remained warm and toasty all night.

REVIEW: I would say I love this except for the color and the weight of the outer fabric. data.title: Coleman 24-Piece Enamel Dinnerware Set, Red
data.description:
Serve up your favorite foods around the campfire or portable stove with the Coleman 24-Piece Enamel Dinnerware Set. Made of double-fired enamel and featuring a timeless red speckled design, this outdoor dinnerware set offers classic style and durability and is dishwasher safe for easy cleaning. This 24-piece set includes four 10-inch dinner plates, four 12-ounce coffee mugs with handles, and four 6-inch bowls, making it a great choice for family meals at picnics, BBQs, and campsites. It also includes a silverware kit with four plastic-handled forks, spoons, and knives stored in a convenient roll-up pouch for easy organization and storage. Ideal for camping, picnicking, and even backyard use, this set of Coleman speckled dinnerware includes everything you need to eat a meal under the stars. The 24-piece set is outfitted with four 10-inch dinner plates, four 10-ounce mugs, and four 6-inch bowls, giving you and three co-campers enough containers for a main course, a side salad, and an after-dinner hot chocolate. In addition, the set comes with four knives, four spoons, and four forks, so you don't need to rely on plastic silverware when eating. Best of all, the set is durable and dishwasher-safe, with a red-speckled enamel surface that holds up to heavy use. The silverware also includes a compact carrying case to keep the cutlery bunched together. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: Good to set cheep paper plates on, Works as it should

data.title: Coleman PowerChill 3000001497 Thermoelectric Cooler with Power Supply (40-Quart), Silver
data.description:
Perfect for the office, dorm, RV, truck or car, the Coleman PowerChill Thermoelectric Cooler is fully portable, plugging into your vehicle's cigarette lighter. The PowerChill's new motor design provides quiet, long-lasting performance, cooling the contents down to 40-degreeF BELOW the surrounding temperature. The PowerChill is designed with many convenient features, including a patented, adjustable door that can open on the right or left side. The cooler can be used in two positions, either as an upright or a chest cooler. An adjustable shelf divider helps keep things organized inside. The PowerChill Cooler's molded handles make it easy to lift and carry. With a 40-quart interior that holds up to 44 cans of soda or beer in addition to snacks, the Coleman portable PowerChill thermoelectric cooler is ideal for the office, dorm, RV, truck, or car. The cooler is compatible with any vehicle's cigarette lighter. As a result, you can bring the PowerChill pretty much anywhere, from the campsite to the tailgater to your backyard BBQ. The PowerChill is equipped with a redesigned motor that provides quiet, long-lasting performance. The motor cools contents down to 40 degrees F below the surrounding temperature, so you don't have to fret once the ice starts melting in your standard cooler. The PowerChill includes several handy features, including a patented adjustable door that can open on both the right and left side, an adjustable shelf divider to keep things organized, and molded handles that make the cooler easy to lift and carry. The PowerChill even functions in two positions: upright or chest style. The PowerChill measures 15 by 21.75 by 17.125 inches (W x H x D). About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: IF THE PLUG DOESN'T MELT FIRST THE FAN WILL STOP WORKING OR WILL STOP BLOWING COOL AIR.  CUSTOMER SERVICE IS BAD, ON HOLD FOR 2 HOURS.. WOULD NOT SEND ME REPLACMENT PLUG BECAUSE I LOST RECEIPT.  OFFERED TO SEND THEM THE MELTED PLUG FIRST. CUSTOMER SUPPORT WAS RUDE AND DOESN'T CARE THAT THESE PLUGS ARE DEFECTIVE.  SHOULD HAVE BOUGHT THE KOOLATRON COOLER.  COLEMAN SHOULD BE ASHAMED TO PUT THIS PRODUCT OUT THERE

data.title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
data.description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText: Good for the survivalist.

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText: Nice tent. Survived it's first few nights in spite of some pretty heavy rain. The vestibule is much smaller that it appears in the picture. The picture in my opinion shows the vestibule to be almost the same footprint as the tent, which would have been nice.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent

DESCRIPTION:
The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..

REVIEW:
Nice tent. Survived it's first few nights in spite of some pretty heavy rain. The vestibule is much smaller that it appears in the picture. The picture in my opinion shows the vestibule to be almost the same footprint as the tent, which would have been nice.

data.title: Coleman Portable Camping Quad Chair with 4-Can Cooler
data.description: Have a cold drink at hand at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with a Coleman Cooler Quad Chair. The cooler built into the armrest holds up to 4 cans and provides easy access to a cold drink once the one in your mesh cup holder is empty. While you sip on your favorite beverage, the fully cushioned seat and back only adds to your relaxation. If you want your hands free, the side pockets work nicely to store your book, magazine or some snacks. When the fun is done, the chair easily folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
data.reviewText: The arms will not stay up. From the first time I used the chair both arms, where they attached to the frame near the back, slide down making them useless. The seat was comfortable but I can't use this without the arms.
data.text:

Coleman Portable Camping Quad Chair with 4-Can Cooler

data.title: Coleman 2-Gallon Party Stacker Jug
data.description: Keep quenched in fun, space-saving style with a Coleman 2 Gallon Party Stacker Jug. The unique stacking design offers vertical storage and use, while its two-gallon capacity is perfect for school parties and family functions. You can even use different colored jugs for different drinks so serving and filling is fast and fun. The removable spigot is easy to clean, and the large bail handle makes carrying easy.Brand: ColemanModel: 3000000736Color: BlueMaterials: PlasticDimensions: 7 inches long x 8 inches wide x 9 inches highConvenient stackable design for multiple beveragesSecure storage and easy-to-clean spigotThis item is not able to be shipped internationally or to a freight forwarding address.
data.reviewText: I was not expecting much from this just needed a water jug that the kids could use on their own. However it surprised me. Easy for kids to use, easy clean up because the spout unscrews and keep thing's cold.

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
data.reviewText: Purchased in March '17. Rinsed out, & pieces of blue plastic came out! Rinsed several more times, then sanitized container. Filled w/ clean distilled sanitized water- Ratio of 50 PPM- certified by heath dept. A week ago, loaded into my truck w/ topper. It sat in 45 degree to 80 degree temps. Went out to load rest of my truck for camping, & my newly carpeted truck bed was soaking wet! My topper DON'T leak. The jug was sitting upright as I had placed it 7 days ago. Don't/ can't find the leak?? Upon arriving- I gave it a leak test. Filled w/ 4 3/4 gall water in  bath tub, w/ dry rag under; & checked next 24 hrs. My rag was 80% wet. Took 2 layers saran wrap & tried upside again. This solved 'lid leak' issue. Then- leak in my truck bed. Next day filled clean & sanitized 2- 1 1/2 gall OJ bottles w/ handles. Sealed lids w/ 2 layers saran wrap; put in truck. Drove about 300 miles on/ off roads. Bottles were laying on sides w/ NO leaks whatsoever. In past, have purchased 1 gall groc store bottles water. Mild temps of 40- 80 degrees- dam things leak. They were laying loose in back of truck but w/ ONLY mild highway driving. Few years ago bought a green [don't remember brand] HEAVY DUTY slim design water bottle. Never used, stored empty in garage/ shop that winter. Next spring, filled & went camping. SOB, it leaked too! ANYBODY PLEASE?? Answers?? Thinking about using the old military metal 5 gall gas cans; but they'd rust. Maybe more OJ bottles? Hell I could buy OJ, dump it out & use!
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue

DESCRIPTION:
Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.

REVIEW:
Purchased in March '17. Rinsed out, & pieces of blue plastic came out! Rinsed several more times, then sanitized container. Filled w/ clean distilled sanitized water- Ratio of 50 PPM- certified by heath dept. A week ago, loaded into my truck w/ topper. It sat in 45 degree to 80 degree temps. Went out to load rest of my truck for camping, & my newly carpeted truck bed was soaking wet! My topper DON'T leak. The jug was sitting upright as I had placed it 7 days ago. Don't/ can't find the leak?? Upon arriving- I gave it a leak test. Filled w/ 4 3/4 gall water in  bath tub, w/ dry rag under; & checked next 24 hrs. My rag was 80% wet. Took 2 layers saran wrap & tried upside again. This solved 'lid leak' issue. Then- leak in my truck bed. Next day filled clean & sanitized 2- 1 1/2 gall OJ bottles w/ handles. Sealed lids w/ 2 layers saran wrap; put in truck. Drove about 300 miles on/ off roads. Bottles were laying on sides w/ NO leaks whatsoever. In past, have purchased 1 gall groc store bottles water. Mild temps of 40- 80 degrees- dam things leak. They were laying loose in back of truck but w/ ONLY mild highway driving. Few years ago bought a green [don't remember brand] HEAVY DUTY slim design water bottle. Never used, stored empty in garage/ shop that winter. Next spring, filled & went camping. SOB, it leaked too! ANYBODY PLEASE?? Answers?? Thinking about using the old military metal 5 gall gas cans; but they'd rust. Maybe more OJ bottles? Hell I could buy OJ, dump it out & use!

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: i can not turn down the flame, when I set it on the low I still high and burns everything you cook.do not buy.

data.title: Coleman C003 Soft Backpack Cooler
data.description: Keep your food and snacks cool from the park to the campsite with a Coleman Backpack Cooler. This stylish bag is large enough to hold 28 cans. Extra padding in the back and waist make it even easier to carry over almost any distance. The BPA- and phthalate-free, heat-welded main compartment is perfect for using with ice and the zippered front pocket lets you store extra snacks and small gear. The bungee cord on the front also provides quick access to other items. Antimicrobial, product-protecting properties are built into the flexible lining to resist odor, mold and mildew, making it easy to clean between uses.
data.reviewText:
It is well made.
I use backpacks like this to bring groceries home on my bike.
I wish someone would make a bigger one, but this is the best one I have found that is a cooler.

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText:
We upgraded to this tent as our kids got older and... well, we did too, and I wanted a cot instead of just an airbed on the ground. We are not serious campers, but wanted something with more space that we could take on our occasional trips (and set up in the backyard from time to time).

So far we have set up and broken it down 3 times, and it seems to be performing well. I just wish that more airflow was possible even with the rainfly up -- you basically have it up and no airflow, or airflow and nothing but mesh over your head -- because even if it is a beautiful day in the 70s, this tent turns into a little heat trap. Not sure if they are all like that. If nothing else, it would be nice if the rainfly was easy to pop on and off or adjust without having to do a full breakdown.

In any case, for our casual use, it has worked out just fine. No leaks, no fabric tearing, nothing crazy like that. The stakes it came with have worked, the included doormat is nice, the storage pockets are adequate, and the room "dividers" are helpful.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent

DESCRIPTION:
Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.

REVIEW:
We upgraded to this tent as our kids got older and... well, we did too, and I wanted a cot instead of just an airbed on the ground. We are not serious campers, but wanted something with more space that we could take on our occasional trips (and set up in the backyard from time to time).

So far we have set up and broken it down 3 times, and it seems to be performing well. I just wish that more airflow was possible even with the rainfly up -- you basically have it up and no airflow, or airflow and nothing but mesh over your head -- because even if it is a beautiful day in the 70s, this tent turns into a little heat trap. Not sure if they are all like that. If nothing else, it would be nice if the rainfly was easy to pop on and off or adjust without having to do a full breakdown.

In any case, for our casual use, it has worked out just fine. No leaks, no fabric tearing, nothing crazy like that. The stakes it came with have worked, the included doormat is nice, the storage pockets are adequate, and the room "dividers" are helpful.

data.title: Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Grill Case for LX Series Grills
data.description: Add even more portability to your Coleman RoadTrip LX Series Grill when you pack it inside a Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Case. This heavy-duty, weather-resistant case includes easy-glide wheels for more mobility and top and side handles for better control. A durable zipper and inner straps secure the grill the entire trip, and inside pockets fit accessories and condiments for whatever meal youre planning to cook up.
data.reviewText:
I chose this because I thought it would be convenient and quality.  I liked the idea of a case that would carry the grill and all the accessories - grill grates, tank adapter, etc.  Unfortunately, the quality is lacking and there's not a good way to stow the handle as far as I can tell.

The very first time I went to put the grill into the case, I noticed first it wouldn't fit.  Ok, so you have to remove the handle - no big deal right?  But there's no place to put the handle - it doesn't fit will in the external mesh pocket, which is better suited for the 20 lb tank adaper hose.  Not a huge deal and I could deal with that.

Unfortunately when I went to strap in the grill inside the case using the supplied straps, one of them broke.  I was lightly cinching down the lower velcro strap and it simply pulled out - came unstitched from the case, rendering it useless.  Not what I expected from what should be a heavy duty case.  Not worth the effort of sending it back, but this shouldn't happen.

data.title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
data.reviewText: Good lightweight bag.  Comfortable shoulder strap.  Almost perfect except netting on each side is not large enough to hold a 16 oz water bottle.

Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).

data.title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
data.description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText: Good backup for car camping cook system.

data.title: Coleman C002 Soft 45 Can Eva Molded Cooler
data.description: Have the convenience of a large cooler when you need one and extra storage room in your home when you don't with the Coleman 45 Can Collapsible Sport Cooler. It's big enough to hold a full day's worth of food and drinks, but still collapses down to the size of a phone book when you're ready to put it away. The zippered closure with an integrated bottle opener ensures you're always ready to enjoy a cold drink. For dry goods, the front mesh pocket lets you carry more items while saving room in the main compartment. After you've packed this cooler full, the adjustable shoulder strap and two side handles make it easy to carry. Antimicrobial, product-protecting properties are built into the flexible lining to resist odor, mold and mildew, making it easy to clean between uses.
data.reviewText: I bought this for vacations because I thought the idea of such a nice size cooler being collapsible was great. I also loved that it had a hard top with drink holders because I thought theyd be perfect for the beach. Sad truth is the look of this cooler is the only thing it has going for it. I just now had an occasion to use it for the first time and before I drove 14 hours with it in my new SUV, I filled it to test for leaks at home with a mix of ice and water. I left it in my kitchen floor and went shopping. Came back two hours later to a huge puddle surrounding the cooler. I picked it up to take it to empty it and water literally poured out from all sides about 2 up from the bottom of the cooler. I guess it would be okay if you were using ice packs. But if you are using actual ice that melts, this thing is absolute crap. Ill admit that Ive had trouble finding a soft cooler that doesnt leak, but Ive never had one that leaked like this one. I expected so much better from a Coleman product.

data.title: Coleman 9-In. Heavy Duty Aluminum Tent Stakes
data.description: You'll Sleep soundly knowing your tent is secured with Coleman 9-in. Heavy duty aluminum tent stakes. These lightweight stakes are specially-designed to hold up to large tents and strong winds. When the fun is done, the lanyard on each tip makes easy work of pulling out the stake. Each package includes four stakes.
data.reviewText:
Did not survive one use in hard-pack gravel.  MSR brand aluminum stakes of a very similar "tri-blade" design did.

The Coleman ones mushroomed at the head, bent at the tie notch, and in one case split up the middle.  Very disappointed but would have written it off as "tough conditions are tough on gear" if the MSR ones had not survived with flying colors.

REVIEW:
Did not survive one use in hard-pack gravel.  MSR brand aluminum stakes of a very similar "tri-blade" design did.

The Coleman ones mushroomed at the head, bent at the tie notch, and in one case split up the middle.  Very disappointed but would have written it off as "tough conditions are tough on gear" if the MSR ones had not survived with flying colors.

data.title: Coleman 9 Cup Percolator
data.description: Brew up enough coffee for everyone to have a couple cups with the Coleman 9 Cup Percolator. The durable percolator includes all the hardware you need brew up the perfect cup of joethe base, tube, basket and basket lid. The double-coated enamel pot resists cracks, the stainless steel rim helps to prevent chipping and the wide base gives the percolator more stability outside. When you're all done, the pot wipes down easily for the next time you use it at home, at a picnic or at the campsite.
data.reviewText: The lid is so tight we tried to pry it off with a screw driver when it first arrived.  Now the top is chipped and if it had hot anything in it, a huge burn would occur.  I use it as a flower pot.  Coleman, what have you not done right?
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 9 Cup Percolator

DESCRIPTION:
Brew up enough coffee for everyone to have a couple cups with the Coleman 9 Cup Percolator. The durable percolator includes all the hardware you need brew up the perfect cup of joethe base, tube, basket and basket lid. The double-coated enamel pot resists cracks, the stainless steel rim helps to prevent chipping and the wide base gives the percolator more stability outside. When you're all done, the pot wipes down easily for the next time you use it at home, at a picnic or at the campsite.

REVIEW:
The lid is so tight we tried to pry it off with a screw driver when it first arrived.  Now the top is chipped and if it had hot anything in it, a huge burn would occur.  I use it as a flower pot.  Coleman, what have you not done right?

data.title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small
data.description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small. Designed to fit small Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 20.4 x 12 x 4.5 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
data.reviewText: This is great for my stove that doesn't have a very good handle and of course there are pockets for tanks on either end.  The stove fits pretty tight.  They could have made the dimensions of the carrying case a little more generous so that you are not stretching the zipper to close the case.
data.text:
TITLE:
 Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small 

DESCRIPTION:
Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small. Designed to fit small Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 20.4 x 12 x 4.5 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).

REVIEW:
This is great for my stove that doesn't have a very good handle and of course there are pockets for tanks on either end.  The stove fits pretty tight.  They could have made the dimensions of the carrying case a little more generous so that you are not stretching the zipper to close the case.

data.title: Coleman 2000016402 Mess Kit, Aluminum, 5-Pc.
data.description: 5 Piece, Aluminum Mess Kit, Includes 8 OZ Cup, 7" Fry Pan, 16 OZ Pot With Lid & 6.5" Dish Pan, Pieces Nest Inside Each Other & Is Covered With A Lid For Easy Storage & Transport.
data.reviewText:
I'm on a tight budget and thought what's the worst that could happen, these end up being as flimsy as everyone says? Surely I can still cook in them no matter how badly constructed they are..

BZZZZZZZ WRONG. I didn't even bother with these I used my friends camping pots to cook stuff. They are a JOKE. The metal is so thin you could puncture it with a knife, I would never dare put these over something hot then grab them later as it's guaranteed to burn your fingers.

They bend/scratch like no other, and worst part is they awkwardly sit inside of each other and can easily fall out unless you put them in a bag.

DON'T BUY THIS JUNK SAVE YOUR MONEY AND GET SOMETHING BETTER FOR A BIT EXTRA. I mean seriously, don't even make the mistake of buying these to 'get buy' if you're on a tight budget, if you're trying to get by just bring pots from home.

data.title: Coleman 70-Quart Xtreme Cooler
data.description: When you need a tough cooler with extraordinary ice retention, choose a Coleman 70-quart Xtreme Cooler. Extra insulation retains ice for five days in temperatures up to 90 degrees Fahrenheit and still performs incredibly well on even hotter days. The insulating foam is free from CFCs, HFCs and HCFCs, which deplete the ozone. Inside, there's plenty of room for all your food and drinks, including 100 cans. A hinged EZ-Clean lid with four cup holders on top keeps your drinks close, with two-way handles make carrying easier. The Xtreme cooler doubles as a bench to provide extra seating.Antimicrobial additive protects liner from odor-causing bacteriaEZ-Clean lid has four cup holdersSturdy lid doubles as an extra seatRust-proof and leak-resistantHolds 2 liter bottles uprightBrand: ColemanSet includes: One (1) coolerColor: Blue/white, Grey/orangeMaterials: Plastic, foamDimensions: 17.7 inches high x 28.1 inches wide x 15.5 inches deepModel: 3000002012
data.reviewText:
Pros:
It's sturdy.
It kept food cold on a 3 day camping trip in 50-70 degree weather.
It's not an awful, gaudy color.

Cons:
It's really heavy when full of food and ice, and I wish I'd purchased one with integrated wheels.

data.title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove
data.description: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory.
data.reviewText:
I was torn between this stove and the MSR Whisperlite international.  I went with this one because it was cheaper, others recommended it and it seemed to have a better design than the other stove with less parts to get gunked up with dirt or grime or whatever else you run into while backpacking.  I like how stable the unit is.  There is this nifty elevated ring you can see in the picture.  You can move it around to level the legs better when you have it sitting on a rock or whatever.  It does have a stupid "hi/lo" sticker instead of painted or engraved markings but if you can't figure where high and where low are after using it once then should you really be out backpacking?

My first use went flawlessly.  It burned with large flames for the first 20 seconds until it was primed then it calmed down.  It burns with a very wide, blue and hot flame even on low.  This will prevent common issue with scorching as seen in the pocket rocket stoves.  The adjustment knob is located in a silly place right under the burner bowl.  As long as you aren't completely inept it should not be a problem though.  The fuel tank did get hot to the touch when on "high" which made me a bit nervous but one rarely cooks on high and the cool mountain air should keep it cool enough.

I fashioned a nice little pack box from the cardboard it came in using masking and packaging tape.  It doesn't fully enclose the stove but it will protect the burner and the valve/knobs from getting broken or bent while in your pack.

4/5 stars because its a little chunky but I expect it to perform better than any other stove.

I went with this one because it was cheaper, others recommended it and it seemed to have a better design than the other stove with less parts to get gunked up with dirt or grime or whatever else you run into while backpacking. My first use went flawlessly. It burned with large flames for the first 20 seconds until it was primed then it calmed down. It burns with a very wide, blue and hot flame even on low. This will prevent common issue with scorching as seen in the pocket rocket stoves. The adjustment knob is located in a silly place right under the burner bowl. As long as you aren't completely inept it should not be a problem though. data.title: Coleman PowerChill 3000001497 Thermoelectric Cooler with Power Supply (40-Quart), Silver
data.description:
Perfect for the office, dorm, RV, truck or car, the Coleman PowerChill Thermoelectric Cooler is fully portable, plugging into your vehicle's cigarette lighter. The PowerChill's new motor design provides quiet, long-lasting performance, cooling the contents down to 40-degreeF BELOW the surrounding temperature. The PowerChill is designed with many convenient features, including a patented, adjustable door that can open on the right or left side. The cooler can be used in two positions, either as an upright or a chest cooler. An adjustable shelf divider helps keep things organized inside. The PowerChill Cooler's molded handles make it easy to lift and carry. With a 40-quart interior that holds up to 44 cans of soda or beer in addition to snacks, the Coleman portable PowerChill thermoelectric cooler is ideal for the office, dorm, RV, truck, or car. The cooler is compatible with any vehicle's cigarette lighter. As a result, you can bring the PowerChill pretty much anywhere, from the campsite to the tailgater to your backyard BBQ. The PowerChill is equipped with a redesigned motor that provides quiet, long-lasting performance. The motor cools contents down to 40 degrees F below the surrounding temperature, so you don't have to fret once the ice starts melting in your standard cooler. The PowerChill includes several handy features, including a patented adjustable door that can open on both the right and left side, an adjustable shelf divider to keep things organized, and molded handles that make the cooler easy to lift and carry. The PowerChill even functions in two positions: upright or chest style. The PowerChill measures 15 by 21.75 by 17.125 inches (W x H x D). About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: The cooler needs supporting ice packs to cool to a safe refrigeration level. I keep an thermometer in it, and without ice support, it doesn't cool below 57 degrees - even when run all day.

data.title: Coleman Camp Oven
data.description:
Bring the joy of fresh-baked muffins, cinnamon rolls, cornbread, and other treats to any campsite with the Coleman Camp Oven. Designed to fit neatly on Coleman propane and liquid fuel camp stoves, this portable oven gives you the ability to bake treats or keep food warm while spending time outdoors. The included interior rack adjusts to 3 heights, while the easy-to-read thermometer lets you bake with confidence. This portable oven is made from smooth aluminized steel that resists corrosion and scratches and wipes clean easily, and it folds flat for convenient carrying and storage when its time to pack up camp. Camping stoves and other cooking accessories have made it possible to cook most things outdoors, but baked goods have always been out of the question. Until now, that is. Made of smooth aluminized steel, the Coleman camping oven lets you bake a batch of muffins, biscuits, or rolls right at the campsite.


The Coleman camping oven is designed to sit on a two- or three-burner camping stove.
The oven doesn't require electric or gas power to work. Instead, campers simply place the oven on top of a Coleman two- or three-burner stove, which provides all the heat you need to bake your favorite dishes. The oven also offers such baking essentials as an adjustable steel bake rack and an easy-to-read thermometer. And when you're done baking, the oven folds flat for easy storage. Made in China, the Coleman camping oven measures 12 by 12 by 12 inches (W x H x D) when standing and weighs 7 pounds. About Coleman

data.reviewText: Burned everything I tried to "bake" in this.  There should be some sort of diffuser on the bottom since it burns the crap out of anything placed in it.

data.title: Coleman 4-Person Instant Tent Rainfly Accessory
data.description: The Coleman 4-Person Instant Tent Rainfly Accessory lets you enjoy added protection from bad weather while increasing airflow inside your tent. Coleman tents are sealed strongly to prevent water from entering, which means campers can sometimes lose airflow when it starts to rain and they zip up the windows. Thats why this rainfly accessory is designed to fit over the top of 8- by 7-foot Coleman 4-Person Instant Tents (sold separately), providing added rain protection while giving you the ability to open windows without having to worry about rainwater getting inside.
data.reviewText: Wish I had read this question before purchasing. This rain fly does not fit the 4 person 59 inch tent.  I got soaked during my vacation. Amazon dropped the ball here should make the description clear which tent model it fits. Called for an exchange and was refused.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 4-Person Instant Tent Rainfly Accessory

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman 4-Person Instant Tent Rainfly Accessory lets you enjoy added protection from bad weather while increasing airflow inside your tent. Coleman tents are sealed strongly to prevent water from entering, which means campers can sometimes lose airflow when it starts to rain and they zip up the windows. Thats why this rainfly accessory is designed to fit over the top of 8- by 7-foot Coleman 4-Person Instant Tents (sold separately), providing added rain protection while giving you the ability to open windows without having to worry about rainwater getting inside.

REVIEW:
Wish I had read this question before purchasing. This rain fly does not fit the 4 person 59 inch tent.  I got soaked during my vacation. Amazon dropped the ball here should make the description clear which tent model it fits. Called for an exchange and was refused.

data.title: Coleman 52 qt. Xtreme Cooler
data.description: The Coleman 52 qt. Xtreme Cooler is perfect for weekend getaways, with the technology to keep ice up to five days in 90-degree weather and the capacity to hold 82 cans. It features two-way handles for easy lifting and carrying, and the rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain allows for no-tilt draining.   You know the name, and you?ll love the company. Coleman is unparalleled in the world of camping and outdoor gear. What started out as an innovation in gas-powered lamps over 100 years ago has grown to an international company that designs and constructs top-quality tents, lanterns, stoves, coolers, sleeping bags, and more. No matter what your outdoor sport: hiking, camping, fishing, hunting, boating, swimming, four-wheeling, relaxing, or tailgating, Coleman has what you need to help get you out there to enjoy it. Durable plastic construction. Features Xtreme 5 technology and Thermozone insulation. Designed to hold up to 82 cans. Includes hinged lid with 4 cup holders and 2-way handle. Dimensions: 27.75L x 14.95W x 17.25H in..
data.reviewText: handle broke after first weekend of use. I ordered a replacement, so hopefully it holds up.

data.title: Coleman QuickBed Elite Extra High Airbed – Queen
data.description: Featuring an extra-tall design for added cushioning, the Coleman QuickBed Elite Extra High Airbed provides a comfortable nights sleep wherever you are. Designed for both indoor and outdoor use, this inflatable mattress has an integrated 4D pump for easily inflating the bed at home or on the go (batteries sold separately). The soft plush top offers a velvet-like sleeping surface, while the ComfortStrong coil construction provides better support and superior comfort, so you wake up feeling rested. Factory tested to be leak-free, the air bed features an AirTight system and Double Lock valve that help keep the mattress at the perfect firmness level. This queen air mattress supports up to 600 pounds and fits standard queen size sheets. It comes with a carry bag for easy storage and portability.
data.reviewText:
Born a 'belly' in the middle of my bed. I do not recommend.

I bought mine at Target.

data.title: Coleman Carlsbad Tent with Screen Room
data.description: Sleep in after the sun rises or put the kids to bed early while the sun is still up with a Coleman Carlsbad Fast Pitch Dome Tent with Screen Room. The Dark Room technology on this tent blocks 90% of sunlight to keep the tent darker if you want to sleep while the sun is up, and it also reduces how much the tent heats up during the day by up to 11.4%.
data.reviewText:
I will start by saying that the design for this tent (I got the 4 person) is pretty good. It took about 15 minutes for 2 people to setup which isn't bad but not that great either,  I believe the 6-person version is adverted as fast pitch while the 4 person is conventional. The screen room does a good job of keeping bugs and dirt out of the main tent although only the middle portion has a rain cover, the sides and front will get wet if it rains.

Now for the reason I bought the tent, the "DarkRoom" Feature. Coleman advertises on their site that the "he Dark Room technology on this tent blocks 97.5% of sunlight." I find this to be extremely misleading. I believe that the darkroom material/coating does block nearly all sunlight however they only made the rain-fly and windows out of that material! The sides of the tent are made form normal tent material that lets in a lot of light. To me this tent is darker than a normal one but only slightly. If I would have known that the tent was not entirely made of the darkroom material I would not have bought it.

Recap: It is a decent overall but I would not buy for the Darkroom feature. Coleman, please make a tent entirely out of this material!
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Carlsbad Tent with Screen Room

DESCRIPTION:
Sleep in after the sun rises or put the kids to bed early while the sun is still up with a Coleman Carlsbad Fast Pitch Dome Tent with Screen Room. The Dark Room technology on this tent blocks 90% of sunlight to keep the tent darker if you want to sleep while the sun is up, and it also reduces how much the tent heats up during the day by up to 11.4%.

REVIEW:
I will start by saying that the design for this tent (I got the 4 person) is pretty good. It took about 15 minutes for 2 people to setup which isn't bad but not that great either,  I believe the 6-person version is adverted as fast pitch while the 4 person is conventional. The screen room does a good job of keeping bugs and dirt out of the main tent although only the middle portion has a rain cover, the sides and front will get wet if it rains.

Now for the reason I bought the tent, the "DarkRoom" Feature. Coleman advertises on their site that the "he Dark Room technology on this tent blocks 97.5% of sunlight." I find this to be extremely misleading. I believe that the darkroom material/coating does block nearly all sunlight however they only made the rain-fly and windows out of that material! The sides of the tent are made form normal tent material that lets in a lot of light. To me this tent is darker than a normal one but only slightly. If I would have known that the tent was not entirely made of the darkroom material I would not have bought it.

Recap: It is a decent overall but I would not buy for the Darkroom feature. Coleman, please make a tent entirely out of this material!

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.

data.title: Coleman 42-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner & Wheels
data.description: The Coleman 42-Can Wheeled Soft Cooler goes with you to the campsite, the BBQ, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It features durable wheels and a retractable handle, so it's easy and comfortable to transport to wherever the party is. The cooler is big enough to hold 42 cans, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! The cooler is soft on the outside and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect the contents from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
data.reviewText: Needed a wheeled portable cooler for our trip to the NJ shore and this one fit the bill. I love the removable liner since I can move things in and out of the house without sand everywhere.  It rolls well the handle is sturdy and it keeps items pretty cool considering ambient air temps were in the low 90s

data.title: Coleman NEW Outdoor Camping Waterproof 6 Person Instant Tent - 10'x9' Foootprint
data.description: The Coleman 6 Person Camping Instant Tent comes with the WeatherTec system that guarantees to keep you dry. This tent has poles that are pre attached to the tent, and takes only one minute to set up and take down. It features a spacious interior, 150D fabric (twice as thick as regular tent fabric) and there's no need for a separate rainfly because the tent is fully taped. The tent can accommodate two queen sized beds, and with a center height of 6 feet, there's plenty of room to move around and get situated. Great for a weekend trip with the whole family.
data.reviewText: Easy to set up and take down.  This tent was great, very roomy for 5 of us.  However, during camping, we experienced a heavy rainstorm, and our tent started to leak from the top middle part.  Next time we'll just place a small tarp over the top.  Other than the leak, it's a good tent.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman NEW Outdoor Camping Waterproof 6 Person Instant Tent - 10'x9' Foootprint

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman 6 Person Camping Instant Tent comes with the WeatherTec system that guarantees to keep you dry. This tent has poles that are pre attached to the tent, and takes only one minute to set up and take down. It features a spacious interior, 150D fabric (twice as thick as regular tent fabric) and there's no need for a separate rainfly because the tent is fully taped. The tent can accommodate two queen sized beds, and with a center height of 6 feet, there's plenty of room to move around and get situated. Great for a weekend trip with the whole family.

REVIEW:
Easy to set up and take down.  This tent was great, very roomy for 5 of us.  However, during camping, we experienced a heavy rainstorm, and our tent started to leak from the top middle part.  Next time we'll just place a small tarp over the top.  Other than the leak, it's a good tent.

data.title: Coleman Divide Plus Spin 525L LED Lantern
data.description: The Coleman Divide Plus Spin 525L LED Lantern features a unique twisting design that lets you rotate the entire lantern to easily change modes.Impact-resistant up to 1 m.
data.reviewText: For sturdy, this in no way competes with the Coleman lanterns of my youth--my earliest memory in the mid-1950s ran on a can of fuel and lit the night when needed in a farm setting, a real tool. The sturdiest part is the metal D-ring handle on the bottom that one uses to engage the "use batteries" or "saves batteries" feature. It is all plastic, and not very impressive plastic, at that--the 4 D batteries are as heavy as the entire housing.  However, this lightness contributes to its ability to purportedly survive a drop on a hard surface up to one meter. Overall, the size is similar to a two liter bottle, minus the screw top. The best thing going for it is the ability to protect the battery life, when not in use and the choice of light output to make the burning lamp last as long as possible. One will have to decide if the investment for one emergency light source is worth the going price on the day one shops.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Divide Plus Spin 525L LED Lantern

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman Divide Plus Spin 525L LED Lantern features a unique twisting design that lets you rotate the entire lantern to easily change modes.Impact-resistant up to 1 m.

REVIEW:
For sturdy, this in no way competes with the Coleman lanterns of my youth--my earliest memory in the mid-1950s ran on a can of fuel and lit the night when needed in a farm setting, a real tool. The sturdiest part is the metal D-ring handle on the bottom that one uses to engage the "use batteries" or "saves batteries" feature. It is all plastic, and not very impressive plastic, at that--the 4 D batteries are as heavy as the entire housing.  However, this lightness contributes to its ability to purportedly survive a drop on a hard surface up to one meter. Overall, the size is similar to a two liter bottle, minus the screw top. The best thing going for it is the ability to protect the battery life, when not in use and the choice of light output to make the burning lamp last as long as possible. One will have to decide if the investment for one emergency light source is worth the going price on the day one shops.

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
data.reviewText: Bought this right before a camping trip. I think you'd have to wash it several time before if you were planning on using it for drinking water. We used it for hand washing and blue flecks of plastic kept coming out.

data.title: Coleman Beach Shade
data.description: " Take your own shady, private space to the beach or park with the Coleman Beach Shade. This pop-up beach tent is made with UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays and durable Polyguard material that is designed to last season after season. The extended floor can be zipped shut for privacy while changing clothes, and the dry line offers a convenient place to hang wet bathing suits and towels. Integrated storage pockets give you a secure place to put your items while you swim or sunbathe. And when it gets hot, the back window can be unzipped to let the breeze in, while the sand bag compartments can be filled as needed to anchor the beach canopy on windy days. The Coleman Beach Shade comes with ground stakes and pre-attached guy lines and can be set up in about five minutes.
data.reviewText:
It is OK once it is put together but it is one pain in the neck to put up.
The sand bag idea was not thought out. It needs to be secure to the ground before you can even start thinking about erecting it. We had a hard time with it using the stakes into dirt so I know it would not be fun putting up on the beach. We wound up ordering another tent. I wish I could return it.
UPDATE 2013 !!!!! We had this tent for a couple a years now and never used it again so we gave it to my Daughter to try and use it on the beach for her two small girls. She could not put it together and gave it back to us.
We would not recommend it and wish we never bought this wast of money tent. ( We hope Coleman reads this and discontinues it )
Brian Cianfrani

data.title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
data.description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText: I was looking for a compact burner for my disaster prep locker. Looks like I found a winner.
