data.title: Coleman Stove Carry Case, Black - 2000020971
data.description: Add even more portability to your Coleman stove when you pack it in a durable Coleman Stove Carry Case. This heavy-duty vinyl case protects the stove while you're storing it and transporting it. The bag's strong, easy-carry handles and durable zipper secure the stove during transport, and two storage pouches keep standard 16.4-oz. propane cylinders close.
data.reviewText:
It is not cordura but rather vinyl, and a super tight fit when new, we'll see how it does after stretching it a few times.  Only could get a single 1 lb bottle in the side pockets, again, maybe better after stretching, I am worried it will split before it gets broken in.
Works with the stove sold here as 3 burner stove, last 4 of model # 4960

data.title: Coleman Propane Lantern Carry Case
data.description: Protect your lantern with the Coleman Propane Lantern Carry Case so it'll be ready to go the next time you head outdoors. This tough case helps protect against dents, scratches and broken globes. Inside, a plastic insert lets you customize the size of the space your lantern rests in. The base has room for extra mantles and miscellaneous other items. The case latch locks securely and is positioned at the top for easy loading and unloading. Simply let your lantern cool, place it inside the case, and you're ready to head on to your next adventure.
data.reviewText: NO PLACE FOR THE BASE OR TANK.

data.title: Coleman Tent Kit
data.description: Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.
data.reviewText: Nice idea, but the items are "child size" for a "child's tent" in the back yard , nothing beyond that----

data.title: Coleman Rainfly Accessory for 6-Person Instant Tent
data.description:
Get better airflow with the Coleman Rainfly Accessory for 6-Person Instant Tent. This rainfly for the Coleman 6-Person Instant Tent, 10 x 9, allows you to crack your windows during a downpour and still stay dry. Get better airflow when camping in your Coleman Instant six-person tent with this Instant tent rainfly. Coleman tents are well sealed and protected from the rain, so campers sometime lose airflow when it starts to pour. Designed for Coleman's 10-by-9-foot Instant tents, the rainfly not only provides added protection from the weather, but it also allows you to crack your windows during a storm and still stay dry. Plus, the rainfly creates a spacious vestibule outside--perfect for stowing shoes and other gear.

data.title: Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower
data.description: Don't miss a shower  even when you're camping  with the Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower. The reservoir holds up to 5 gallons of waterenough for multiple showers. It's constructed of heat-locking material that warms the water when it's left in the sun, even on cool days. The shower features a strong handle for reliable hanging and carrying as well as a handy on/off shower head valve to conserve water.
data.reviewText: I was initially concerned about how well the PVC would hold up but I've had this shoved into the storage area of my Jeep CJ-7 with loads of metal for tops and chairs and fishing gear. The shower has yet to be punctured or really even dis-colored. I'm pleasantly surprised. I go to the beach frequently in the summer and I was looking for a way to get the sand off the kids before I get them back in the Jeep. This fits the bill and the temperature is better than the showers that are provided.

Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower

DESCRIPTION:
Don't miss a shower  even when you're camping  with the Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower. The reservoir holds up to 5 gallons of waterenough for multiple showers. It's constructed of heat-locking material that warms the water when it's left in the sun, even on cool days. The shower features a strong handle for reliable hanging and carrying as well as a handy on/off shower head valve to conserve water.

REVIEW:
I was initially concerned about how well the PVC would hold up but I've had this shoved into the storage area of my Jeep CJ-7 with loads of metal for tops and chairs and fishing gear. The shower has yet to be punctured or really even dis-colored. I'm pleasantly surprised. I go to the beach frequently in the summer and I was looking for a way to get the sand off the kids before I get them back in the Jeep. This fits the bill and the temperature is better than the showers that are provided.

data.title: Coleman StormBeam Dynamo Lantern with Radio and Cell Phone Chargers
data.description: Strong, reliable, and easy to use, Coleman's StormBeam Lanterns are designed specifically for use during power outages, storms, and other emergencies. The StormBeam Dynamo Lantern With Radio and Cell Phone Chargers has a built-in crank to recharge the lantern with no outside power source, so you'll always have light when you need it. Just one minute of hand-cranking yields 15 minutes of high-powered light. This StormBeam Lantern features a built-in FM radio with scan function, to help you track weather reports and developing emergency situations. What's more, the Lantern comes with adapters for the 6 most common types of cell-phone chargers (stored inside lantern cap), so you can use the Lantern crank to charge your phone -- vital in an emergency. The Dynamo's bright 0.7-watt LED sheds strong, reliable light and will never need to be replaced. The lantern's battery pack (included) offers consistent charging. The StormBeam Lantern's convenient molded-grip carrying handle folds under the lantern when not in use, for compact storage.
data.reviewText: Mine came defective and drove me nuts for awhile cause I would follow the instructions exactly and the light would stay on for like 1 min or 2 then start dimming to a worthless glow. Finally I just upped and bought one at Wal-mart the same exact thing and it worked fine. So as with all electrical stuff beware ordering them on internet as they can come beat up and may be a hassle to return. But Amazon has good customer service so your covered chief. Thank You Amazon .. never have so many owed so much to so few....

The StormBeam Dynamo Lantern With Radio and Cell Phone Chargers has a built-in crank to recharge the lantern with no outside power source, so you'll always have light when you need it. Just one minute of hand-cranking yields 15 minutes of high-powered light. This StormBeam Lantern features a built-in FM radio with scan function, to help you track weather reports and developing emergency situations. What's more, the Lantern comes with adapters for the 6 most common types of cell-phone chargers (stored inside lantern cap), so you can use the Lantern crank to charge your phone -- vital in an emergency. The Dynamo's bright 0.7-watt LED sheds strong, reliable light and will never need to be replaced. The lantern's battery pack (included) offers consistent charging. The StormBeam Lantern's convenient molded-grip carrying handle folds under the lantern when not in use, for compact storage.

REVIEW:
Mine came defective and drove me nuts for awhile cause I would follow the instructions exactly and the light would stay on for like 1 min or 2 then start dimming to a worthless glow. Finally I just upped and bought one at Wal-mart the same exact thing and it worked fine. So as with all electrical stuff beware ordering them on internet as they can come beat up and may be a hassle to return. But Amazon has good customer service so your covered chief. Thank You Amazon .. never have so many owed so much to so few....

data.title: Coleman Dunnock Cold Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description: Ideal for camping at higher altitudes or in cooler months, the Coleman Dunnock Cold-Weather Sleeping Bag helps you sleep comfortably in temperatures as low as 20F. This sleeping bag features Fiberlock construction that keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A cotton cover and ultra-soft cotton flannel liner also keep you warm, as well as a Thermolock draft tube that prevents heat loss through the zipper. The bottom of the bag unzips to provide extra ventilation on warmer nights. The 2-way patented ZipPlow system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. Plus, the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N' Roll integrated packing system make this lightweight backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. This sleeping bag has a spacious design that can accommodate most people up to 5 feet 11 inches tall and is machine washable for easy cleaning.
data.reviewText: Nothing used for cold weather should be cotten.  You want poly or down fill.  I'm not saying it's not a comfortable bag and will be fine for a cool weather camping..but you are risking a good nights sleep if you use this for cold weather camping.

data.title: Coleman 5-Gallon Beverage Cooler
data.description: Keep the group cool and hydrated on sweltering days with a Coleman 5 Gallon Beverage Cooler. The side handles make carrying easy, and the screw-top lid stays secure during transport or while lifting this beverage cooler on a table top or bench. Once you're set up at your destination, the entire group can fill their cups from the easy-use faucet and stay refreshed all day long.
data.reviewText: Holds ice ok, but the lid is a nightmare to get on. The grooves aren't deep enough to get traction for your fingers. Bought coleman vs igloo cuz typically their coolers are better, but the lid design is a major flaw. Always frustrating to put on or remove. Also, the spout on mine is twisted. Had to pull the nut and attempt to remount it. Still not perfect. It comes out at the 7 o'clock position. Probably just bad quality control. Needs a redesign.

data.title: Coleman 24-Hour 16-Can Cooler
data.description: Keep your food and drinks cold for an extended time when you bring a Coleman 24-Hour 16-Can Cooler to the park or the campsite. Extra insulation keeps your items cold for up to 24 hours. The zippered main compartment also helps seal in the cold, while a front pocket lets you store extra snacks. No matter where you go or what extras you bring, an adjustable shoulder strap makes it easy to carry. Once you're back home, the antimicrobial liner resists odor, mold, mildew and fungus on the product, making it easy to clean between each adventure.
data.reviewText: I had high hopes for this cooler, my expectations were not met. I used this for 2 days at work (12hr shifts indoors 75F). I tested this with 2 different sets of ice packs. I used mostly pre-chilled foods and drinks with just a couple things like pretzels being not pre-chilled and both lunches were very similar in amounts and items. The first day was with a Arctic Ice Alaskan Series Reusable Cooler Pack, 2.5-Pound, the second day was with 4 of the Set of 5 Cooler Shock lunch bag size ice packs - high performance 18 degree Fahrenheit using phase change science to achieve 8-10 hour cooling - avoid spoilage so you can eat your lunch!. The cooler failed to get down to fridge temps(below 40F), and failed to maintain temps with both sets of cooler packs. I took temperatures with a Elitech RC4 Temperature Data Logger Recorder with External Temperature Sensor every 5 min both days. I attached a pic of the graph. Over all I cannot recommend this cooler, it does not have enough insulation.

data.title: Coleman 40-Can Collapsible Soft Cooler
data.description: Have the convenience of a large cooler when you need one and extra storage room in your home when you don't. The Coleman 40 Can Collapsible Cooler is big enough to hold almost two cases of soda and then collapse down to the size of a phone book when you're ready to put it away. A bungee cord on the back, front pocket and external lid storage make keeping snacks and utensils convenient. An adjustable shoulder strap makes it easy to carry. Antimicrobial, product-protecting properties are built into the flexible lining to resist odor, mold and mildew, making it easy to clean between uses. Heat-welded seams help prevent soft liner leaks.
data.reviewText: Great lunch box for work. My only complaint is that the rectangle board that you push to the bottom to give it it's shape is absorbent. Anything that spills in the bag that piece absorbed and after a few weeks had to toss it. I still have the bag though, it's just not always box shaped.

data.title: Coleman Tent Kit
data.description: Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.
data.reviewText: As other reviewers have said, this mallet stinks. Literally. It smells awful. I'm not sensitive to smells so I thought I might not have an issue with it, but it's bad. I've had it sitting on my deck for three weeks and it's still off gassing. The rest of the components are convenient and nice to have. Coleman needs to replace that mallet though.

data.title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
data.description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText: sturdy little single burner stove,love it

data.title: Coleman Instant Dome Tent
data.reviewText:
Good quality tent overall. But...
1. the main selling point; its quick set up; is exaggerated. You can save a minute or two by not fumbling around with tent poles. However you still have to hammer in stakes in the 4 corners as well as the guy lines. Very little time saved there. Also, the tent must be folded up I a very specific way to fit back in the bag. Took me a few tries to figure out the fold up order.
2. If the permanently attached poles break, you may be stuck with a tent that cant be pitched.
3. The built in rain fly is a pain. It is too easily caught in the door zipper. And offers no additional material extending outward from the door like some models.
4. Coleman claims this is a 3 person tent. As usual, that notion is laughable. Yes, you can squeeze 3 adults in. But only if everyone sleeps in a full straightened out position with no movement at all.
5. No bargain on price considering the above limitations. I would have had the same camping experience with my traditional Coleman 3 person tent at about $40 less.

Coleman Instant Dome Tent

REVIEW:
Good quality tent overall. But...
1. the main selling point; its quick set up; is exaggerated. You can save a minute or two by not fumbling around with tent poles. However you still have to hammer in stakes in the 4 corners as well as the guy lines. Very little time saved there. Also, the tent must be folded up I a very specific way to fit back in the bag. Took me a few tries to figure out the fold up order.
2. If the permanently attached poles break, you may be stuck with a tent that cant be pitched.
3. The built in rain fly is a pain. It is too easily caught in the door zipper. And offers no additional material extending outward from the door like some models.
4. Coleman claims this is a 3 person tent. As usual, that notion is laughable. Yes, you can squeeze 3 adults in. But only if everyone sleeps in a full straightened out position with no movement at all.
5. No bargain on price considering the above limitations. I would have had the same camping experience with my traditional Coleman 3 person tent at about $40 less.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: This axe is cheap yes but not worth it. The axe feels cheap and is made cheap. My axe came with rust on it and chips and notches in the blade just save your money and buy a better axe for a little more money! I have a Fiskar axe and hatchet now and they are way better products might cost a little more but worth it!

data.title: Coleman Cooler Standard Drain Plug Assembly - 1" Shaft Length
data.reviewText: So if you're replacing your cooler drain assembly, this definitely fits.  The problem is that if your replacing the drain and the original drain assembly has a large nut on the interior to back up the gasket, this isn't the same.  Yes, it fits, but it's difficult to tighten in order to snug up the new gasket.  I haven't pressed this repair into service yet, so the jury is still out.  If you have a large hex key, you can tighten this assembly properly by placing the hex key on the interior fitting.  It's hollow (as it should be) but it is a large hex opening.  I don't know about you, but I've got all the tools I thought I would ever need, and I just don't have that large a hex key.  As far as I can tell, if you have a large enough SAE sized hex key you should be able to get this good and snug enough to compress the interior rubber washer.  I just didn't have one large enough.  I guess I just don't have enough tools.  Well, I'm off to buy more tools.  I hope this review was helpful.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Cooler Standard Drain Plug Assembly - 1" Shaft Length

DESCRIPTION:
For use on the following Coleman Cooler models: 5227, 5235, 5239, 5240, 5241, 5245, 5248, 5254, 5255, 5256, 5262, 5264, 5265, 5269, 5272, 5274, 5276, 5283, 5284, 5286, 5294, 5299, 5296, 5299, 5848, 5850, 5894, 5896, 6262, and 6270

REVIEW:
So if you're replacing your cooler drain assembly, this definitely fits.  The problem is that if your replacing the drain and the original drain assembly has a large nut on the interior to back up the gasket, this isn't the same.  Yes, it fits, but it's difficult to tighten in order to snug up the new gasket.  I haven't pressed this repair into service yet, so the jury is still out.  If you have a large hex key, you can tighten this assembly properly by placing the hex key on the interior fitting.  It's hollow (as it should be) but it is a large hex opening.  I don't know about you, but I've got all the tools I thought I would ever need, and I just don't have that large a hex key.  As far as I can tell, if you have a large enough SAE sized hex key you should be able to get this good and snug enough to compress the interior rubber washer.  I just didn't have one large enough.  I guess I just don't have enough tools.  Well, I'm off to buy more tools.  I hope this review was helpful.

data.title: Coleman Instant Cabin
data.reviewText: I got this tent to replace a Eureka Timberline, my wife and I wanted a tent we could stand in. This tent is a haven for two, plenty of room for a queen size air bed and still have room to move around. We just took it camping at the state park for 4 days and didn't get a chance to test the weatherproofing, I would recommend getting the additional fly for it as it adds protection and looks good on the tent. I do agree that it should have been included and it fits in the tent bag with the tent when rolled. The attached poles are sturdy and the zippers are durable so far. After researching several tents like it I think I made the best choice for the money.

data.title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
data.reviewText:
I am an avid tent camper, having camped in wilderness conditions with temperatures as low as 17 degrees F up to 110 degrees (at night). Ive gone through more tents than I can remember and there are things Ive loved about some of them and other things Ive disliked. This is the second instant tent Ive owned and resisted buying it exactly for that feature  the first one was too delicate and didn't even last a season.

Starting at the very beginning, the absolutely essential thing a person should do upon receiving a new tent is to set it up in their backyard (or other appropriate place if an apartment dweller) and be sure all of the parts are there, that they fit, that all zippers work and that there are no obvious defects.  The second thing?  And before that first camping trip, spray a garden hose at your new tent to be assured that it is reasonably water-tight.

Passing that test, you can approach your first camping trip with a fair degree of confidence.  My issue of this tent passed perfectly.

Heres what I like about this tent, especially as compared to other tents I have owned:

1. The fabric is a bit heavier than most tents in the sub-$300 price range. You can feel the thickness.
2. The zippers are stronger feeling and being D shaped they don't have any convoluted twists and turns.
3. The tent does not need a separate storm/rain cover. The second night we were camping it rained most of the night and the inside of the tent was bone dry.
4. It does set up about as fast as Coleman says it will. I set it up the first time by myself and it took about 3 minutes. With another person it truly can be erected in just over a minute.
5. The high-wind performance is phenomenal. Our first night ever using the tent the winds averaged 20 MPH with gusts close to 40 MPH and higher just after sunrise. The tent bucked and bowed and swayed and felt like a thrill ride at a theme park but with only 6 cheap stakes it held firm. It flexed like crazy but never lost its footing.
6. The walls are almost vertical with very high ceilings.  Yes! Finally a tent I can stand up in and walk around. During the peak of the rain we were able to sit in the living room in our camp chairs with plenty of room to relax.

Here are some observations:

1. I love that the bedroom can be closed off to help keep it dark and the living room can be almost totally opened up with screened windows for the maximum view. But I have mixed feelings that there is no skylight at all  I love to fall asleep gazing up at the stars and this tent does not make that possible.
2. For a tent this large there aren't enough pockets for personal belongings (in the bedroom) and no pass-throughs for shoes and such.
3. At 23 lbsthis tent is big and heavy. I cant fault Coleman for that, there would be no way to make a tent this large with the extra mechanisms to be self-erecting and make it light.

A good tent can cost $500 - $600 (or more) and frankly it does not cost that much to make a tent. At about $190 when reviewed it is a LOT of tent for the price and well worth the money. Its not quite 5-stars because it lacks a few things that a company like Coleman would have been expected to provide and what could be a deal breaker for some people who camp a lot.  But its wind/rain protection are admirable and the quick setup and teardown really make handling such a large tent much more pleasant. Overall, at the selling price it may not be a lifetime tent but it is a very good value and the quality of materials is marginally better than most other tents Ive seen anywhere near the price.

data.title: Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart
data.description: Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.
data.reviewText: I didn't buy mine on Amazon but have had one for around 4 years.  Mine is all stainless steel which gets hot but somehow keeps ice for days.  I have a Coleman 120 quart that advertises keeps ice for 6 days. Not even close.  After 3 days all the ice was melted in the 120 quart and steel one still had ice.  The big disappoint for me is my latch is steel but the hook is plastic.  After a few uses I could open the lid without turning the latch open.  Important for float trips if you turn the canoe over.  All my stuff in the cooler fell out into the river.  I hate plastic parts on pieces that get used repeatedly.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart

DESCRIPTION:
Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.

REVIEW:
I didn't buy mine on Amazon but have had one for around 4 years.  Mine is all stainless steel which gets hot but somehow keeps ice for days.  I have a Coleman 120 quart that advertises keeps ice for 6 days. Not even close.  After 3 days all the ice was melted in the 120 quart and steel one still had ice.  The big disappoint for me is my latch is steel but the hook is plastic.  After a few uses I could open the lid without turning the latch open.  Important for float trips if you turn the canoe over.  All my stuff in the cooler fell out into the river.  I hate plastic parts on pieces that get used repeatedly.

data.title: Coleman EasyStay Single High Airbed
data.description: Pack lightweight support with a Coleman Quickbed Lite Airbed. With its internal coils, you ll rest on superior comfort and support, while a soft top offers relaxation for sleepers who are on the go. Once you determine your perfect firmness, it will be yours all night long with the airtight system that's guaranteed not to leak and the easy to use double lock valve that also helps inflate and deflate the bed quickly. When you're ready to pack up, the built in storage system makes putting the bed away a matter of folding it, rolling it and storing it.
data.reviewText: Tried to use this over the weekend camping and within minutes of getting it aired up, it went flat! I am NOT impressed with this airbed what so ever. I can honestly say with everything I have ever ordered on Amazon this was the worst purchase ever.

data.title: Coleman Camp Oven
data.description:
Bring the joy of fresh-baked muffins, cinnamon rolls, cornbread, and other treats to any campsite with the Coleman Camp Oven. Designed to fit neatly on Coleman propane and liquid fuel camp stoves, this portable oven gives you the ability to bake treats or keep food warm while spending time outdoors. The included interior rack adjusts to 3 heights, while the easy-to-read thermometer lets you bake with confidence. This portable oven is made from smooth aluminized steel that resists corrosion and scratches and wipes clean easily, and it folds flat for convenient carrying and storage when its time to pack up camp. Camping stoves and other cooking accessories have made it possible to cook most things outdoors, but baked goods have always been out of the question. Until now, that is. Made of smooth aluminized steel, the Coleman camping oven lets you bake a batch of muffins, biscuits, or rolls right at the campsite.


The Coleman camping oven is designed to sit on a two- or three-burner camping stove.
The oven doesn't require electric or gas power to work. Instead, campers simply place the oven on top of a Coleman two- or three-burner stove, which provides all the heat you need to bake your favorite dishes. The oven also offers such baking essentials as an adjustable steel bake rack and an easy-to-read thermometer. And when you're done baking, the oven folds flat for easy storage. Made in China, the Coleman camping oven measures 12 by 12 by 12 inches (W x H x D) when standing and weighs 7 pounds. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: The temp gage shows approximately 50 degrees cooler than the actual temp. Made with very thin material. It works with some practice and after determining the actual temp.

data.title: Coleman Tent Light
data.description:
The Coleman Tent Light is a great addition to any camping trip. The strong, built-in magnet allows for easy attachment without damaging the tent fabric. The light operates on four AA batteries (sold separately). The powerful xenon bulb is replaceable. A great addition to any camping trip, the Coleman tent light will keep your tent illuminated during the evening hours. The light attaches to your tent poles easily and securely via the built-in magnet, so you needn't worry about damaging your tent fabric. The light's powerful, replaceable Krypton bulb, meanwhile, offers plenty of light for reading, playing games, or chatting. Best of all, the light--which operates on four AA batteries (sold separately)--won't get in your way while you sleep or hang out, unlike standard lanterns. Made in China, the light includes an easy on/off switch. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: Use a lot of energy

data.title: Coleman Carlsbad Tent with Screen Room
data.description: Sleep in after the sun rises or put the kids to bed early while the sun is still up with a Coleman Carlsbad Fast Pitch Dome Tent with Screen Room. The Dark Room technology on this tent blocks 90% of sunlight to keep the tent darker if you want to sleep while the sun is up, and it also reduces how much the tent heats up during the day by up to 11.4%.
data.reviewText:
Pluses: 1. It is a dark room but only for short time (see picture: dark coating from fabric is peeling off after 3 days). 3). 2. Fast set up (10 min.). 3. Took a heavy rain without drip inside. See update below. Minuses. 1. Seems are week and tearing a part. It will not be a long time use tent. Needs repair after first night. It will not take well a wind above 10 m/h.

Update after 19 days of use: tent started leak after 3th rain.

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: The Coleman Sundome 6, 10'x10', dome tent is part of the classic Sundome series of tents from Coleman. The Sundome 6 sleeps 6 happy campers and includes a large door for easy entry/exit with a hooded fly to provide ventilation even in rain. A large rear hooded window also provides airflow for cross ventilation. Mesh vents on the roof provide additional ventilation and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against a leaky floor. The coated polyester fabrics on the fly and walls are rugged and durable. Like all Coleman Sundome tents it is easy to set up or take down and features an Electrical Access Port, a ground vent and an interior mesh storage pocket.Freestanding design is easy to set up and take down.2 windows and roof vents for maximum ventilation and comfort.Mesh roof aids in ventilation and reduces condensation.Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.Polyester fly is tough and durable and won't stretch when wet.1 zippered Electrical Access Port to run AC power into the tent.1 vented Cool Air Port for increased ventilation and comfort.1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient clip and continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import.
data.reviewText:
the tent has some nice features. The only bad thing is lack of ventilation with the rain fly on. I guess if I wasn't worried about rain I could have left the rainfly off-
Roomy for 2 people with COTS but sleeping bag users might bet 6 in it- would be very tight.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: I bought a Coleman Camp Axe a year or so ago for camping and for mostly breaking down firewood into kindling. I bought it because it was cheap and had decent reviews. Overall that axe seemed fine, and for the price I suppose it is. However, I had to replace it. The cons with the Coleman are that it seems as though the steel is soft--it shows major dents from hitting who knows what--and most importantly it doesn't come with any realistic sheath to protect the blade. Sheaths on Amazon will double the price of this, if not more. Based upon the damage to the Coleman and wanting a sheath to protect the blade I ended up buying the Gerber 31-000914 Camp Axe II High Performance Axe. I've used it now on one camping trip, and I have to say I love it. It seems to be an almost perfect tool. No comparison to the Coleman at all.

With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency. REVIEW: I bought a Coleman Camp Axe a year or so ago for camping and for mostly breaking down firewood into kindling. I bought it because it was cheap and had decent reviews. Overall that axe seemed fine, and for the price I suppose it is. However, I had to replace it. The cons with the Coleman are that it seems as though the steel is soft--it shows major dents from hitting who knows what--and most importantly it doesn't come with any realistic sheath to protect the blade. Sheaths on Amazon will double the price of this, if not more. Based upon the damage to the Coleman and wanting a sheath to protect the blade I ended up buying the Gerber 31-000914 Camp Axe II High Performance Axe. I've used it now on one camping trip, and I have to say I love it. It seems to be an almost perfect tool. No comparison to the Coleman at all.

data.title: Coleman Micro Quad LED Mini Lantern
data.description: Clever, versatile, and powerful, the Coleman Microburst Mini-Lantern is four lights in one. Give one to each family member at the campsite or nighttime event! The four detachable light "pods" can be used separately -- each has its own On/Off switch, runs for up to 12 continuous hours and sheds 10 lumens of light -- or they can be docked into the base station for a single bright mini-lantern. When docked, the lights are powered by four AA batteries, and run up to 25 continuous hours, shedding 40 lumens of light. Each pod sports a pocket clip, elastic rubber loop, and magnet -- so it can be attached to virtually anything! Four colored lenses (red, amber, blue, and green) store in the base and easily clip onto each pod, so you can assign a color to each person carrying one and easily identify them in the dark.
data.reviewText: Hardly ever used it and the battery door on the back of one of the lights already broke off and it doesn't stay connected to the unit anymore. It needs a ton of different batteries that have to be replaced at the same time

data.title: Coleman 48-Quart Performance Cooler
data.description:
Keep your food and drinks cold, and keep the fun going, for up to 3 days when you bring a Coleman 48 Quart Performance Cooler. Large enough to hold 63 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the 2-way handles make getting to your destination easy. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant drain makes it easy to empty any excess water, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. The Coleman 48-Quart Chest Cooler combines the easy-to-carry convenience of two-way handles with the rugged durability of a hard plastic exterior and hinged lid, making it the perfect cooler for afternoons on the beach or on the game field. This innovative cooler is constructed with Thermozone insulation to keep food and beverages fresh and cold. This American-made cooler also features a rust-proof, leak-proof drain for easy emptying and cleaning, and it has the capacity to hold up to 63 12-ounce cans. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: It keeps things good and cool, but the darn thing is almost impossible to open!

data.title: Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad
data.description: Get a good night's rest with the Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad. The closed cell foam protects you from uncomfortable surfaces and helps insulate you from the cold ground.
data.reviewText:
If this pad had been a few inches longer it would have gotten 5 stars from me.

It's not super cushy, so you need a flat and smooth place to lay it on, but it does provide some cushioning against the hard ground.  We got this to use as an isolation mat, not for its cushioning, and it does that job exceptionally well.  It's wide enough to easily fit a "thick" adult on it, from shin to head (I am 5'10") and keep the ground from sucking all the heat out of you.  The bottom side of me was the warmest side of me while laying on this mat, and that was in 35* nights, so I would guess that even at cooler temperatures it would do a good job of insulating your body from the cold ground.
For the price, it can't be beat for its insulating properties, but if you are looking for a "feather bed" affect, this isn't the mat for you.

Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad

Get a good night's rest with the Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad. The closed cell foam protects you from uncomfortable surfaces and helps insulate you from the cold ground.

For the price, it can't be beat for its insulating properties, but if you are looking for a "feather bed" affect, this isn't the mat for you.

data.title: Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter with LED Lighting, 10 x 10 Feet
data.description:
The Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter with LED Lighting, 10 x 10 Feet, lets you enjoy the outdoors any time of day or night. Great for picnics, barbecues, campouts, and more, this sun shelter is made with UV Guard material that offers UPF 50+ protection from the sun's harmful rays. The lightweight telescoping poles with pinch-free Comfort Grip technology can be extended comfortably without having to reach over your head, allowing you to set up the canopy tent quickly. Once assembled, the canopy offers stable shelter on grass, concrete, and asphalt thanks to its welded steel feet. When the sun goes down, the fun can keep going thanks to the 6 built-in CPX LED lights that supply 400 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by 4 D-cell batteries or one CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge (both sold separately). When its time to pack up, the shelter folds down quickly and fits easily inside most cars. Whether you're camping, dining in the backyard, or hosting a wedding reception, the Coleman Instant Canopy makes an ideal companion. The Instant Canopy is Coleman's most innovative canopy, with a three-step setup that takes three minutes or less to pitch.


The canopy is easy to set up via a 3-step, 3-minute process.
No more fiddling with hard-to-interpret pole assemblies or poorly designed attachment systems. More importantly, the canopy comes with four built-in ultra-bright LED fixtures at the top of the shelter. With light emanating out from the ceiling, you can dine at dusk with full overhead illumination and dance after dark without bringing out multiple lanterns. The light system even comes with a convenient dimmer switch, so you can choose low-light ambience all the way up to bright task lighting. The lighting system operates on four D batteries (sold separately), but is also compatible with Coleman's CPX 6 battery power system. The canopy is sturdily constructed, with a 28mm steel frame and a tough cover that's made using UVGuard fabric, which provides a superior 50+ ultraviolet protection factor. And Coleman added pinch-free Comfort Grips to the frame, so you can protect your fingers during setup and takedown. Just a single squeeze locks or releases the frame for fast setup or teardown. The Instant Canopy offers 10 feet by 10 feet of shade and a center height of 9 feet 4 inches. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: This canopy destroyed our camping trip.  I spent almost $200.00 on this Coleman product and thought it would be capable of blocking rain and being the shelter it claims to be.  Instead, it COLLAPSED after 30 minutes of hard rain and 2 years of use.  We had enjoyed it immensely before the bad weather came.  It works great as a sun blocker!  But this product CAN NOT protect you in a storm.  All of our equipment we were storing under the canopy was soaked and some of it destroyed.  The frame literally snapped in two places!  Our Coleman tent did the same a few years ago while we were asleep in it.  Never buying Coleman products again!  Cheap and unreliable.  Destroyed a $1,000 camping trip and over $400 in equipment (radios, flashlights, solar chargers!!!)  Buyer beware: this canopy is a huge liability.

data.title: Coleman Oak Point Cool-Weather Big and Tall Sleeping Bag
data.description: Stay comfortable during chilly nights outdoors with the Coleman Oak Point Cool-Weather Big and Tall Sleeping Bag. This lightweight sleeping bag has heat-locking construction with a polyester cover, Coletherm insulation, and a soft cotton flannel lining to keep you warm in temperatures as low as 30F. Fiberlock construction keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. For additional warmth, a Thermolock draft tube helps prevent heat from escaping through the zipper. The 2-way patented ZipPlow system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. And thanks to its Roll Control design and Quick Cord patented no-tie closure, this backpacking sleeping bag is easy for one person to pack without assistance. This adult sleeping bag can accommodate most people up to 6 feet 4 inches tall and is machine washable for easy cleaning.
data.reviewText: Needs more filling for posted temps and needs a collar or drawer string at the neck to cut drafts

data.title: Coleman 2000014864 Mosquito Head Net
data.description: Enjoy the outdoors without the pests when you're wearing a Coleman Mosquito Head Net. The fine mesh stops insects from biting, while still allowing you to see nature's beauty. The Mosquito Head Net is designed especially for wide, all-around brim hats, but still fits other styles well. Keep flying insects out when hiking or working in mosquito- or gnat-ridden territory with the Coleman insect head net. The net is designed to fit comfortably over most headgear, and is made with a 1mm maximum netting grade.
data.reviewText:
I've been using this product almost every afternoon when I take about a 3 hour hike through the woods, and so far so good!

As long as you try to be diligent on not catching the head net on branches and stuff, this product should last you a great long while.

I would recommend this to anyone who is like me and prefers to not wear DEET bug sprays.

data.title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium
data.description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium. Designed to fit Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 21.5 x 14 x 4.8 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
data.reviewText:
I really like the grill and the burner on the side. I think having both features is key and the stove cooks well. It makes for great camping meals and we have been happy with it's cooking ability. However, there are some annoying design flaws that could be easily addressed.

Firstly, the propane arm that screws into the side of the grill is difficult to install unless you have really tiny fingers. It's hard to screw it in because there is hardly any texture on the knob to give you a good grip, and the knob is small, barely giving you enough room to get a good hold on it and turn. The knob needs more texture and more room to grip so you can screw it in with ease. You will get it on eventually, but it can be a pain.

Secondly, I was aware of the carry case issues, and I shopped around to make sure I was getting the right size bag, the grill-stove carry case, not the grill case... And then I got the bag and it was indeed too small. It needs about one more inch on the width and height. My question to Coleman is, if both bags are so similar in size, why not just make the larger of the two and avoid this frustration? The grill will fit in the bag, but you need to stretch it over the knobs. I don't know if there was a mixup at the Coleman factory or Amazon warehouse, but buying the right case should be this confusing for the consumer. Even with all the analysis of model numbers I had to do, I still managed to get the wrong case!

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Camping Tent with Built-in Closet | Tenaya Lake Cabin Tent with Fast Pitch Setup
data.description: Enjoy some comforts from home while camping with the Coleman Tenaya Lake Fast Pitch 8-Person Cabin Tent with Closet. This camping tent includes a built-in closet with shelves and a hanger bar, making it easy to keep your clothes and gear organized without having to dig through your backpack. Thanks to pre-attached, color-coded top poles and hub, fast-fit feet, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension, this cabin tent with the Fast Pitch system assembles 55 percent quicker than similar Coleman tents with a conventional setup. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you and your gear stay dry, while the included rainfly offers extra weather protection. This backpacking tent offers enough room for two queen air beds and comes with a room divider for added privacy and storage pockets to keep your items stored away. An E-Port lets you easily bring power inside the tent. A hinged door lets you enter and exit the tent effortlessly, and Illumiline reflective guy lines offer greater visibility at night, so you wont accidentally trip in the dark. And when its time to pack up, the 8-person tent fits neatly inside the included wheeled carry bag for easy storage and transportation.
data.reviewText: it takes at least 3 people to put it up.

data.description: Coleman Classic Propane Stove About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText:
Received one of these as part of a package deal with our little camper.  Honestly, only came to own one due to the package deal.  Had I set out to buy one I probably would have picked one of a more modern design that was more expensive.  I read the reviews here, including the leading review that discusses cleaning the valves.  I did that little repair (sure enough, my valves were caked with dark grease), and fired it up.  I have to say, I'm impressed with the heat this little monster puts out.  I was able to bring a small pot of water to a boil in only a few minutes.  Surprisingly quickly!

Even after cleaning the valves the flame adjustment is pretty touchy.  Only a small amount of movement at the knobs makes large changes to the flame.  Regardless, you can turn the flame down quite a bit.  The heat range seems to be from "hot" to "surface of the sun".  No using this as a food warmer, for sure.

Regardless.  Yes, is inexpensive.  Yes, there are some parts on it that are not rugged.  You'll have to be careful with the little wind screens on the sides as the little tabs that hold them on would bend easily.  The whole thing is made of fairly thin stamped metal and sounds like a hollow metal box.  Because it is.  However, I'm pretty confident that if I can avoid abusing this little stove, it should work well for a long time.

If you're like me and you need a picture for size reference, I have attached a picture of my stove with a 10" Lodge cast iron skillet and an Alpine Stowaway 1.1L pot on it.  Should be able to use both without issue.  Think two 10" skillets would be snug, but doable.

There are surely newer, fancier, hotter, more rugged stoves out there.  For what this one costs I think it'll be just fine for normal use.  Time will tell.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Classic Propane Stove

DESCRIPTION:
Coleman Classic Propane Stove About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.

REVIEW:
Received one of these as part of a package deal with our little camper.  Honestly, only came to own one due to the package deal.  Had I set out to buy one I probably would have picked one of a more modern design that was more expensive.  I read the reviews here, including the leading review that discusses cleaning the valves.  I did that little repair (sure enough, my valves were caked with dark grease), and fired it up.  I have to say, I'm impressed with the heat this little monster puts out.  I was able to bring a small pot of water to a boil in only a few minutes.  Surprisingly quickly!

Even after cleaning the valves the flame adjustment is pretty touchy.  Only a small amount of movement at the knobs makes large changes to the flame.  Regardless, you can turn the flame down quite a bit.  The heat range seems to be from "hot" to "surface of the sun".  No using this as a food warmer, for sure.

Regardless.  Yes, is inexpensive.  Yes, there are some parts on it that are not rugged.  You'll have to be careful with the little wind screens on the sides as the little tabs that hold them on would bend easily.  The whole thing is made of fairly thin stamped metal and sounds like a hollow metal box.  Because it is.  However, I'm pretty confident that if I can avoid abusing this little stove, it should work well for a long time.

If you're like me and you need a picture for size reference, I have attached a picture of my stove with a 10" Lodge cast iron skillet and an Alpine Stowaway 1.1L pot on it.  Should be able to use both without issue.  Think two 10" skillets would be snug, but doable.

There are surely newer, fancier, hotter, more rugged stoves out there.  For what this one costs I think it'll be just fine for normal use.  Time will tell.

data.title: Coleman Soft Plush Top Inflated Quickbed
data.description: Take the comfort of an indoor bed with you when you sleep outside on the Coleman Queen Airbed Folding Cot with Side Tables and 4D Battery Pump. Connect the included 4D pump to the Double Lock valve for easy, leak-free inflation. Once you find the perfect firmness, the AirTight system will help keep it that way through the night, since it's factory-tested to be leak-free. The ComfortStrong coil system provides all-night support. It is more than a place to sleep with 2 side tables with cup holders to easily place your drink, a snack, a magazine, or personal items on both sides of the bed. A sewn-in cover keeps the airbed in place on the strong steel frame. When youre ready to pack up, the Double Lock valve lets you deflate the airbed just as easily, and it folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
data.reviewText: A decent bed for the money.  i now have 2 of them and have had no problems.

data.title: Coleman Beach Shade
data.description: " Take your own shady, private space to the beach or park with the Coleman Beach Shade. This pop-up beach tent is made with UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays and durable Polyguard material that is designed to last season after season. The extended floor can be zipped shut for privacy while changing clothes, and the dry line offers a convenient place to hang wet bathing suits and towels. Integrated storage pockets give you a secure place to put your items while you swim or sunbathe. And when it gets hot, the back window can be unzipped to let the breeze in, while the sand bag compartments can be filled as needed to anchor the beach canopy on windy days. The Coleman Beach Shade comes with ground stakes and pre-attached guy lines and can be set up in about five minutes.
data.reviewText: Once assembled, the tent is great. But the poles are very cheap feeling, and get caught on the pole sleeves and poked a hole through it. Now it's a pain to put together again. I would have expected something better from Coleman.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Beach Shade

DESCRIPTION:
" Take your own shady, private space to the beach or park with the Coleman Beach Shade. This pop-up beach tent is made with UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays and durable Polyguard material that is designed to last season after season. The extended floor can be zipped shut for privacy while changing clothes, and the dry line offers a convenient place to hang wet bathing suits and towels. Integrated storage pockets give you a secure place to put your items while you swim or sunbathe. And when it gets hot, the back window can be unzipped to let the breeze in, while the sand bag compartments can be filled as needed to anchor the beach canopy on windy days. The Coleman Beach Shade comes with ground stakes and pre-attached guy lines and can be set up in about five minutes.

REVIEW:
Once assembled, the tent is great. But the poles are very cheap feeling, and get caught on the pole sleeves and poked a hole through it. Now it's a pain to put together again. I would have expected something better from Coleman.

data.title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
data.description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText: While this stove creates plenty of heat and will do so under wet, windy conditions. The few times i have used it, when I was finished with the stove, and removed the gas cylinder for transport. The Cylinder SEALS were compromised and leaked until the cylinder was EMPTY. I have several other stoves which use the same cylinders and NONE of them compromise the cylinder seals. Dangerous waste of fuel and a definite weakness in stove function..

data.title: Coleman Portable Camping Quad Chair with 4-Can Cooler
data.description: Have a cold drink at hand at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with a Coleman Cooler Quad Chair. The cooler built into the armrest holds up to 4 cans and provides easy access to a cold drink once the one in your mesh cup holder is empty. While you sip on your favorite beverage, the fully cushioned seat and back only adds to your relaxation. If you want your hands free, the side pockets work nicely to store your book, magazine or some snacks. When the fun is done, the chair easily folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
data.reviewText: Good size, good material, cheap frame.  Like others noted, the arm snapped off the first time I used it.  Unfortunately it has been too long and I can not return.  :-(

data.title: Coleman 70-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler, Tan
data.description: Food, drinksand the funlast longer with the Coleman 70-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler. To be precise, this cooler keeps ice up to 5 days at temps as high as 90F thanks to an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls. Large enough to hold 100 cans, this spacious cooler transports and chills enough food and drinks to keep the entire party fueled and refreshed for the duration of your adventures. The comfort-grip, no-crush handles make it easier to lift your cooler in and out of the car or onto a picnic bench with no pinching. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid ensures you have a place to sit and relax, and the cup holders molded into the lid keep your drinks close and prevent spills. When the fun is done, the EZ-Clean top has a smoother surface that wipes clean easily, and the leak-resistant channel drain lets you empty any excess water without tilting the cooler. You'll be ready for your next fun adventure in no time.
data.reviewText:
This is a very good basic cooler. We're using it on a road trip and it works well. I used a couple frozen gallon jugs of water as ice and prechilled both the cooler itself as well as our drinks. Three days into the trip and everything is still nice and cold with the ice about half melted.

I especially like the size, which is compact for how much it holds. The recessed handles are nice since they don't catch on the other luggage in the back of the van. Just an all around good cooler.

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: Gave to my sister. Got smashed in Shipping. Never used it. Seemed like a nice unit

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: Perfect size for keeping in camping supplies. Will need sharpening, comes in with a very slight edge.

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText: tent leaks.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent

DESCRIPTION:
The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..

REVIEW:
tent leaks.

data.title: Guide Series Dual-Fuel Camping Stove
data.description: With fuel-efficient burners and a wind-blocking design, the Coleman Guide Series Dual-Fuel Stove gives you the ability to make family meals for any outdoor adventure. The two independently adjustable Band-a-Blu burners in this portable stove combine for 14,000 BTU of reliable cooking power. Dual Fuel technology conveniently uses either Coleman Liquid Fuel or unleaded gasoline (both sold separately) to efficiently create steady heat with less fuel. This propane stove has adjustable Wind Block panels that provide wind protection while giving you the stovetop space to use two 10-inch pans at the same time. All Season Strong technology helps this propane gas stove function well in all conditions, from summer tailgates to winter hunting trips. The durable chrome-plated grate on this camping stove is removable for easy cleaning.
data.reviewText: We have one and bought this as a gift for special friends at Christmas.  It was a big hit!  Can't go wrong.

data.title: Coleman Autumn Trails 30 Degree Sleeping Bag
data.description: Warm and durable for the outdoorsman, the Coleman Autumn Trails 30 Degree Big & Tall Sleeping Bag is a great rectangular bag choice for 3-season use in camp.  Stay comfortable when its 30F outside  Big & tall design for people up to 6 ft. 4 in. tall  Roll Control system keeps your bag straight while rolling it up  Wrap-n-Roll system for easy packing, storing and transporting your sleeping bag.  Comfort Cuff design surrounds your face in softness  ZipPlow zipper for snag-free opening and closing  Thermolock draft tube prevents heat loss through the zipper  ColeTherm insulation keeps you warm and comfortable  Exterior tuck pocket to conveniently store small items  Rectangular sleeping bag, 81 x 39 in.
data.reviewText: This is a nice classic style sleeping bag that is an XL size. I bought it for camping with my son and his scout troop.  It says it's rated for 20 deg F. Maybe 50 deg F would be a better rating.  Don't count on this keeping you warm when it gets close to freezing. You'll be freezing too!

data.title: Coleman Compact Propane Lantern
data.description:
Take along personal-sized light that lasts all night when you head outdoors with the Coleman Compact Propane Lantern. The compact design of this lantern provides a perfect amount of light for one person - up to 300 lumens - and saves space for other gear when you pack. A quick match strike is all you need to get your lantern started, then simply dial in the perfect brightness with the adjustable control knob. Once your lantern is lit, the chain handle allows for easy hanging. Meanwhile, the globe guard helps protect the globe from breaking. No matter where you go, the PerfectFlow pressure control technology produces a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather. The Coleman 5132A700 One-mantle Compact Propane Lantern is the lantern that made camping cozy, at its easiest and most portable. Clean-burning propane fuel provides a soft glow for late-night stories or a beacon to guide you back to camp. This Coleman compact lantern lights with matches and is pressure-regulated for consistent light, regardless of weather. The porcelain ventilators will prevent rusting and help this lantern last you a long time. Powered by one 16.4 ounce propane cylinder (not included), this handy little lantern features slip-on mantles and a chain handle that can be used for carrying or hanging. Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText:
While this lantern works, there are a few drawbacks.

1. The light it puts out isn't that bright.
2. It is not very steady sitting on the propane cylinder. It is easy to knock over and break the top.
3. It hisses a lot burning the gas to create light. And if you turn it up to be somewhat bright, it REALLY makes a lot of noise.

There are good battery powered alternatives which have none of these drawbacks and are similarly priced, or less. Because of that, I cannot recommend this product.

data.title: Coleman Triton Series 2-Burner Stove
data.description: Cook breakfast, lunch and dinner on the legendary durability of a Coleman Triton Series 2-Burner Stove. Once this propane camping stove starts to heat up, PerfectHeat Technology gets the temperatures just right, WindBlock Shields keep the wind from blowing the flames around and the PerfectFlow Pressure Control System keeps the heat steady on each of the high-performance 11,000-BTU burners. The spacious cooking surface is large enough to fit a 10-in. (25.4 cm) pan and 12-in. (30.48 cm) pan at the same time, and independently-adjustable controls allow you to set your heat anywhere from simmer to boil. The two burner camp stove is also efficient, with a 1-hr. runtime on high using a single 16.4-oz. propane cylinder. When the fun is done, removable heavy-duty, nickel-chrome grates make cleaning easy, and the built-in handle will have you carrying this lightweight stove effortlessly.
data.reviewText:
I guess it's shame on me for not reading into the info and guessing what was missing.
Great stove for the money-- if you don't mind carrying matches. Wish this was represented before the purchase.

Now I will have to upgrade, only I will look in the stores to make sure of what I am getting
