-The rear side of the tent is all mesh. It's great for ventilation and looking out, but it can't be closed. It should have a zipper window just like the other sides. It got a bit drafty at night.
-The U-shaped door inside the tent could be improved. Only the upper half can be unzipped to make a mesh window. I'd prefer the whole panel have the option. Also, it would be great to have a "double-door" on a tent this size.
-The 2 corners on both sides of the screen area, where they meet the tent, had ~1" holes that were not sewn shut. Manufacturing defect? Either way, this allowed some ants access to the screen area. Rather annoying!!

We didn't have "weather" so I can't attest to how it holds up in wind and rain. If you are looking for a good tent to take a small family camping, I'd recommend this tent.

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText:
It's a good cooler, its a very good cooler for $13 bucks for sure. I gave it three stars because:

It leaks horribly through the zipper. I mean it's like the top doesn't even exist. would work better if you used some of the blue ice packs from Rubbermaid rather than ice.

The shoulder strap is well I don't know. but it made me so mad adjusting it and the fact that it doesn't unclip I just cut it off.

This cooler will definitely not work well in ATV racks (I did not deduct any stars for that that is something more than I would have expected but just noting it on here.

data.title: Coleman CPX 6 Rugged XL LED Lantern, 700 Lumens
data.description: Illuminate any outdoor excursion with the convenient and reliable Coleman CPX 6 Rugged XL LED Lantern. This camping lantern provides 360 degrees of light from LEDs that never need to be replaced, and it runs cool for worry-free use throughout the night. CPX compatibility allows this LED lantern to run on either four D-cell batteries or one CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge (batteries and cartridges sold separately). With two settings to choose from, this Coleman Lantern produces a beam distance of 40 feet on high (700 lumens) and 20 feet on low (140 lumens). Energy-efficient design delivers up to 60 hours of light on the low setting or 8 hours on the high setting from a single charge. The textured tube reduces glare for a streak-free beam pattern. Impact-resistant and IPX4 water-resistant construction help this rugged lantern survive any adventure, and the large bail handle makes it easy to carry along the way.
data.reviewText: DO NOT waste your money on this piece of crap. Mine came with rechargeable battery pack which I didn't want-prefer to use 3 or 4 D batteries. No choice here. Well, plugged it in to charge 14 hours like instructions said and battery pack light was green which means fully charged. Charged several hours anyway. Removed plug, turned on and no light. Would light up when plugged in but backwards-low was high and high was low. Then I find out that battery pack to use 4 D batteries is 20 bucks more. This whole deal is insane. Coleman should be ashamed.

data.title: Coleman Camping First Aid Kit an All Purpose First Aid Kit for Emergencies at Home, Car, Workplace, Hiking, or Survival, 205-Pieces
data.description: The Coleman Expedition First Aid Kit is the ultimate all purpose first aid kit for larger groups or outdoor adventures. This 205 piece kit features components needed in the field, including various standard and specialty bandages, first aid tape, gauze pads, antiseptic towels, antibiotic ointment, sting relief wipes, a cold pack and common first aid tools. All the first aid supplies are organized in a zipped, durable, semi-hard case. The unique EZ Find System keeps your items organized and easy to find during an emergency. There is space in the kit for your specialized medical supplies or medications so you can fully customize this first aid kit to fit your needs. The Coleman Expedition First Aid Kit is the perfect first aid kit for your home, RV, car, bug out bag, or for groups when camping, hiking, at sporting events, workplace, and more.
data.reviewText: Okay kit, but after using it during a moderately serious fall, I removed and replaced some of the contents.  Lousy quality adhesive tape went out and was replaced by more high tech stretchy type, added some polysporin.  Removed and replaced the old fashioned bandaids with good quality name brand ones that don't turn your skin wrinkly white, and some better gauze pads.  I didn't mind the extra purchases, because it is a nice little kit.  Just open it and look over the contents and add and subtract BEFORE YOU NEED IT.

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
data.reviewText: Nice water carrier for camping or traveling. The only thing I don't like is the faucet. It is kind of small and fragile. My first one was broken after 2 years. .

data.title: Coleman Trailhead II Cot
data.reviewText:
Well - I should have believed all the comments about installing the crossbar!  The wife and I spent 30 minutes trying everything to get the 2nd crossbar in place on both cots - no go.  It is simply impossible unless you use a screwdriver or some type of wedge.  I REFUSE to use a screwdriver to put together a darn cot!  Hey, if Amazon wants to cover return postage for everyone with this problem - I guess it is in their cost model.  I for one will never buy another coleman product.  Obviously they know the problem but simply don't care.

UPDATE:  Just sent Coleman a e-mail outlining my problem (and that of others) - will let ya know what they say.
FLASH - REPLY:  HOLD ONTO YOU SEATS MY FRIENDS!  Coleman replied and said they NEVER HEARD of the crossbar problem.  I believe that says it all - unreal.

data.title: Coleman Deluxe PerfectFlow Propane Lantern
data.description:
Illuminate your outdoor adventures with the Coleman Deluxe PerfectFlow Propane Lantern. This powerful camping lantern produces 967 lumens of ultra-bright light. You can light the lantern with a quick match strike and dial in the desired brightness level with the adjustable control knob. The footed base keeps the lantern stable when placed on a tabletop or other surface, and the feet on the base collapse for compact storage. Thanks to PerfectFlow pressure-control technology, this propane lantern delivers a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather. This outdoor lantern uses a 16.4-ounce propane cylinder (sold separately) and lasts 7 hours on the high setting or 13 hours on low. Coleman's Two-Mantle Propane Lantern is as steady as they come. This lantern is pressure-regulated to control fuel flow, so you get consistent light in any temperature. This Coleman propane lantern lights with a match and has a control knob that works like a dimmer switch to let you adjust the brightness and burn time. The porcelain ventilators will prevent rusting and help this lantern last you a long time. This lantern operates on a 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included), and will burn for up to seven hours on high or 14 hours on low. With a unique, stable base and foldable feet it just doesn't get much more convenient than this. Manufacturer's Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: The lantern I received is a bit crooked when screwed on to a gas tank. And the stand cup it sits in isan't wide enough . Not the same stand cup in the picture. I don't even care though because it certainly works good and its bright enough with out the need to say I need more light!

The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture. REVIEW: The lantern I received is a bit crooked when screwed on to a gas tank. And the stand cup it sits in isan't wide enough . Not the same stand cup in the picture. I don't even care though because it certainly works good and its bright enough with out the need to say I need more light!

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: We bought this to replace our tabletop Coleman grill.  I really like the idea of this grill.  The only negative is that it is very heavy and not as easy to maneuver once collapsed as we had hoped for.  Takes up a lot of space in our storage compartment.

data.title: Coleman Camping Coffee Maker
data.description: Easily make drip-brewed coffee while enjoying the outdoors with the Coleman Camping Coffee Maker. Designed with a steel base that fits over most 2- and 3-burner camp stoves, this camping coffee maker features a large reservoir that enables you to brew up to 10 cups of hot coffee at once. Making coffee with this outdoor coffee maker is easy. Simply spoon grounds into the removable swing-out filter basket, fill the reservoir with water, and place the coffee maker over your stove burner. Within minutes, the 10-cup glass carafe will be filled with freshly brewed coffee ready to be enjoyed in the great outdoors. A Pause 'N Serve feature lets you grab a cup before the brew cycle is finished. The Coleman Camping Coffee Maker measures 8.5 x 11 x 14 inches. About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come. END
data.reviewText:
I'm not sure if it was the grind of my coffee or the coffee maker but the water wasn't draining through the coffee and filter very well. It seemed to be backing up in the tray the coffee is held in. Also the door to the coffee tray kept popping open.

Having said that, it's still better than a  percolator so I'll keep using it.

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent with Screen Room | Evanston Camping Tent with Screened-In Porch
data.description: Enjoy the great outdoors without the bugs inside the Coleman Evanston 8-Person Tent with Screen Room. Great for car camping and extended camping trips, this spacious tent offers enough room for 2 queen-size airbeds and is designed to set up in under 15 minutes thanks to snag-free, continuous pole sleeves and Insta-Clip pole attachments. The tents WeatherTec system features patented welded floors and inverted seams to keep the rain off you and your gear; a rainfly is included for extra weather protection. Extended awnings let you keep the windows open for enhanced air circulation without letting in the rain. The tent also comes with a separate screened-in room for extra ventilation, covered storage for your gear, or an extra sleeping area on warmer, dry nights. Remove the included rainfly to let in more sunlight through the mesh roof on dry days or to gaze up at the stars on dry nights. "
data.reviewText:
FIRST VACATION IN 10 years!!!!  This tent is IT and then some! I always read reviews but only write ones that I believe may be helpful! I have Severe RA and Lupus and now that the kids are grown and gone and thanks to new meds I am anxious to see if camping can be possible when you have "issues" or challenges. The sun sensitivity and extra years and weight held me back from enjoying the outdoors again. The right tent is the most important. I melt in the heat, cannot handle UV sun sensitivity from the meds, and the kids are grown and gone. So everything has to be light and easy. After getting it wrong twice (thank you Amazon CSR secret agents!) THIS IS THE TENT (No reduced priced no free merchandise) Just amazed at this. It has all this going for it

1. Light
2. Ventalation Ventalation Ventalation
3. Easy Peasy set up
4. Well made
5. Haven't been rained on but reviews are assuring. I will update if needed

So I would recommend and buy again!!!

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText:
***Update***
DO NOT BUY THIS OR ANY COLEMAN TENT
We have only used this tent four times, it has been stored properly and never put up wet. The hooks that attach the tent to the poles on one half of the tent have all started ripping off. The other half is completely fine. I contacted Coleman about a possible quality control issue, and they were not only NO help, but also pretty rude about it. I expected to get more than four uses out of a product, and I certainly expected them to at least seem concerned about a possible quality control issue for their products. Shame on them!! I will be researching other brands for my family's camping needs.

We took this tent camping for the first time last Spring, and it worked very well.  It was much larger than what my husband was used to using, but I really liked it.  My son slept in one of the side compartments and we kept our gear in the other one.  In the middle, we had a queen sized air mattress.  We still had plenty of room to move around.  My husband also liked that he could stand up in the middle section of the tent.
It was very simple to set up, two of us set it up in about 20 minutes.  I think one person could set it up on their own, it would just take longer.  We had to break it down in the pouring rain, and even that was pretty easy to do.  Once we got it home and dried it out, everything easily went back into the bag it came in!
I really loved all of the little storage compartments in it.  We took our three year old son, and he loved being able to put his toys in them.
The only issue we had was with a small leak.  The last night we were camping, a really big storm blew through and it was absolutely pouring down rain.  One of the places where the pole attaches to the top of the tent started to drip into the tent.  In a lighter rain, we might not have even noticed it.  And my husband thinks he can weatherproof that spot to prevent us from having the problem in the future. But I should also mention that the bottom of the tent was literally floating on the rainwater in some places, and it did not leak one tiny bit!
All in all, we were very pleased with the tent!  You might just want to waterproof the spots where the tent poles attach to the tent before you take it out--just to be on the safe side.  :0)

data.title: Coleman Pack-Away Camping Cot
data.description: Bring along a perfect relaxation spot on your next camping trip with the Coleman Pack-Away Camping Cot. Ideal for sitting, reclining, and sleeping, this versatile camping cot features an extra-wide design that provides ample resting room. It also comes equipped with a convenient removable side table with a cup holder, where you can easily keep drinks, magazines, and other personal items. The steel frame can support up to 300 pounds and is built to withstand season after season of rugged use. Thanks to its folding, portable design and included carry bag, this camping cot fits inside most car trunks and is easy to transport. The Coleman Pack-Away Camping Cot accommodates most people up to 6 feet 6 inches tall.
data.reviewText:
Would have given 5 stars because it is very comfortable and large. Also very easy to set up and put away. However, on about its 10th or so use i rolled over to 1 side of the cot and the material ripped right off the side. I weigh 160 pounds...

Overall I loved it except for its early demise.

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText:
So far, my husband and I, have already managed to go through two of these tents. Though the tent is nice, spacious, and easy to set up it is anything but durable. We have no children and are both into our adult years so the tent sees little, light use and should be expected to hold up more than halfway through a weekend camping.

However, these tents keep breaking in strange ways and strange places despite our gentle treatment. We aren't tough with them by any means, but they just don't hold up. The first one we had the zipper break within the first few hours of set up. Graciously, Amazon sent us a return. Now, a few months after we are out of our return or refund period, we are yet again camping and it broke. This time where the base of the tent connects to the stakes that secure it down.

Unfortunately, I won't be purchasing this tent again, though we did enjoy it while it held together. It's lack of durability even under very gentle, general use is frustrating for the price and make it an unreliable product. I will be trying another brand at this point and hopefully it will hold up better than this particular product did.

data.title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium
data.description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium. Designed to fit Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 21.5 x 14 x 4.8 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
data.reviewText: I saw these last night at a camping goods store and am ordering this one online now.  I wanted to put in a note to tell anyone that is unsure about the sizing that the stove we have recommends this model number for the carry bag (recommended on the box under the flap on one of the sides), but ours is not the grill type one as shown above.  I can see why people are confused.  I suggest you look at the box of the grill/stove you buy and see which bag they recommend you buy to get the right size.  Our stove is #2000007502 Perfectflow Stove.  These look to be very sturdy and will be helpful to transport the stove.  I will update if the one recommended doesn't fit or is not the same as we examined last night.  Update:  It fit perfectly with room to spare and is very well made.  My only problem is that I accidentally bought two of them and only need one.  Guess I will have to send one back.

data.description: Have a cold drink at hand at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with a Coleman Cooler Quad Chair. The cooler built into the armrest holds up to 4 cans and provides easy access to a cold drink once the one in your mesh cup holder is empty. While you sip on your favorite beverage, the fully cushioned seat and back only adds to your relaxation. If you want your hands free, the side pockets work nicely to store your book, magazine or some snacks. When the fun is done, the chair easily folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
data.reviewText:
We needed extra chairs for our RV. These were well-rated and inexpensive.
Pros: They come in a carrying case and take up little storage space when folded.  They are more comfortable to sit in than our low-slung beach chairs that these replaced. Morning dew quickly dries off in the sun.
Cons: The drink pocket could be a little deeper and wider to handle a wine glass. The top of the back of the chair offers no support for the neck.
Bottom line: At this price, they're a deal!

data.title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small
data.description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small. Designed to fit small Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 20.4 x 12 x 4.5 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
data.reviewText: I bought it because I was tired of having bottles rolling around in my jeep and also concerned about beating up the stove.  It does that.  I am unsure of the material it is made of though.  No rips yet.  There is also a little extra room in the bag to place a couple thin cutting boards and some cooking utensils.

data.title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove
data.description: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory.
data.reviewText:
I bought mine on a whim when I found them for 1/2 price on the Coleman website.

Pros:
Size is great for those who don't want to lug around a briefcase sized double burner style stove.
Unit comes with two generators so you can use camp fuel or kerosene.
Coleman fuel is easy to find.
BTU output is great.

Cons:
Size is not the best for backpacking.
Burn cup and pot stand fins are more flimsy than they look, they would bend up inside a backpack.
The tank body gets hot after a short while, and the flame control knob melts a bit because it is so close to the burn cup.
Pressure pump knob assembly is unique to the stove, and not as easy to come by as the standard lantern replacement part.
Kerosene not always easy to find.

Mine arrived broken right out of the box. When ignited, the flame was on full all the time, no matter where I set the valve. I called Coleman and immediately got a real person (pretty rare these days). They were stumped, but sent me a new generator free of charged. I installed the generator but got the same result. I called back again and they sent me a new needle valve (again free of charge). The valve did the trick, but took time waiting for the parts to arrive.  Also, I tried swapping the generators from Coleman fuel to Kerosene, and the exercise was a real disaster. The generator was leaking from the tank base, the fuel smelled terrible and I was unable to get the stove to light.

This stove would be best used in a canoe or kayak type of situation, where  you have more cargo storage than just your backpack.  Compared to the Coleman 533, this stove is smaller and has better flame / heat control.

In conclusion, we're all nostalgic for Coleman stoves, but I think in this instance, the micro butane variety of stoves is a better, more practical solution.

Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Burn cup and pot stand fins are more flimsy than they look, they would bend up inside a backpack. The tank body gets hot after a short while, and the flame control knob melts a bit because it is so close to the burn cup. Pressure pump knob assembly is unique to the stove, and not as easy to come by as the standard lantern replacement part. Kerosene not always easy to find. Mine arrived broken right out of the box. When ignited, the flame was on full all the time, no matter where I set the valve. I called Coleman and immediately got a real person (pretty rare these days). They were stumped, but sent me a new generator free of charged. I installed the generator but got the same result. I called back again and they sent me a new needle valve (again free of charge). The generator was leaking from the tank base, the fuel smelled terrible and I was unable to get the stove to light. This stove would be best used in a canoe or kayak type of situation, where you have more cargo storage than just your backpack. Compared to the Coleman 533, this stove is smaller and has better flame / heat control. data.description: The Coleman Expedition First Aid Kit is the ultimate all purpose first aid kit for larger groups or outdoor adventures. This 205 piece kit features components needed in the field, including various standard and specialty bandages, first aid tape, gauze pads, antiseptic towels, antibiotic ointment, sting relief wipes, a cold pack and common first aid tools. All the first aid supplies are organized in a zipped, durable, semi-hard case. The unique EZ Find System keeps your items organized and easy to find during an emergency. There is space in the kit for your specialized medical supplies or medications so you can fully customize this first aid kit to fit your needs. The Coleman Expedition First Aid Kit is the perfect first aid kit for your home, RV, car, bug out bag, or for groups when camping, hiking, at sporting events, workplace, and more.

data.title: Coleman Quad LED Lantern Special Edition Ultra Bright 190 Lumens, Red
data.description:
Take the convenience of four lights in one with you with the Coleman Quad LED Lantern. Together the lantern shines 190 ultra-bright lumens for up to 75 hours. During an emergency, pop one of the four lights off the base for a personal flashlight. They recharge once theyre back on the base for hands-free light. The 24 LEDs last a lifetime, so they never need replacing and the lantern runs cool so its always safe to touch, even when its been on for hours. Combining an area light with four portable lanterns in a single clever unit, the Coleman LED Quad lantern is a versatile choice for indoor or outdoor use. The Quad is equipped with four removable LED panels that hold 24 LED lights total. Users can remove any of the four panels and use them independently, such as when needing a portable light for the bathroom or tent, or they can keep all four attached to the lantern and produce 190 total lumens of light. As a result, the Quad is the perfect light for group camping trips and any other occasion where folks might splinter off into smaller groups during the night. Each of the panels is a fully functioning light, with six 5mm white LEDs, a rechargeable NiMH battery, an independent on/off switch, and a top handle for carrying or hanging. The NiMH battery--which recharges when stored in the lantern base--provides up to 1.5 hours of light on its own. The lantern base, meanwhile, requires eight D-cell batteries (not included), which provide an approximate run time of 75 hours for the entire lantern. Other features include a main on/off switch located on top of the lantern, a light range of 26 feet, and a limited five-year warranty. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: Best purchase I have made for camping. This lantern is awesome. Each panel will last for about 2 hours of constant use. They are bright enough that I take two panels off to use for light around the campsite, once they start to get dim I pop the other two off that are fully charged and pop the two that are dim on to charge. That give me a cycle of constant light. Great for night time hiking as well. My boys are 6 and 10 and they can get the panels off and on easily. I am thinking of getting a second one just to have another, I don't even need it, just want another one. There is a model that has two larger more powerful panels rather than the 4 smaller ones, I look to purchase that as well at some point.

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: This thing is rad. Haven't received that many compliments in a long time. Everyone on our camping trip drooled over this lol.

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
data.reviewText: We LOVED this water carrier! We bought 3 for a camping weekend recently, and couldn't be more pleased. The pressure release valve was awesome, and the spout was great as well. I highly recommend these for emergency water storage or camping.

data.title: Coleman All Day Dome Shelter 1539;x1539; Canopy

data.title: Coleman 2-Person Pop-Up Tent
data.description: Great for festivals and car camping, the Coleman 2-Person Pop-Up Tent sets up in as little as 10 seconds, letting you get back to the fun. This camping tent is uniquely designed with preassembled poles, allowing the tent to pop into place in a snap. The taped floor seams keep you dry even when the ground is soaking wet, while the multi-position rainfly allows you to find the perfect balance of protection and ventilation. This instant tent offers room for two camp pads and comes with two integrated storage pockets to help you keep small items organized. This 2-person tent folds flat for simple storage and is lightweight for easy portability.
data.reviewText: Bought this for my daughter. It was an excellent choice. Taking it down took a few tries but she has now mastered it.

data.title: Coleman Large Dual Action Hand Pump
data.description: Pump, Large Dual Action Hand
data.reviewText: I was looking for something to fill my swiss balls which came with the little hand pumps.  I first tried an adapter for my little compressor (the type plugged into a car's outlet).  That didn't work.  The Coleman dual action pump work spendlidy.  It pumps air on both the upward and downward strokes of the handle filling the ball quite rapidly.  I couldn't be more pleased with this thing.

data.title: Coleman Soft Plush Top Inflated Quickbed
data.description: Take the comfort of an indoor bed with you when you sleep outside on the Coleman Queen Airbed Folding Cot with Side Tables and 4D Battery Pump. Connect the included 4D pump to the Double Lock valve for easy, leak-free inflation. Once you find the perfect firmness, the AirTight system will help keep it that way through the night, since it's factory-tested to be leak-free. The ComfortStrong coil system provides all-night support. It is more than a place to sleep with 2 side tables with cup holders to easily place your drink, a snack, a magazine, or personal items on both sides of the bed. A sewn-in cover keeps the airbed in place on the strong steel frame. When youre ready to pack up, the Double Lock valve lets you deflate the airbed just as easily, and it folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.

data.title: Coleman Self-Inflating Camping Pad with Pillow
data.description: Enjoy additional support and cushioning with the Coleman Self-Inflating Camping Pad with Pillow. This camping sleeping pad features a comfy tufted design that adds an extra layer of padding between your sleeping bag and the ground. Equipped with a free-flow valve, the sleeping pad self-inflates with no air pump needed. Simply roll it out and open the valve, and the pad will begin inflating. When it's time to pack up, reopen the valve and push the air out using the compression straps. Thanks to its durable, weather-resistant polyester shell, this camping sleeping pad can endure night after night of use.

data.title: Coleman Mens Apparel 10mm Pvc Rain Suit Advantage Max-4
data.description: Light Weight east to carry and put on when the weather changes quickly. Plenty of ventalation and adjustability.
data.reviewText: Glad you have it in your pack if you get caught in an unexpected rain or snow..Packs down and takes up very little room..It holds up well in most situations, but if you have to go through any briers, they shred like paper! Very noisy if your trying to hunt...But all that said, if you want something cheap to keep in your pack for unexpected weather and don't care to throw away after a couple uses, these fit the bill. The suit does a good job keeping you dry if you are sitting on stand or walking where there is nothing for it to snag on. For the price, and small size of the package, I keep one in my pack...May not last but better than getting soaking wet!

data.title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
data.reviewText: For the money, this thing is quality!!  I put a cooler shock dry shell pack with a towel (because it got too cold!) and my granddaughter's Calciworms in it to keep them at 50-60 degrees.  (they're for her bearded dragon)  It's a neat, inexpensive way to keep them for a month or more and saves me money. I'm really pleased...The worm company recommened a wine cooler!!! I think those are way more expensive!!!

data.title: Coleman Coleman 62 qt. Xtreme Cooler
data.description: The Coleman 62 qt. Xtreme Cooler features Xtreme 5 technology and the capacity to hold up to 85 cans. It features two-way handles for easy lifting and carrying, and the rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain allows for no-tilt draining. Dimensions: 26L x 14.75W x 14H inches. About Coleman Company Inc. Fueled by poor eyesight and blind ambition, W.C. Coleman began his company with the invention of his gas lamps in 1905. Twenty years later, the company saw a country at war and an Army without adequate stoves. The Coleman pocket stove is today considered one of the two most important innovations in non-combat equipment in the war effort. It is this commitment to quality and innovation that continues to push the Coleman brand to create essential and reliable products that are convenient and easy to use. High-quality plastic construction with hinged lid. Features Xtreme 5 technology and Thermozone insulation. Designed to hold up to 85 cans. Comes with tow handle, 2-way handles, and sturdy wheels.
data.reviewText: Very Spacey. Good for trip camping.

data.title: Coleman Exponent Outfitter Camp Kitchen
data.description: The Coleman Exponent Outfitter Camp Kitchen is a complete, simple-to-set-up outdoor food preparation center. A spacious countertop, sink, pantry, and cooking stand are combined in this efficient unit to make preparing meals in the outdoors easy and enjoyable. No assembly is required -- the Outfitter Camp Kitchen simply unfolds and is ready to go to work. Its ideal for camping, backyard barbecues, picnics, beach parties, and tailgating. Measuring over six feet long (72.5") and over four feet high (50") when fully opened, the Camp Kitchen offers a spacious meal-prep area. It features a spacious polished-aluminum work surface for preparing foods, a side extender shelf for holding a camp stove, and a removable ABS plastic sink with drain plug and hose for doing dishes, rinsing vegetables or cleaning the days catch. There are built-in sections for just about everything else, including a paper-towel holder, chrome-plated wire hooks for cups and utensils, and shelving for spices or food storage. Weighing only 35 pounds, the Camp Kitchen folds down to a compact 42" x 21.75" x 7.5" and fits easily in most car trunks. It carries like a suitcase in its own rugged zippered nylon storage case, so its ready to go on a moments notice. The Outfitter Camp Kitchen can be used with legs extended to stand on its own, or with the legs folded underneath and placed on a tabletop or tailgate. Its durable enough to withstand heavy use, with strong, double-reinforced corners, positive stainless steel latches, a heavy-duty aluminum frame with stable leg braces for added support, and zinc-plated storage racks, dipped in PVC for extra rust-resistance. What's more, with the lid closed over the work surface between meals, the Camp Kitchen becomes a handy game table: enjoy playing backgammon, chess or checkers on the molded-in game boards; checker and backgammon pieces are even included! Simplify cooking for large groups on car and RV camping expeditions or tailgating parties with the Coleman Exponent 2142A700 Outfitter camp kitchen--a complete outdoor food preparation center. This two-tiered table system features a main preparation and storage area and a side extender shelf that easily holds most Coleman stoves. The storage/prep area includes a removable sink with drain plug and hose, paper towel holder, and chrome plated wire hooks and shelving for additional storage. It has a closeable lid with painted game boards (checkers/backgammon pieces included). It's made from heavy-duty channel extruded aluminum for added strength and features stable leg braces for added support, a convenient carry handle and rugged storage bag.
data.reviewText:
If you're thinking of buying this, DON'T. YOU DON'T NEED IT. My friend has one, and it's the most useless thing we've ever taken camping, and WAY more trouble than it's worth. If you have enough camping cups and dishes to fill the hooks and shelves, you have too many. Who wants to spend time washing all that stuff (including the unit itself) with freezing cold water in the woods?? Plus it's wobbly on uneven ground (ALL CAMP SITES) and there's really no reason to have a sink if you don't also have running water.

Anyway, isn't part of the fun of camping learning to simplify and improvise, instead of bringing your house and all the complicated maintenance that goes with it into the wilderness with you?
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Exponent Outfitter Camp Kitchen

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman Exponent Outfitter Camp Kitchen is a complete, simple-to-set-up outdoor food preparation center. A spacious countertop, sink, pantry, and cooking stand are combined in this efficient unit to make preparing meals in the outdoors easy and enjoyable. No assembly is required -- the Outfitter Camp Kitchen simply unfolds and is ready to go to work. Its ideal for camping, backyard barbecues, picnics, beach parties, and tailgating. Measuring over six feet long (72.5") and over four feet high (50") when fully opened, the Camp Kitchen offers a spacious meal-prep area. It features a spacious polished-aluminum work surface for preparing foods, a side extender shelf for holding a camp stove, and a removable ABS plastic sink with drain plug and hose for doing dishes, rinsing vegetables or cleaning the days catch. There are built-in sections for just about everything else, including a paper-towel holder, chrome-plated wire hooks for cups and utensils, and shelving for spices or food storage. Weighing only 35 pounds, the Camp Kitchen folds down to a compact 42" x 21.75" x 7.5" and fits easily in most car trunks. It carries like a suitcase in its own rugged zippered nylon storage case, so its ready to go on a moments notice. The Outfitter Camp Kitchen can be used with legs extended to stand on its own, or with the legs folded underneath and placed on a tabletop or tailgate. Its durable enough to withstand heavy use, with strong, double-reinforced corners, positive stainless steel latches, a heavy-duty aluminum frame with stable leg braces for added support, and zinc-plated storage racks, dipped in PVC for extra rust-resistance. What's more, with the lid closed over the work surface between meals, the Camp Kitchen becomes a handy game table: enjoy playing backgammon, chess or checkers on the molded-in game boards; checker and backgammon pieces are even included! Simplify cooking for large groups on car and RV camping expeditions or tailgating parties with the Coleman Exponent 2142A700 Outfitter camp kitchen--a complete outdoor food preparation center. This two-tiered table system features a main preparation and storage area and a side extender shelf that easily holds most Coleman stoves. The storage/prep area includes a removable sink with drain plug and hose, paper towel holder, and chrome plated wire hooks and shelving for additional storage. It has a closeable lid with painted game boards (checkers/backgammon pieces included). It's made from heavy-duty channel extruded aluminum for added strength and features stable leg braces for added support, a convenient carry handle and rugged storage bag.

REVIEW:
If you're thinking of buying this, DON'T. YOU DON'T NEED IT. My friend has one, and it's the most useless thing we've ever taken camping, and WAY more trouble than it's worth. If you have enough camping cups and dishes to fill the hooks and shelves, you have too many. Who wants to spend time washing all that stuff (including the unit itself) with freezing cold water in the woods?? Plus it's wobbly on uneven ground (ALL CAMP SITES) and there's really no reason to have a sink if you don't also have running water.

Anyway, isn't part of the fun of camping learning to simplify and improvise, instead of bringing your house and all the complicated maintenance that goes with it into the wilderness with you?

data.title: Coleman SUV/Packable Twin QuickBed
data.description: Drive your "tent" up to the campsite when the Coleman SUV/Packable Twin QuickBed makes the back of your vehicle a cozy place to sleep. The airbed is the perfect size when you lay the seats down and spread out. An expansion pillow even allows you to customize your bed's size for your SUV. Once you find your perfect firmness, the comfort lasts all night long with the Airtight System that's guaranteed not to leak and the Double Lock Valve that allows the bed to deflate and inflate quickly. When you're ready to pack up, the built-in patented Wrap 'N' Roll Storage System makes putting the bed away a matter of folding it, rolling it and storing it. The bed is also great for vehicle emergency kits.
data.reviewText: Fits perfectly in a Honda Element. Easy to inflate and holds it's air well.

data.title: Coleman Lantern Carry Case
data.description: Protect your lantern with the Coleman Lantern Carry Case so it'll be ready to go the next time you head outdoors. This tough case helps protect against dents, scratches and broken globes. The base has room for extra mantles and miscellaneous other items. The case latch locks securely and is positioned at the top for easy loading and unloading. Simply let your lantern cool, place it on the base and place the lid on top and lock it, and you're ready to head on to your next adventure.
data.reviewText: Exactly as described. Fit my 40 year old lantern like a glove. Can finally get rid of the original cardboard box.

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: Amazing, love the portability!!! sugguestion is to season your grills prior to cooking on them...grease and bake for a bit....will help with nonstick.

data.title: Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad
data.description: Get a good night's rest with the Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad. The closed cell foam protects you from uncomfortable surfaces and helps insulate you from the cold ground.
data.reviewText: This camp pad is basically packing material. It is made of a very thin, cheap bubbly foam. It did keep me warm while sleeping on the snow, but is not durable and will likely be unusable after another trip or so. I would advise you to spend a little more on a more durable piece of foam.

REVIEW:
This camp pad is basically packing material. It is made of a very thin, cheap bubbly foam. It did keep me warm while sleeping on the snow, but is not durable and will likely be unusable after another trip or so. I would advise you to spend a little more on a more durable piece of foam.

data.reviewText: My husband and I had high hopes with this sleeping bag but soon realized that it was not going to work. First of all it was too short for my husband who is 6 feet tall. data.title: Coleman WeatherMaster 6-Person Tent with Screen Room
data.description:
Watch nature unfold before your eyes in Coleman WeatherMaster 6-Person Tent with Screen Room. During the day, the screen room lets you enjoy the great outdoors away from bugs and can be used on warmer nights for extra sleeping space. If it rains, this tent keeps you dry thanks to WeatherTec technology, which uses a tub-like floor with patented corner welds, protected seams, and a covered zipper to help keep water out. Meanwhile, the strong frame is tested to withstand 35+ mph winds. Additionally, an angled window keeps rain out while allowing for extra air circulation. A hinged door makes for easy coming and going. Setup is simple with snag-free, continuous pole sleeves and a patented pin-and-ring system. Inside, there's enough room to stretch out on 2 queen-size airbeds, and storage pockets keep small items organized and off the floor. When it's time to head home, the tent easily packs up into an expandable carry bag with a rip strip for extra storage until you're off on your next adventure.


A patent-pending hinged door provides easy entry and exit, and a touch of at-home convenience.
The Coleman WeatherMaster 6 Tent has two rooms and plenty of space for the entire family. Up to six campers can sleep easily inside thanks to the 17-by-nine (W x D) footprint and 6 ft. 10 in. center height. Two doors�including a patent-pending hinged front door�offer easy in-and-out. An additional floor-less screened room provides extra ventilation on warm days, a great place for meals and gathering, rain or shine, and protected storage space. Coleman's Comfort System allows excellent ventilation control via Variflow adjustable airflow, a vented Cool-Air privacy Port, privacy vent window, and all-mesh ceiling. A convenient electrical access port lets you run a cord from your device in the tent to an outside power source, while an interior gear pocket helps you keep things organized. Setup is quick and simple thanks two color-coded poles and continuous pole sleeves, shock-corded fiberglass poles, an exclusive pin-and-ring design, and InstaClip attachments. Straightforward instructions are sewn into the convenient carry bag, and separate stake and pole bags are also included. Coleman's WeatherTec System
This proprietary series of interworking features keeps the weather where it belongs�outside the tent�at all times.
  • Leak-free Seams: In addition to fully-taped rainfly seams, the rainfly covers doors and windows, and incorporates easy-to-use Velcro frame attachments
  • Weather-resistant Fabric: Coated polyester fabric combined with anti-wicking thread, webbing, and zippers are designed to keep you dry
  • Protected Seams: Inverted floor seams dramatically increase weather resistance by hiding needle holes inside the tent, away from the elements
  • Waterproof Floors: Welding technology strengthens the tent floor and eliminates needle holes
  • Zipper Protection: Zipper cuff adds protection from the elements to the door
  • Wind Strong Frame: Engineered to be a stronger, more wind-responsive frame by using redesigned poles and guy-out triangles to anchor tents and increase performance
Specifications
  • 75-denier polyester taffeta fly
  • 68-denier polyester mesh inner tent
  • 1,000-denier polyethylene floor
  • 19-millimeter steel poles
The Coleman Limited Warranty
Coleman has been in business for more than 100 years, and continues to produce high-quality, affordable tents, lanterns, table-top stoves, and more. All Coleman products have a warranty against defects in material and workmanship, and the WeatherMaster 6 Tent includes a limited one-year manufacturer's warranty. About Coleman Outdoor Products
More than 100 years ago, an entrepreneurial young man named W.C. Coleman had an idea for manufacturing better lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. A century later, Coleman's current catalog features a wide-ranging array of products that make spending time outside a pleasure. The company crafts coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days, airbeds that are comfortable and won't deflate during the night, a complete line of bright and long-lasting LED lights, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and more.
data.reviewText: This is not an easy tent to put up. It took me about an hour the first time without doing all of the extra lines and I am an experienced camper.  I tore one of the sleeves a little with the joint in the metal pole.  My scouts tore a tent seam when they tried to put it up. But it seems like it will be a sturdy tent with the metal poles.

data.title: Coleman 40-Can Collapsible Soft Cooler
data.description: Have the convenience of a large cooler when you need one and extra storage room in your home when you don't. The Coleman 40 Can Collapsible Cooler is big enough to hold almost two cases of soda and then collapse down to the size of a phone book when you're ready to put it away. A bungee cord on the back, front pocket and external lid storage make keeping snacks and utensils convenient. An adjustable shoulder strap makes it easy to carry. Antimicrobial, product-protecting properties are built into the flexible lining to resist odor, mold and mildew, making it easy to clean between uses. Heat-welded seams help prevent soft liner leaks.
data.reviewText: PURCHASED BECAUSE IT WAS COLLAPSIBLE AND COULD USE ON MY MOTORCYCLE, BUT THE BOTTOM HARDER SECTION GOT A SMALL HOLE IN IT AFTER USING 2 TIMES ,AND NOW WHEN THE ICE MELTS IS FILLS UP TO BOTTOM SECTION. OTHER THAN THAT IT WORKS WELL .

data.title: Coleman Converta Cot
data.description: The Coleman Converta Cot provides comfortable seating at the campsite or in the backyard. Four back position and two foot positions for use as a lounge chair or cot.Unfolded Dimensions: 76 in. x 26 in. x 15.5 in..Folded Dimensions: 27.8 in. x 25 in. x 4.9 in..
data.reviewText: Not at all comfortable to sit in.  Since the middle part is level with the ground, your body just wants to slide down.  As a cot, it was alright.  Kind of low to the ground for my taste, and you can't really get it perfectly flat.  Worst of all though was the locking mechanism for the head rest.  It's hard to lock it in and unlock it.  Half the time the retaining bar just winds up popping off and you have to bend it back in place.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Converta Cot

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman Converta Cot provides comfortable seating at the campsite or in the backyard. Four back position and two foot positions for use as a lounge chair or cot.Unfolded Dimensions: 76 in. x 26 in. x 15.5 in..Folded Dimensions: 27.8 in. x 25 in. x 4.9 in..

REVIEW:
Not at all comfortable to sit in.  Since the middle part is level with the ground, your body just wants to slide down.  As a cot, it was alright.  Kind of low to the ground for my taste, and you can't really get it perfectly flat.  Worst of all though was the locking mechanism for the head rest.  It's hard to lock it in and unlock it.  Half the time the retaining bar just winds up popping off and you have to bend it back in place.

data.title: Coleman Cooler Stainless Steel Hinges and Screws
data.description: For replacement on most Coleman Coolers with plastic hinges as well as Coleman steel Coolers made after 2000.
data.reviewText: The hinges are an excellent fit, and seem to be reasonably good quality.  The factory plastic hinges that Coleman uses are absolutely worthless, and I've had them snap apart on multiple coolers for multiple reasons.  These seem like they will last a while - will be especially interested in how they hold up in a salt air environment.  My only complaint is the price.  I got my cooler on sale (from Amazon); these hinges (from a third-party Amazon affiliate, I think) cost just under half the price of the cooler itself.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Cooler Stainless Steel Hinges and Screws

DESCRIPTION:
For replacement on most Coleman Coolers with plastic hinges as well as Coleman steel Coolers made after 2000.

REVIEW:
The hinges are an excellent fit, and seem to be reasonably good quality.  The factory plastic hinges that Coleman uses are absolutely worthless, and I've had them snap apart on multiple coolers for multiple reasons.  These seem like they will last a while - will be especially interested in how they hold up in a salt air environment.  My only complaint is the price.  I got my cooler on sale (from Amazon); these hinges (from a third-party Amazon affiliate, I think) cost just under half the price of the cooler itself.

data.title: Coleman Coleman 62 qt. Xtreme Cooler
data.description: The Coleman 62 qt. Xtreme Cooler features Xtreme 5 technology and the capacity to hold up to 85 cans. It features two-way handles for easy lifting and carrying, and the rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain allows for no-tilt draining. Dimensions: 26L x 14.75W x 14H inches. About Coleman Company Inc. Fueled by poor eyesight and blind ambition, W.C. Coleman began his company with the invention of his gas lamps in 1905. Twenty years later, the company saw a country at war and an Army without adequate stoves. The Coleman pocket stove is today considered one of the two most important innovations in non-combat equipment in the war effort. It is this commitment to quality and innovation that continues to push the Coleman brand to create essential and reliable products that are convenient and easy to use. High-quality plastic construction with hinged lid. Features Xtreme 5 technology and Thermozone insulation. Designed to hold up to 85 cans. Comes with tow handle, 2-way handles, and sturdy wheels.
data.reviewText: We have much higher expectations for this cooler in regards to holding ice for a longer number of days.  It is not coming through.

data.title: Coleman Divide Plus Spin 525L LED Lantern
data.description: The Coleman Divide Plus Spin 525L LED Lantern features a unique twisting design that lets you rotate the entire lantern to easily change modes.Impact-resistant up to 1 m.
data.reviewText:
The Divide + Spin is a solid-enough lantern once you get it set up. However, inserting the batteries and closing the cover can be challenging. Maybe you will luck into closing it correctly on the first try. Or maybe you will struggle with it for 15 minutes like I did. I tried to follow the instructions but failed at "aligning the post and side tabs."  Align tabs with what? Are the release tabs the same as side tabs? It would help if Coleman would mark these with yellow. Also, I had to use a little force to close this, which I was hesitant to do.

Don't wait until you are sitting by a campfire to put this together! You need light to find the tabs, plus you need to see how to insert the 4 D batteries.

The lantern is held in the package with a very strong cord so I recommend scissors. Once this is set up, it seems like a good lantern. Not terribly bright but it will light your picnic table, and it is adjustable. To save batteries, turn the bottom so the red areas are visible. Turn it back when you want to use it.

data.title: Coleman Triton+ Propane Camping Stove
data.description: With powerful burners and a wind-blocking design, the Coleman Triton+ Propane Stove gives you the ability to make family meals in windy conditions. The two independently adjustable burners combine for 22,000 BTUs of cooking power, and PerfectHeat technology ensures the burners deliver steady and even heat with efficient fuel use. This propane stove has adjustable Wind Block pan supports that provide wind protection while giving you the stovetop space to use a 12-inch and a 10-inch pan at the same time. PerfectFlow pressure control keeps the fuel stream steady for consistent burner performance, and starting up this propane gas stove is a snap with Instastart push-button ignition. Built for toughness and reliability, this portable camping stove features a heavy-duty lid latch for security in transport. The durable chrome-plated grate is removable for easy cleaning, and the aluminized steel cooktop resists rust for years of reliable use.
data.reviewText: This thing gets as hot as my grill at home. Large surface and the wind shield is nice. I do wish the grates were a little closer together but not a big deal.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: pleased with this hatchet

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
data.reviewText: be sure to wash this before you use it!!!! apparently i did not do it well enough because when i pulled it out for water at a soccer game all the girls had flakes of blue plastic in their cups. when i got home and washed it again a large piece if thin blue plastic "shaving" i would call it came out with more small flakes. it works, it's a good shape for storage and the spout works well. but i wonder what other stuff might be seeping out of the plastic now. i'm not going to freak out about it, but it was very off putting to start out :(

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText: Good at first, but too heavy and poles bend easily under heavy winds. Also, the fabric ripped very easily after 20 days of heavy winds. You get what you pay for

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: The left burner does not ignite via the igniter. I'm having to use a manual lighter for that. I can't find anything in the manual as to how to fix this issue. Also, very difficult to clean after cooking food that has grease, such as hamburgers. The grease gets all over in the bottom and there's no way to take out the bottom tray and clean it.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill

DESCRIPTION:
Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.

REVIEW:
The left burner does not ignite via the igniter. I'm having to use a manual lighter for that. I can't find anything in the manual as to how to fix this issue. Also, very difficult to clean after cooking food that has grease, such as hamburgers. The grease gets all over in the bottom and there's no way to take out the bottom tray and clean it.

data.title: Coleman Quickbed with Pump Combo
data.description: The Coleman Quickbed and Pump Combo combines comfort, reliability and versatility in one great airbed. This twin-sized airbed measures 74" x 39" x 5" high when inflated, and fits standard-sized sheets. The Quickbed is constructed of high-grade, puncture-resistant PVC, with a Comfort Coil system inside for great support, and a soft plush top for luxurious comfort. The conveniently included pump operates on 4D batteries (sold separately); it inflates and deflates the bed quickly, thanks to Coleman's Double-Lock valve. Like all Coleman inflatable products, this Quickbed features Coleman's Airtight System, so your bed stays inflated while you sleep. The attached Coleman Wrap 'N' Roll System makes packing, storing and transporting a breeze.

The conveniently included pump operates on 4D batteries (sold separately); it inflates and deflates the bed quickly, thanks to Coleman's Double-Lock valve. Like all Coleman inflatable products, this Quickbed features Coleman's Airtight System, so your bed stays inflated while you sleep. The attached Coleman Wrap 'N' Roll System makes packing, storing and transporting a breeze.

REVIEW:
Good

data.title: Coleman 2000016402 Mess Kit, Aluminum, 5-Pc.
data.description: 5 Piece, Aluminum Mess Kit, Includes 8 OZ Cup, 7" Fry Pan, 16 OZ Pot With Lid & 6.5" Dish Pan, Pieces Nest Inside Each Other & Is Covered With A Lid For Easy Storage & Transport.
data.reviewText: I just received this yesterday and I was happy at first. I opened the box, pulled everything out, which was lighter and thinner than what I remembered it used to be. I saw at the bottom that it had a sticker, "Made in China". Now, I don't mind things being made from there. That wasn't the issue, the issue started when I looked at the box again, I just happened to look at the bottom of the box when I saw a warning notice! It said, that under California law, Chemicals used in this product can cause cancer and other reproduction issues. I'll edit this when I get back and even post a picture of this, but I feel that putting a warning label, at the bottom of the box is WRONG, I also think its WRONG for Amazon to not post warning information about items on the site. Had I had known the warning label, I wouldn't have brought this! Coleman isn't the company it used to be..it seems it  buys cheap, hazardous products from other countries and sell them elsewhere for a high profit at the risk of our family lives. As a scout, and a gulfwar vet, I'd recommend going elsewhere!

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText: For about $100 dollars great value. We are a family of four with two small children. We bought this tent to have enough space in the event of a rain day. I was disappointed after the first rain, of pooling along the inside edges of the tent. I was disappointed that directions need to be better. The tent does indeed pack back with all accessories into the carry bag included. I do not camp often, so I wanted a tent that I did not have to waterproof. Because of the claim by Coleman with their Weathertec water proofing, I was more confident in my purchase. I did see the rain bead and run off the tent fabric. But after awhile, the fabric below the rain fly and above the floor tub became saturated and wet to the touch inside. Also, on the very cool nights, this same section became wet to the touch inside the tent. The fabric under the rain fly never was wet and never had condensation in the morning. The ventilation is okay if there is a good breeze, otherwise the sun really heats up the  tent. Since the rain fly is velcroed to tents poles, guywired to ground stakes and attached to tent pole rings with bungee cord, removing the rain fly was not practical. It is really better to be outside the tent to remain cool than to rely on the mesh to keep the tent cool. This was our experience. We had battery operated fans which helped. I returned the tent after one week of use and two rainy nights primarily due to the water issues.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent

DESCRIPTION:
Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.

REVIEW:
For about $100 dollars great value. We are a family of four with two small children. We bought this tent to have enough space in the event of a rain day. I was disappointed after the first rain, of pooling along the inside edges of the tent. I was disappointed that directions need to be better. The tent does indeed pack back with all accessories into the carry bag included. I do not camp often, so I wanted a tent that I did not have to waterproof. Because of the claim by Coleman with their Weathertec water proofing, I was more confident in my purchase. I did see the rain bead and run off the tent fabric. But after awhile, the fabric below the rain fly and above the floor tub became saturated and wet to the touch inside. Also, on the very cool nights, this same section became wet to the touch inside the tent. The fabric under the rain fly never was wet and never had condensation in the morning. The ventilation is okay if there is a good breeze, otherwise the sun really heats up the  tent. Since the rain fly is velcroed to tents poles, guywired to ground stakes and attached to tent pole rings with bungee cord, removing the rain fly was not practical. It is really better to be outside the tent to remain cool than to rely on the mesh to keep the tent cool. This was our experience. We had battery operated fans which helped. I returned the tent after one week of use and two rainy nights primarily due to the water issues.

data.title: Coleman #21 Instaclip Mantles: 2-Pack, Model 21B132C
data.description:
It only takes a few moments to get clear white light with Coleman Insta-Clip 2 #21 Mantles. All you have to do is remove the old mantles, slip the new mantle(s) onto the burner tube(s) and then pull the tab.

Coleman #21 Instaclip Mantles

Keep your lantern shining with Coleman's Instaclip Mantles. Thanks to Coleman's patented Instaclip tab mantle installation is simple and easy--with no more strings to tie or wires to twist. Simply slip the mantle over the burn tube and pull the tab away. The mantle instantly secures itself and your lantern shines on. Fits Coleman lantern models: 200, 201, 214, 220, 275, 282, 286, 290, 295, 5107, 5114, 5150 5152, 5153, 5154, 5155, 5156, 5157, 5159, 9950 and 9960
data.reviewText:
In my experience, these are harder to deal with than the tie ones. By the time you mess with the wire, shaping it to fit, having the clip fall off, and stabbing your fingers on the wire, it's a pain.

Just buy the tie ones. Takes literally half the time to tie them on than it does to mess with this on my Dual fuel lanterns.

data.title: Coleman ComfortSmart Cot
data.reviewText: I ordered two of these so my wife and I can sleep on the porch now that it is cooler. They seem to be very well made and are comfortable. Time will tell. The only thing about them is that they are not really long beds. I am 6'2" and I cannot stretch out. I think the tallest person that can lay on the coat straight would be 5'6". Not a real problem. I will bend my legs when I sleep on the cot. After all, a 6'3" cot would be rather large to carry around even when folded. Nice product.
