data.title: Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description:
Sleep comfortably--even when the temperature dips below freezing--with the Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag. Long enough for people up to 6 feet 5 inches in height, this adult sleeping bag is designed to keep you warm in temperatures as low as 0 degrees Fahrenheit. The cotton canvas cover and cozy cotton flannel liner retain heat, so you feel warm from head to toe. For additional comfort, a Thermolock draft tube helps prevent heat from escaping through the zipper, and a ComfortCuff surrounds your face in soft, warm fabric. Fiberlock construction keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A two-way patented ZipPlow zipper system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. And the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N Roll integrated packing system make this backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. The Big Game Sleeping Bag is machine washable and comes with a matching flannel pillow. Stay snugly warm as you slumber on camping and hunting excursions with the Coleman Big Game sleeping bag, which is rated to temperatures as low as -5 degrees Fahrenheit. It's filled with five pounds of Holofill 808 insulation, which offers high-loft, hollow-core fibers that prevent body heat from escaping, thus holding more air inside the bag for good warmth. This super king-sized, rectangular sleeping bag measures 40 by 85 inches, and will fit most sleepers up to 6 foot, 5 inches. Other features include a heavy duty cotton cover material, cotton flannel liner, an included pillow, and Coleman's exclusive set of ComfortSmart sleeping bag technologies, including:
  • ZipPlow: Prevents zipper snags by plowing fabric away from zipper
  • FiberLock: Prevents insulation from shifting, increasing durability
  • ThermoLock: Reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer
  • Zipper GlideTailoring: Allows smooth zipper operation around the corner
  • Comfort Cuff: Surrounds your face with softness
  • Roll Control: Locks bag in place for easier rolling
About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: Not as described: even on the box it says "big and tall, fits up to 6' 7"  " and it claims the size is 40" x 84", it is not even close. I am 6'4" (76") and with my feet flat and jammed against the bottom zipper it doesn't even cover my head all the way, so I would say it's 74" long, not 84". If someone was 6'7" half their head would be sticking out the top. Also it claims to be good to -5 degrees, that is an outright lie as well. The insulation is not "puffy", only maybe 1/2" thick in places, and sleeping INDOORS it is barely warm enough. I'm amazed that all of the people involved in its manufacture could miss the fact that it's 10" shorter than it says on the box! I doubt I'll risk buying another Coleman product, this isn't just a little bit off but is totally falsely advertised.

data.title: Coleman EasyStay Single High Airbed
data.description: Pack lightweight support with a Coleman Quickbed Lite Airbed. With its internal coils, you ll rest on superior comfort and support, while a soft top offers relaxation for sleepers who are on the go. Once you determine your perfect firmness, it will be yours all night long with the airtight system that's guaranteed not to leak and the easy to use double lock valve that also helps inflate and deflate the bed quickly. When you're ready to pack up, the built in storage system makes putting the bed away a matter of folding it, rolling it and storing it.
data.reviewText: Sleep comfortably for 5 nights during our family camping but need to inflate after couple of nights to adjust the firmness of the airbed. Not bad after all, exactly what the product info stated.

data.title: Coleman Rugged A/T Wheeled Cooler, 55 Quart
data.description: Easily transport cold food and beverages over rugged terrain with the Coleman 50-Quart Xtreme Series Wheeled Cooler. Ideal for camping, hikes, and other outdoor activities that take you off the beaten path, this wheeled cooler box comes equipped with two oversized, 8-inch wheels that provide smooth transport over nearly any terrain. Simply extend and lock the telescoping handle, close the hinged lid, and wheel the cooler wherever you need to go. For the rare occasions when the wheels can't handle the terrain, the EZ-Lift swing handles allow you to easily tout around the cooler by hand. Featuring Keeps the Ice! insulation, this cooler offers exceptional ice retention and keeps your items cold for up to five days in temperatures as high as 90 degrees Fahrenheit. The heavy-duty lid latch helps secure the cooler's contents, while an integrated bungee tie-down system allows you to tether extra gear to the lid. When it is time to go, the leak-resistant drain allows you to easily empty excess water without tilting the cooler. And thanks to its integrated cup holders, this hard cooler helps you keep a beverage always close at hand.
data.reviewText: Way more capacity than I thought or needed.  Donated it to a local little league.  They were thrilled.

data.title: Coleman Triton+ Propane Camping Stove
data.description: With powerful burners and a wind-blocking design, the Coleman Triton+ Propane Stove gives you the ability to make family meals in windy conditions. The two independently adjustable burners combine for 22,000 BTUs of cooking power, and PerfectHeat technology ensures the burners deliver steady and even heat with efficient fuel use. This propane stove has adjustable Wind Block pan supports that provide wind protection while giving you the stovetop space to use a 12-inch and a 10-inch pan at the same time. PerfectFlow pressure control keeps the fuel stream steady for consistent burner performance, and starting up this propane gas stove is a snap with Instastart push-button ignition. Built for toughness and reliability, this portable camping stove features a heavy-duty lid latch for security in transport. The durable chrome-plated grate is removable for easy cleaning, and the aluminized steel cooktop resists rust for years of reliable use.
data.reviewText: Its light and its what I was looking for.  The only reason I gave it a 3 star is because the self ignition button does not work. I think it worked once and then it didn't work again.  I kept it because I love the stove itself and just use a lighter or a match.

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
data.reviewText: Purchased about 9 months ago and has been out on 3 camping Cub Scout camping events. So far it is holding up great. Wish there was a clear version. I have a 40 yr old clear collapsible version and it will heat up nicely in the sun. The blue doesn't trap heat the same way. For the price, this has been great! The valve is still working fine, just make sure it is screwed on properly. Will probably be buying a second one before our next camp out. Very helpful in controlling the boys wasting water by playing with the water hydrants. Makes a great hand washing station. Throw a bar of soap in a nylon stocking and tie round the handle.

Purchased about 9 months ago and has been out on 3 camping Cub Scout camping events. So far it is holding up great. Wish there was a clear version. I have a 40 yr old clear collapsible version and it will heat up nicely in the sun. The blue doesn't trap heat the same way. For the price, this has been great! The valve is still working fine, just make sure it is screwed on properly. Will probably be buying a second one before our next camp out. Very helpful in controlling the boys wasting water by playing with the water hydrants. Makes a great hand washing station. Throw a bar of soap in a nylon stocking and tie round the handle.

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: Great concept, nice looking and once you learn how to use it, cooks ok.  Problem lies in the fact that it is a real pain to clean and after about 10 uses, the chrome pan is rusting.  Oh well, probably get 3 or 4 years out of it.

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: Sundome 2P Dome TentFeatures:Up to 33% more water resistant verses a comparable standard Coleman tent Weathertec System patented welded floors and inverted seams help keep water outSnag-Free continuous pole sleeves for easy setup Ground Vent for increased ventilationTwo windows for great ventilation and viewsDoor awning helps keep the rain out E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside your tent Storage pocket to keep your gear organized Expandable carry bag lets you tear away the rip strip for easy packingSpecifications:Doors:1 D-DoorRainfly:PolyguardTent:Polyguard,Polyester Mesh 68DPoles:FiberglassWeight: 7.2 (3.3 KG)<br/
data.reviewText: This tent was used for a weekend away at a motorcycle rally that my husband went to.  Of course hotel rooms were booked as he decided to go last minute so he got a tent site at a nearby campground.  The tent is compact enough in it's case/carry bag to be able to fit on top of the side bag of his Harley.  It was easily removed from it's carry bag, easy to put up and definitely roomy enough for two.  The bathtub like bottom helped to keep the moisture away from his sleeping bag and other personal items.  With the vents on the tent it was a little chilly (it actually went down to 45 degrees at night in Texas) so this needs to be kept in mind when deciding what you are using it for and where.  It was easy to take down and put it back into it's carry case.  Overall a great tent for the price and good quality!

data.title: Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower
data.description: Don't miss a shower  even when you're camping  with the Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower. The reservoir holds up to 5 gallons of waterenough for multiple showers. It's constructed of heat-locking material that warms the water when it's left in the sun, even on cool days. The shower features a strong handle for reliable hanging and carrying as well as a handy on/off shower head valve to conserve water.
data.reviewText:
I brought this to MOAB where the dirt blows into every pore of you body. A bath at the end of the day is the perfect thing to crawl into your sleeping bag clean. I found out fine dust can stop the spout from operating as well as it pulled off and detached from the hose. The other thing that seems troubling is to clean it properly and put it away dry inside without mold growing. Coleman should take a lesson from Camelback in having a wide mouth not only to fill it but to clean and dry it properly!
Very disappointed!
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower

DESCRIPTION:
Don't miss a shower  even when you're camping  with the Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower. The reservoir holds up to 5 gallons of waterenough for multiple showers. It's constructed of heat-locking material that warms the water when it's left in the sun, even on cool days. The shower features a strong handle for reliable hanging and carrying as well as a handy on/off shower head valve to conserve water.

REVIEW:
I brought this to MOAB where the dirt blows into every pore of you body. A bath at the end of the day is the perfect thing to crawl into your sleeping bag clean. I found out fine dust can stop the spout from operating as well as it pulled off and detached from the hose. The other thing that seems troubling is to clean it properly and put it away dry inside without mold growing. Coleman should take a lesson from Camelback in having a wide mouth not only to fill it but to clean and dry it properly!
Very disappointed!

data.title: Coleman Motorcycle Helmet Bag
data.description: This gorgeous MadDog Gear motorcycle helmet bag has the finest details and highest quality you will find anywhere! MadDog Gear motorcycle helmet bag is truly remarkable. Product details:  condition: brand new  item SKU: ss-sms-4003578  information: your helmet keeps you safe on your bike; now it's your turn to keep it safe from bumps and scratches with a motorcycle helmet bag designed by Paul Jr. Himself. The heavy-duty, water-resistant polyester bag fits most street bike helmets, and the reinforced leather-like bottom adds extra protection and stops the case from sliding. Inside, the fleece lining keeps your helmet warm and adds extra protection from scratches and other damage.
data.reviewText:
My BRP moduar 3 helmet didn't fit in this bag so i use it for an open face helmet with a sheild.
Really is a nice bag for the price.

data.title: Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description:
Sleep comfortably--even when the temperature dips below freezing--with the Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag. Long enough for people up to 6 feet 5 inches in height, this adult sleeping bag is designed to keep you warm in temperatures as low as 0 degrees Fahrenheit. The cotton canvas cover and cozy cotton flannel liner retain heat, so you feel warm from head to toe. For additional comfort, a Thermolock draft tube helps prevent heat from escaping through the zipper, and a ComfortCuff surrounds your face in soft, warm fabric. Fiberlock construction keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A two-way patented ZipPlow zipper system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. And the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N Roll integrated packing system make this backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. The Big Game Sleeping Bag is machine washable and comes with a matching flannel pillow. Stay snugly warm as you slumber on camping and hunting excursions with the Coleman Big Game sleeping bag, which is rated to temperatures as low as -5 degrees Fahrenheit. It's filled with five pounds of Holofill 808 insulation, which offers high-loft, hollow-core fibers that prevent body heat from escaping, thus holding more air inside the bag for good warmth. This super king-sized, rectangular sleeping bag measures 40 by 85 inches, and will fit most sleepers up to 6 foot, 5 inches. Other features include a heavy duty cotton cover material, cotton flannel liner, an included pillow, and Coleman's exclusive set of ComfortSmart sleeping bag technologies, including:
  • ZipPlow: Prevents zipper snags by plowing fabric away from zipper
  • FiberLock: Prevents insulation from shifting, increasing durability
  • ThermoLock: Reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer
  • Zipper GlideTailoring: Allows smooth zipper operation around the corner
  • Comfort Cuff: Surrounds your face with softness
  • Roll Control: Locks bag in place for easier rolling
About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: Perfect fit for a 6'2" 300lb. Large frame  person. Very warm & roomy. Good zipper.

data.reviewText: We loved this tent for its size, ease of setting up, and overall quality - until...the second time we went to use it a poll snapped rendering the whole tent garbage. The polls are a lightweight plastic. I wish I could recommend this tent, because we otherwise liked it - but Coleman shouldn't have skimped on quality here.

data.title: Coleman Carlsbad Tent with Screen Room
data.description: Sleep in after the sun rises or put the kids to bed early while the sun is still up with a Coleman Carlsbad Fast Pitch Dome Tent with Screen Room. The Dark Room technology on this tent blocks 90% of sunlight to keep the tent darker if you want to sleep while the sun is up, and it also reduces how much the tent heats up during the day by up to 11.4%.
data.reviewText:
this is a good tent for people who want to bring some of the conveniences of home to a campsite. The tent itself is relatively easy to pitch. It is descried as a four person tent, which may be a tight fit for four average or larger adults. Two to three adults could fit comfortably or may  an adult and a couple of little kids. It has some nice features including an eport which allows access to power. The major selling point is the dark room feature. And it is dark. As a sper light sleeper I wish I had that feature in my bedroom but in all honesty one of the charms of camping is waking to the light of the early morning sun,. Still it probably is effective at helping kids sleep and certainly will block any light. The tent also has a screened in porch. It is not waterproof or weather resistant but is an enjoyable feature.

This is a good tent for people who enjoy camping but not necessarily roughing it. It is too heavy for backpacking but great for campsite use. It is roomy and  designed for comfort. It is well made and should provide years of enjoyment. .

data.title: Coleman 16-Quart Personal Wheeled Cooler, Blue
data.description:
Keep your food and drinks cold, and keep the fun going, for up to 2 days when you roll in with a Coleman 16 Quart Performance Wheeled Cooler. Large enough to hold 22 cans, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for several people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or across the ball field, the recessed wheels and long, telescoping handle let you roll up with hardly any effort. The additional molded handles make it easy to lift your cooler in and out of the car or onto a picnic bench. Coleman provides a convenient way to keep things cold, even when you're on-the-go, with the 16-Quart Personal Wheeled Cooler. This convenient cooler can hold up to 22 12-ounce cans or two 2-liter bottles upright and features traditional Coleman construction for rugged durability. Other features include an extendable handle that retracts for convenient storage and recessed wheels for easy transport to and from your favorite sporting events and outdoor activities. This durable Coleman cooler comes with a limited manufacturer's warranty. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.

data.title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
data.reviewText:
Not so sure about this one. It was relatively easy to set up, thanks to the built in poles. My middle schooler did not have enough strength to clip in the clips though, despite being plenty strong. I like the way the instructions are sewn into the carry bag and printed on a waterproof material. The instructions were a bit off though, they told me to connect poles for the "rain tarp" that were already connected at the factory.

Speaking of which, the "rain tarp" is just a triangle that covers over the single door. If it was raining you would need to close all the other windows or you would get drenched. There is a little vent that is below the back window that has a rain cover (if you stake it out) but still  I can't imagine this being comfortable in a humid rainy situation.

I like the center ceiling mounted o-ring that will allow for a lantern to be hung. It has 2 sewn in patches for your wallet and phone and such which is nice.

Of course you know that take the # of people a tent says it can fit and divide by half to see what it will actually comfortably accommodate.

It has a "power port" a little zipper in the corner that opens and allows a cord to come in. Not sure I'd want A/C power coming into my tent in the rain - but this may also be useful to pass small items back and forth from the outside without having to open the whole door.

Folding up is kind of another issue. The instructions just say "reverse the setup procedure" but after all of the origami like movements you have to do, it's hard to remember what it looked like at the start and to get it back to a place where it fits in the bag. I'd easily say that the time I saved not having to take out the poles and fold them was eaten up by trying to figure out how to fold the thing and stuff it back in the carry bag. I suspect this would improve over time and with experience.

Also, the "1 minute" to set up is a bit disingenuous. Maybe if you really have your act together, but no way does this time include setting up all the necessary stakes for the tent corners and guy wires.

Overall, a great experience setting up, a little less so breaking down. Could prevent many family fights at the campsite ;-)

Also, very light and pretty compact, Overall great kit, only reason I gave it 4 instead of a 5 is that the handle is very easy to bend by your own strength. Should hold up to a decent amount of weight in the pan though....

data.title: Coleman RoadTrip LXX Portable Propane Grill, Black
data.description: Show off your grilling skills on camping trips or at tailgate parties with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Portable Propane Grill. This outdoor grill combines a collapsible, portable design with convenient features that facilitate grilling in tough conditions. The grill's individually adjustable dual burners each blast out 10,000 BTUs of heat, giving you enough cooking power to prepare a wide range of foods, from grilled veggies to thick slabs of steak. The Swaptop interchangeable design allows you to switch out the grill grates for a griddle top or stove grate (both sold separately), while the Instastart ignition lets you light the burners by simply pushing a button. Ideal for summer cookouts, this portable gas grill also excels in chilly conditions thanks to PerfectFlow technology, which helps ensure steady heat output. Sliding tables on both sides of the grill provide easy access to ingredients and utensils and can be tucked away for easy transport and storage. With its 285-square-inch cooktop, this grill offers a large cooking surface area without sacrificing portability. Its collapsible stand sets up in seconds and folds up for compact storage, while its large handle and wheels make the grill easy to transport. And thanks to its porcelain-coated cast iron grates and removable grease tray, this camp grill is easy to clean.
data.reviewText: I like the grill for portability and features, but it has some major flaws. For one, if you are thinking about hooking this to a camper quick connect supply or a hose adapter on a 20# propane tank forget about it. It will work, just know you will need about $50 worth of adapters and may need to cut and splice propane hoses which most folks have no business doing for safety reasons. Secondly, just know this grill has a very low profile lid which makes it hard to grill in foil pans and impossible to warm up anything in a saucepan without having the lid wide open.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman RoadTrip LXX Portable Propane Grill, Black

DESCRIPTION:
Show off your grilling skills on camping trips or at tailgate parties with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Portable Propane Grill. This outdoor grill combines a collapsible, portable design with convenient features that facilitate grilling in tough conditions. The grill's individually adjustable dual burners each blast out 10,000 BTUs of heat, giving you enough cooking power to prepare a wide range of foods, from grilled veggies to thick slabs of steak. The Swaptop interchangeable design allows you to switch out the grill grates for a griddle top or stove grate (both sold separately), while the Instastart ignition lets you light the burners by simply pushing a button. Ideal for summer cookouts, this portable gas grill also excels in chilly conditions thanks to PerfectFlow technology, which helps ensure steady heat output. Sliding tables on both sides of the grill provide easy access to ingredients and utensils and can be tucked away for easy transport and storage. With its 285-square-inch cooktop, this grill offers a large cooking surface area without sacrificing portability. Its collapsible stand sets up in seconds and folds up for compact storage, while its large handle and wheels make the grill easy to transport. And thanks to its porcelain-coated cast iron grates and removable grease tray, this camp grill is easy to clean.

REVIEW:
I like the grill for portability and features, but it has some major flaws. For one, if you are thinking about hooking this to a camper quick connect supply or a hose adapter on a 20# propane tank forget about it. It will work, just know you will need about $50 worth of adapters and may need to cut and splice propane hoses which most folks have no business doing for safety reasons. Secondly, just know this grill has a very low profile lid which makes it hard to grill in foil pans and impossible to warm up anything in a saucepan without having the lid wide open.

data.title: Coleman Elite WeatherMaster 6 Screened Tent, Multi Colored, 6L x 9W ft. (Screened Area)
data.reviewText:
Pros:
- Really big room, feels cozy
- Rollable windows are fantastic
- The screen porch is just adorable
- Perfect for star-gazing without the rain fly

Cons:
- Bad design on the light switch (explained below)
- Takes 2 people 20 mins to set up, and even more time to pack...
- If packed wet, the dark colors will stain the white areas
- The light is powered by heavy giant D cells, and you need 4 of them

The light switch issue is what really made me disappointed. The LED light was the highlight of this tent and also the reason I chose it. However it stopped working after my first use. At first I thought it was the light itself, because the switch was so simple that you didn't expect it to fail. After sending a hundred photos to Coleman customer support to prove the light stopped working and I'm not reselling it, they sent me a new unit, which still didn't work. So I decided to open the switch and take a look before bugging them again, and it turned out to be totally rusted inside!! The indicator lights are still working but the push buttons are dead.

Yes it rained when I used it, so I had to pack it wet. Though I unpacked it and let it dry later, I guess there was still some moisture left which killed the switch.

It's an outdoor product, but I don't see any effort on the switch to prevent it from getting wet. They should just seal it with rubber and make it detachable like the light and the power unit.

data.title: Coleman 2-Pole Sun Shade
data.description: A great choice for outdoor parties, sporting events, or a day at the lake, Coleman Sun Shades feature UVGuard fabric to provide excellent protection from the sun's ultraviolet rays. The 2-Pole Sun Shade measures 10'x10', with a center height of 7'6". The Shade sets up easily on strong 22mm steel poles. UVGuard 185T 75D polyester taffeta fabric.
data.reviewText: I found this Sun Shade more trouble than it's literally worth ($$$) to set up; after the fact, I found other choices available that would provide the same amount of sun shade with less set up hassles. At least the shade portion was able to be repurposed as a "tarp" to anchor and sit on outdoors. Would not purchase again or recommend this item.

TITLE:
Coleman 2-Pole Sun Shade

DESCRIPTION:
A great choice for outdoor parties, sporting events, or a day at the lake, Coleman Sun Shades feature UVGuard fabric to provide excellent protection from the sun's ultraviolet rays. The 2-Pole Sun Shade measures 10'x10', with a center height of 7'6". The Shade sets up easily on strong 22mm steel poles. UVGuard 185T 75D polyester taffeta fabric.

REVIEW:
I found this Sun Shade more trouble than it's literally worth ($$$) to set up; after the fact, I found other choices available that would provide the same amount of sun shade with less set up hassles. data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: Would be a decent grill but my right burner does not work correctly (yellowish flame). It seems like the regulator is defective on mine since I don't see anyone else reporting this issue. I got it for Christmas one year and didn't use or notice it wasn't working until the warranty was expired.  Need better quality control.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill

DESCRIPTION:
Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.

REVIEW:
Would be a decent grill but my right burner does not work correctly (yellowish flame). It seems like the regulator is defective on mine since I don't see anyone else reporting this issue. I got it for Christmas one year and didn't use or notice it wasn't working until the warranty was expired.  Need better quality control.

data.title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small
data.description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small. Designed to fit small Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 20.4 x 12 x 4.5 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
data.reviewText: This is ok.  My husband got it as a gift and was disappointed it wasn't leather. It fits the Coleman Triton Series InstaStart 2-burner stove just fine and has great pockets for the propane.  It does the job protecting the stove and providing easy carry with everything you need in the bag.  It's ok for the money and does the job.

data.title: Coleman Stratus Adult Fleece Sleeping Bag Liner
data.description:
The Coleman Stratus Adult Fleece Sleeping Bag Liner doubles as both a warm, cozy liner and a low-profile sleeping bag. On its own, the liner can keep you warm in temperatures as low as 50 degrees Fahrenheit. When placed inside another sleeping bag, it can provide 12 degrees Fahrenheit of extra thermal protection. This low-profile sleeping bag liner features a two-way patented ZipPlow zipper system that plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. It also features an elastic storage strap and stuff sack for easy packing and transport. The Stratus Fleece Sleeping Bag Liner can accommodate most people up to 5 feet 11 inches in height. It is machine washable for easy cleaning. The Coleman 8197C410 Stratus Fleece Sleeping Bag Liner is the perfect accompaniment to any sleeping bag, especially when you are camping where you are not sure how cold it is going to be. This sleeping-bag liner is full-size, measuring 33-inches by 75-inches, and fits most full-size sleeping bags up to 5-feet 11-inches long. When used with a sleeping bag, this liner adds 12 degrees of thermal protection, making a 35-degree sleeping bag usable at temperatures as low as 23 degrees! The Coleman fleece sleeping-bag liner is commercial-machine-washable only and comes with its own stuff sack. Manufacturer Warranty
Five-year warranty
data.reviewText: The zipper is a problem. The dual zipper system is apparently not intuitive. Twice I have had people using the bag end up with the zipper jammed and broken.  I like the bag, it is perfect for warm climate camping here in Florida, but I need somethning that does not require instructions to use.

data.reviewText:
It's a decent enough grill.  Really lacks any control over flame.  High might as well be "Volcano" and low might as well be "Afterburner"

That's fine.. my first cook on it went ok, once I got a feel for it, the second was better.

What kills me is the migraine inducing rattle when the thing is in a car being moved.  The grill plates rattle, the wheels rattle, the lid rattles..Fine..  I remove the plates, bungee the lid down, and tape the wheels in place.

Then the hinge hardware rattles too.  And it's all tightened down.  Sigh.  Call Coleman, it's normal for it to do that.

So, it's in the box.. Waiting for UPS to pick it up.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText:
It's a ten-dollar hatchet sold at department stores. This same hatchet is also marketed by Coghlan's, Ozark Trail, Land's End, and many others. They're all the same China-made hatchet, except the branding.

The hatchet is 13" long, and except for the rubber grip cover, is all steel. The handle is solid tubular steel, and it goes all the way through the rubber handle cover. The rubber cover is black and slightly oval-shaped, a welcome addition to the circular handle. The handle cover is 6-1/2" long and has a slight axe handle shape, as opposed to a straight channel. A hard plastic disc is molded on to the handle to serve as (I think) overstrike protection. It's not attached to anything, it's just formed around the handle, and will slide around after the first few strikes. If you want to add a lanyard hole, you can drill one into the rubber handle cover. Go through the rear part and you'll avoid drilling into the steel handle. If you leave this hatchet in the rain, or even outside overnight, it will rust.

The head is attached to the handle with a metal fusion. It's drop-forged 1045 carbon steel and weighs 1 pound, 4 ounces. I tested mine at 47 on the Rockwell scale. The edge is 3" top-to-bottom, and has a flattened butt for driving stakes or hammering. The entire head, except for the edge, is painted black. The edge is absolutely horrible; it's too high. I had to buy a 40-grit belt for my 1" X 30" sander to get the edge angle right, as all the other belts I had were too fine. With the edge angle established, final sharpening was easy. The hatchet will take a razor-sharp edge, but the steel is too soft to hold it for long. For splitting wood, it's fine. The head hasn't come loose at all. There's also a small nail removal notch, but it's pretty much worthless. To use it, the nail has to sticking out by at least 1/4", and the opening slot is only 1/8" at its widest.

This is typical of a department store hatchet for ten dollars. It's serviceable once you grind the edge right and do some basic rust prevention. If you want a better hatchet for $10, buy a used one at a flea market or yard sale and bring it back to life yourself. This Coleman hatchet is okay for the average homeowner, but is hardly worth mention as a serious tool.

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. TITLE:
Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent

DESCRIPTION:
The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. data.title: Coleman 2000014864 Mosquito Head Net

data.title: Coleman Rechargeable QuickPump Unit
data.description:
Bring your Coleman Rechargeable QuickPump Unit on your trip and inflate your air mattress at lightning speedwell, almost. More like two minutes. This handy pump inflates products that feature Coleman's Double Lock Valves, Boston valves, and, with adapters (included), pinch valves. Use the re-charger to re-juice the QuickPump whenever you're near an outlet.

Product Features
  • Material:
  • Weight:
  • Recommended Use: Car camping, travel, inflating air mattresses
  • Manufacturer Warranty:
  • Country of Origin: China
data.reviewText: Terrible pump. I fully charged it and once we pulled it out to use for our air mattress, the air came out ridiculously slow and barely did anything to inflate the mattress. Tried to recharge it again but it won't even charge. Don't waste your money. We bought a similar coleman pump that uses batteries and it works 100x better.

data.title: Coleman Pocket Compass
data.description: Quicker accuracy with liquid-filled design Easy-turn needle sits on jeweled bearing Made of tough, impact-resistant materials Eight directional reference points: N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW Glow-in-the-dark pointer is easy to see in the dark Snap lid protects the face in transport Hang tab to keep it close on a belt loop, backpack or keychain.
data.reviewText: I have limited experience with a compass but can operate one regardless. This particular compass is very confusing as the needle will tend to change often and can bend down to the point where it hits the bottom panel. This can happen when held level or on a flat surface. I am happy with the size and weight of it but doubt I will put it to much use over another compass, shop around for a more well made one.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Pocket Compass

DESCRIPTION:
Quicker accuracy with liquid-filled design Easy-turn needle sits on jeweled bearing Made of tough, impact-resistant materials Eight directional reference points: N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW Glow-in-the-dark pointer is easy to see in the dark Snap lid protects the face in transport Hang tab to keep it close on a belt loop, backpack or keychain.

REVIEW:
I have limited experience with a compass but can operate one regardless. This particular compass is very confusing as the needle will tend to change often and can bend down to the point where it hits the bottom panel. This can happen when held level or on a flat surface. I am happy with the size and weight of it but doubt I will put it to much use over another compass, shop around for a more well made one.

data.title: Coleman 6-Day 50 Qt. Ultimate Xtreme Wheeled Cooler
data.description:
With extra insulation and rugged design, the Coleman Ultimate Xtreme Cooler is in it for the long haul. Two inches of insulation in the lid and case provide superior cold retention: it will keep food, beverages and ice for up to 6 days at outside temperatures up to 90 F. This 50-quart cooler can hold up to 72 cans with ice. The cooler's large, sturdy wheels are designed for pulling the cooler through rough terrain. A comfortable tow handle provides one-handed portability, while two-way carry handles make it easy to lift or move. The convenient hinged lid sports four built-in drink holders, each a reliable 2" deep. The lid is designed to accommodate 24" elastic cords (sold separately) for additional storage. A rustproof, leak-resistant Channel Drain provides easy, no-tilt draining of melted ice or other liquids. Color: ice green. Take your Coleman 50-Quart Wheeled Ultimate Cooler on the road, to the job site, on vacation, or to game day. With plenty of food and beverage storage space, four stay-put beverage holders, all-terrain wheels, and an easy-grasp tow handle, this ice green cooler can handle any event. The chest cooler stores ice for up to six days at temperatures of up to 90 degrees and can hold 72 beverage cans with ice. Made of durable plastic, the exterior resists scratching and denting, and the interior features a Channel Drain for easy, no-tilt draining and clean-up. This models lid can be fitted with 24-inch cords (not included) for additional dry storage--lawn chairs, picnic blankets, beach towels--on top. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: doesn't have that great of a seal.  can turn it upside down and the lid will flip open without any hesitation.  highly disappointed in the product.  I placed a flashlight inside the box and was able to see a lot of light coming thru.  not sure how this will keep anything cold more than a day or two at the most.  I'm blessed that there is an easy return policy from the seller.  I wouldn't recommend this series of Coleman coolers.

data.title: Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad
data.description: Get a good night's rest with the Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad. The closed cell foam protects you from uncomfortable surfaces and helps insulate you from the cold ground.
data.reviewText: Not as padded as I expected. The foam itself is not very volumous and has little in the way of air pockets for compression.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad

DESCRIPTION:
Get a good night's rest with the Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad. The closed cell foam protects you from uncomfortable surfaces and helps insulate you from the cold ground.

REVIEW:
Not as padded as I expected. The foam itself is not very volumous and has little in the way of air pockets for compression.

data.title: Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description:
Sleep comfortably--even when the temperature dips below freezing--with the Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag. Long enough for people up to 6 feet 5 inches in height, this adult sleeping bag is designed to keep you warm in temperatures as low as 0 degrees Fahrenheit. The cotton canvas cover and cozy cotton flannel liner retain heat, so you feel warm from head to toe. For additional comfort, a Thermolock draft tube helps prevent heat from escaping through the zipper, and a ComfortCuff surrounds your face in soft, warm fabric. Fiberlock construction keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A two-way patented ZipPlow zipper system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. And the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N Roll integrated packing system make this backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. The Big Game Sleeping Bag is machine washable and comes with a matching flannel pillow. Stay snugly warm as you slumber on camping and hunting excursions with the Coleman Big Game sleeping bag, which is rated to temperatures as low as -5 degrees Fahrenheit. It's filled with five pounds of Holofill 808 insulation, which offers high-loft, hollow-core fibers that prevent body heat from escaping, thus holding more air inside the bag for good warmth. This super king-sized, rectangular sleeping bag measures 40 by 85 inches, and will fit most sleepers up to 6 foot, 5 inches. Other features include a heavy duty cotton cover material, cotton flannel liner, an included pillow, and Coleman's exclusive set of ComfortSmart sleeping bag technologies, including:
  • ZipPlow: Prevents zipper snags by plowing fabric away from zipper
  • FiberLock: Prevents insulation from shifting, increasing durability
  • ThermoLock: Reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer
  • Zipper GlideTailoring: Allows smooth zipper operation around the corner
  • Comfort Cuff: Surrounds your face with softness
  • Roll Control: Locks bag in place for easier rolling
About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: Buyer beware !  Once you zip Coleman sleeping bags together, they can't be separated.  I talked directly to Coleman customer service.  Even they don't know how to get them apart.

data.title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
data.description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText: Use it to roast coffee outdoors using a whirley pop popcrn popper. Superb in this role as you can place it  sheltered  from wind. Plenty  of BTU's.

data.title: Coleman Lantern Hanger
data.description: Whether it's fuel or battery-powered, the Coleman Lantern Hanger can hold your bail-handled camp light. The chain wraps tightly around trees, limbs and posts, and the support pegs give extra stability when you set the lantern on the hook. The hanger is easy to use and safe for trees.
data.reviewText: It's a great concept, and can work. But I just could not get it to hold my dual fuel lantern. First problem is the angle it is held at. The little "dip" that holds the lantern handle is held at a low point that I do not trust holding a $50 flammable object. The second problem is the S-hook that you connect the chain with is constantly falling off. I have lost mine after one weekend. I just got a nice nail from my toolbox, hammered it in, and hung my lantern. I'll play with this a little more and see if it can meet my needs.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Lantern Hanger

DESCRIPTION:
Whether it's fuel or battery-powered, the Coleman Lantern Hanger can hold your bail-handled camp light. The chain wraps tightly around trees, limbs and posts, and the support pegs give extra stability when you set the lantern on the hook. The hanger is easy to use and safe for trees.

REVIEW:
It's a great concept, and can work. But I just could not get it to hold my dual fuel lantern. First problem is the angle it is held at. The little "dip" that holds the lantern handle is held at a low point that I do not trust holding a $50 flammable object. The second problem is the S-hook that you connect the chain with is constantly falling off. I have lost mine after one weekend. I just got a nice nail from my toolbox, hammered it in, and hung my lantern. I'll play with this a little more and see if it can meet my needs.

data.title: Coleman WeatherMaster 6-Person Tent with Screen Room
data.description:
Watch nature unfold before your eyes in Coleman WeatherMaster 6-Person Tent with Screen Room. During the day, the screen room lets you enjoy the great outdoors away from bugs and can be used on warmer nights for extra sleeping space. If it rains, this tent keeps you dry thanks to WeatherTec technology, which uses a tub-like floor with patented corner welds, protected seams, and a covered zipper to help keep water out. Meanwhile, the strong frame is tested to withstand 35+ mph winds. Additionally, an angled window keeps rain out while allowing for extra air circulation. A hinged door makes for easy coming and going. Setup is simple with snag-free, continuous pole sleeves and a patented pin-and-ring system. Inside, there's enough room to stretch out on 2 queen-size airbeds, and storage pockets keep small items organized and off the floor. When it's time to head home, the tent easily packs up into an expandable carry bag with a rip strip for extra storage until you're off on your next adventure.


A patent-pending hinged door provides easy entry and exit, and a touch of at-home convenience.
The Coleman WeatherMaster 6 Tent has two rooms and plenty of space for the entire family. Up to six campers can sleep easily inside thanks to the 17-by-nine (W x D) footprint and 6 ft. 10 in. center height. Two doors�including a patent-pending hinged front door�offer easy in-and-out. An additional floor-less screened room provides extra ventilation on warm days, a great place for meals and gathering, rain or shine, and protected storage space. Coleman's Comfort System allows excellent ventilation control via Variflow adjustable airflow, a vented Cool-Air privacy Port, privacy vent window, and all-mesh ceiling. A convenient electrical access port lets you run a cord from your device in the tent to an outside power source, while an interior gear pocket helps you keep things organized. Setup is quick and simple thanks two color-coded poles and continuous pole sleeves, shock-corded fiberglass poles, an exclusive pin-and-ring design, and InstaClip attachments. Straightforward instructions are sewn into the convenient carry bag, and separate stake and pole bags are also included. Coleman's WeatherTec System
This proprietary series of interworking features keeps the weather where it belongs�outside the tent�at all times.
  • Leak-free Seams: In addition to fully-taped rainfly seams, the rainfly covers doors and windows, and incorporates easy-to-use Velcro frame attachments
  • Weather-resistant Fabric: Coated polyester fabric combined with anti-wicking thread, webbing, and zippers are designed to keep you dry
  • Protected Seams: Inverted floor seams dramatically increase weather resistance by hiding needle holes inside the tent, away from the elements
  • Waterproof Floors: Welding technology strengthens the tent floor and eliminates needle holes
  • Zipper Protection: Zipper cuff adds protection from the elements to the door
  • Wind Strong Frame: Engineered to be a stronger, more wind-responsive frame by using redesigned poles and guy-out triangles to anchor tents and increase performance
Specifications
  • 75-denier polyester taffeta fly
  • 68-denier polyester mesh inner tent
  • 1,000-denier polyethylene floor
  • 19-millimeter steel poles
The Coleman Limited Warranty
Coleman has been in business for more than 100 years, and continues to produce high-quality, affordable tents, lanterns, table-top stoves, and more. All Coleman products have a warranty against defects in material and workmanship, and the WeatherMaster 6 Tent includes a limited one-year manufacturer's warranty. About Coleman Outdoor Products
More than 100 years ago, an entrepreneurial young man named W.C. Coleman had an idea for manufacturing better lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. A century later, Coleman's current catalog features a wide-ranging array of products that make spending time outside a pleasure. The company crafts coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days, airbeds that are comfortable and won't deflate during the night, a complete line of bright and long-lasting LED lights, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and more.
data.reviewText: Not bad looking and lay out. But, when I first used it, it rained and leaked horribly. Spent an additional $90 to get a high quality water proofer.

  • 68-denier polyester mesh inner tent
  • 1,000-denier polyethylene floor
  • 19-millimeter steel poles
The Coleman Limited Warranty
Coleman has been in business for more than 100 years, and continues to produce high-quality, affordable tents, lanterns, table-top stoves, and more. All Coleman products have a warranty against defects in material and workmanship, and the WeatherMaster 6 Tent includes a limited one-year manufacturer's warranty. About Coleman Outdoor Products
More than 100 years ago, an entrepreneurial young man named W.C. Coleman had an idea for manufacturing better lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. A century later, Coleman's current catalog features a wide-ranging array of products that make spending time outside a pleasure. The company crafts coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days, airbeds that are comfortable and won't deflate during the night, a complete line of bright and long-lasting LED lights, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and more. REVIEW: Not bad looking and lay out. But, when I first used it, it rained and leaked horribly. Spent an additional $90 to get a high quality water proofer.

data.title: Coleman 16-Quart Personal Wheeled Cooler, Blue
data.description:
Keep your food and drinks cold, and keep the fun going, for up to 2 days when you roll in with a Coleman 16 Quart Performance Wheeled Cooler. Large enough to hold 22 cans, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for several people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or across the ball field, the recessed wheels and long, telescoping handle let you roll up with hardly any effort. The additional molded handles make it easy to lift your cooler in and out of the car or onto a picnic bench. Coleman provides a convenient way to keep things cold, even when you're on-the-go, with the 16-Quart Personal Wheeled Cooler. This convenient cooler can hold up to 22 12-ounce cans or two 2-liter bottles upright and features traditional Coleman construction for rugged durability. Other features include an extendable handle that retracts for convenient storage and recessed wheels for easy transport to and from your favorite sporting events and outdoor activities. This durable Coleman cooler comes with a limited manufacturer's warranty. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: The cooler was OK but it was smaller than advertised so I returned it and ordered the larger size 28 qt Igloo cooler.

data.title: Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter, 13 x 13 Feet
data.description:
The Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter, 13 x 13 Feet, assembles in 3 minutes in 3 easy steps to create a shady area outdoors. This canopy cover is made with UV Guard material that offers protection from the suns harmful rays and features 2-way roof vents to keep the air circulating when the temperature rises. The lightweight preattached telescoping poles can be assembled without having to reach over your head, so anyone can set up the tent easily, while comfort grips lock the shelter into place with pinch-free adjustability. Once the canopy tent is assembled, it offers stable shelter on grass, concrete, and asphalt thanks to its welded steel feet. When its time to pack up, this shelter can be stored and transported in the included easy-pull wheeled carry bag, which fits inside most cars. The Coleman 13-by-13-foot Back Home Instant Shelter provides an easy, convenient shelter in the backyard, at a campsite, sporting event, or wherever you need reliable cover from the sun. The Shelter's sturdy, one-piece steel frame sets up quickly and easily in only three minutes, with Coleman's exclusive push-button release levers so your fingers stay safe during setup and takedown. The heavy-duty shelter canopy gives you an instant hex-shaped shelter with a generous center height of eight feet, four inches and also features SPF 50+ UV Guard protection from the sun's rays. Features:
  • Sets up in less than 3 minutes
  • Heavy-duty 150D canopy with 50+ UV Guard sun protection
  • Sturdy 28mm one-piece steel frame
  • Exclusive push-button release levers take it easy on fingers
  • Hexagon shape for a distinct shelter
  • Wheeled carry bag
  • Footed poles for easy securing and stabilization with steel tent pegs
Specifications:
  • Shaded Area: 13 by 13 feet
  • Footprint: 10 by 10 feet
About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: I bought this back in March, put it up at the beginning of this month (June) and our winds in Vegas have been wicked this year.  First one of the joints gave way so I wired it and then the wind pushed the metal corner arm through the tent fabric.  Just buy a cheap pop up if you are in a windy city.  This was definitely not worth the money for the not even one month it lasted.

A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. data.title: Coleman RoadTrip Swaptop Steel Stove
data.description: The porcelain-coated cast iron construction is easy to clean at the campsite or at home. The chrome-plated steel wire construction keeps rust at bay, so you'll be able to bring it on your adventures year after year.
data.reviewText: 1 star less because it's too high.  Would be better if it's closer to the actual fire.  But this makes instant noodles, moka pot coffee, and 1 pot pastas a joy to make at the camp site.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman RoadTrip Swaptop Steel Stove

DESCRIPTION:
The porcelain-coated cast iron construction is easy to clean at the campsite or at home. The chrome-plated steel wire construction keeps rust at bay, so you'll be able to bring it on your adventures year after year.

REVIEW:

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText: Our family of 5 is happy with this tent.  My only complaint is that to enter the tent, you have to step over like 12" of tent.  I guess it's good to keep rain out but my 3 year old trips every time.  GREAT space inside though and easy to put up and take down.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent

DESCRIPTION:
Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.

REVIEW:
Our family of 5 is happy with this tent.  My only complaint is that to enter the tent, you have to step over like 12" of tent.  I guess it's good to keep rain out but my 3 year old trips every time.  GREAT space inside though and easy to put up and take down.

data.title: Coleman Cabin Tent with Instant Setup | Cabin Tent for Camping Sets Up in 60 Seconds
data.description: The Coleman Instant Cabin makes getting camp set up quicker and easier than ever. This instant tent has pre-attached poles that make setting up camp as simple as unfolding, extending, and securing. You can assemble the tent in about a minute and then get started on your adventure. This camping tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry if it starts to rain. An integrated vented rainfly offers added protection from the rain and improves airflow without the need for extra assembly. This backpacking tent is made from rugged Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric that stands up to the rigors of the outdoors, so you can use it reliably season after season. This 6-person tent offers enough room for two queen size air beds and comes with integrated storage pockets to help you keep small items organized. And when its time to go home, the camping tent packs away securely in the included expandable carry bag that lets you tear away the rip strip for easy packing.
data.reviewText:
I live in "Tornado Alley" in the Midwest, Kansas City area. If you don't know, we get some of the worst possible thunderstorms here that you could possibly imagine. 80+ mph winds, torrential downpours, low and VERY LOUD thunder and lightening. It feels like the roof of your house is going to blow off, as it creaks and moans the entire night during the storm.

We had one of these storms this past weekend, and I was camping in it with two of my little girls. And of course, I had my Coleman Instant Tent on it's maiden voyage. I knew we'd have bad weather, so I set a heavy duty tarp under the tent, partially to keep the water away from the tent, but also to protect the tent floor from wear. It was a typical hot and sticky midwest summer evening. Then about 2 AM, the winds came howling in at gale forces of 50 mph (according to the weather service), followed by EXTREMELY loud and heavy rain and thunder. The wind was blowing so crazily that it was hitting the tent from every different direction. Not one square inch of the tent exterior had any chance of staying dry. The rain and thunder continued for the next 5 or so hours with continued moments of VERY heavy rain and winds.

I am very pleased to say that we stayed totally dry and the tent incurred no damage from the storm. The also brand new tent (of a different Coleman model) of my friend who was next to us caved in on him and his kids at some point during the night (although he did stay dry). This Instant Tent turned out to be a great investment.

PROS
1) Easy setup... almost too easy. ;)

2) This tent weathers the storm!!! Remember, there is no fly on this tent, which is a great convenience for setup. But Coleman makes up for it with it's great seams and stronger material on this tent.

3) Very spacious with lots of head room.

4) Strong, well stitched material.

5) I like to take my tents home and put them up again so they will fully air dry before putting them away. The setup of this tent is so easy that it makes this very convenient.

6) It's much harder to lose parts on a tent that is already put together.

CONS
1) I've been involved in the Boy Scout program for decades and I've used my share of tents. This tent is sturdy, however, I can see how certain facets of the construction might not last through heavy regular use. For example the extension of the poles causes some wear on the painted surface which could cause rust. Certain critical parts of the poles and pop-up mechanism are plastic which I could see being points of failure after usage in several heavy storms or really cold weather. I can even see kids not caring for the mechanism as they should during setup and take down. However, don't let this deter you from this tent. The tent needs to be made of lighter parts in order to provide the convenience that makes this tent attractive. However, if you are planning on using this as your primary tent and/or use it quite often, keep this in mind. In my particular case, I have several tents, but wanted one that was spacious and easy to setup for quick overnight trips... This tent is perfect.

2) The bag that holds this thing is HUGE. I figure it's about twice the size (lengthwise) of a regular equivalent sized tent. This is expected because of the heavier material and the way the poles need to be constructed for the pop-up mechanism to work. This isn't a terrible thing, just don't expect it to be a small bag.

I would buy this tent again.

data.title: Coleman Portable Camping Quad Chair with 4-Can Cooler
data.description: Have a cold drink at hand at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with a Coleman Cooler Quad Chair. The cooler built into the armrest holds up to 4 cans and provides easy access to a cold drink once the one in your mesh cup holder is empty. data.reviewText:
Very large and roomy.  Took two adults and 4 kids, lots of extra room for bags and toys.  The room dividers were ok, nothing more than a thin sheet but my 8 and 9 yr old loved it.

Setup is a two person job, I did put it up once but it is hard to do alone.  No issues with product, great tent and its big!  windows and door were solid as normal, this is the second Colman tent and would never buy anything else.  The floor is tarp like and it always keeps the rain and moisture out.

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText: This is a good cooler. I use it as a lunchbox for work. I like that the hard lining can be removed if needed. The only concern so far is that this cooler is so well insulated that when I leave it in the fridge, I don't believe it is letting the cold air in. My food isn't spoiling but at times, yogurt or fruit isn't as cold as if it were simply in a bag or different style box.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner

DESCRIPTION:
Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.

REVIEW:
This is a good cooler. I use it as a lunchbox for work. I like that the hard lining can be removed if needed. The only concern so far is that this cooler is so well insulated that when I leave it in the fridge, I don't believe it is letting the cold air in. My food isn't spoiling but at times, yogurt or fruit isn't as cold as if it were simply in a bag or different style box.

data.title: Coleman Self-Inflating Camping Pad with Pillow
data.description: Enjoy additional support and cushioning with the Coleman Self-Inflating Camping Pad with Pillow. This camping sleeping pad features a comfy tufted design that adds an extra layer of padding between your sleeping bag and the ground. Equipped with a free-flow valve, the sleeping pad self-inflates with no air pump needed. Simply roll it out and open the valve, and the pad will begin inflating. When it's time to pack up, reopen the valve and push the air out using the compression straps. Thanks to its durable, weather-resistant polyester shell, this camping sleeping pad can endure night after night of use.
data.reviewText: Heavy and very plastic-y feeling and smelling.  After airing it out and leaving it inflated for a week or so, it finally smoothed out and the small faded some.  To call is "self-inflating" is true only if you want it to self inflate about half-way, and then you finish it off by blowing it up, like you have to blow up the so-so pillow.  I like Coleman stuff, but this one is pretty so-so.  In fact, that sums up this pad in my book.  Just so-so.

data.reviewText:
Great air mattress, kept my big arse comfy camping 2 nights without losing any air.

Actually was very comfortable for my wife and I.
