data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: Nice portable unit, folds up almost flat, and stands up to full size with ease. Heats up fast and has good heat distribution. Once hot I dial down the heat because it gets too hot and burns, but that saves propane. One small bottle lasts for 3-4 days camping.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill

DESCRIPTION:
Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.

REVIEW:
Nice portable unit, folds up almost flat, and stands up to full size with ease. Heats up fast and has good heat distribution. Once hot I dial down the heat because it gets too hot and burns, but that saves propane. One small bottle lasts for 3-4 days camping.

data.title: Coleman Tent Light
data.description:
The Coleman Tent Light is a great addition to any camping trip. The strong, built-in magnet allows for easy attachment without damaging the tent fabric. The light operates on four AA batteries (sold separately). The powerful xenon bulb is replaceable. A great addition to any camping trip, the Coleman tent light will keep your tent illuminated during the evening hours. The light attaches to your tent poles easily and securely via the built-in magnet, so you needn't worry about damaging your tent fabric. The light's powerful, replaceable Krypton bulb, meanwhile, offers plenty of light for reading, playing games, or chatting. Best of all, the light--which operates on four AA batteries (sold separately)--won't get in your way while you sleep or hang out, unlike standard lanterns. Made in China, the light includes an easy on/off switch. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText:
01/23/2013: I received my product very fast but after installing the batteries the product was useless. I even tried a new batteries set and yet didn't work. I am not going to give up and going to get a replacement and see what happens.

01/26/2013: Received the second one and after installing the new batteries it worked as expected. so far no complaint but I haven't used it in the field. will update later

data.title: Coleman Stove Carry Case, Black - 2000020971
data.description: Add even more portability to your Coleman stove when you pack it in a durable Coleman Stove Carry Case. This heavy-duty vinyl case protects the stove while you're storing it and transporting it. The bag's strong, easy-carry handles and durable zipper secure the stove during transport, and two storage pouches keep standard 16.4-oz. propane cylinders close.
data.reviewText: Good quality and fit.

Add even more portability to your Coleman stove when you pack it in a durable Coleman Stove Carry Case. This heavy-duty vinyl case protects the stove while you're storing it and transporting it. data.title: Coleman Quad LED Lantern Special Edition Ultra Bright 190 Lumens, Red
data.description:
Take the convenience of four lights in one with you with the Coleman Quad LED Lantern. Together the lantern shines 190 ultra-bright lumens for up to 75 hours. During an emergency, pop one of the four lights off the base for a personal flashlight. They recharge once theyre back on the base for hands-free light. The 24 LEDs last a lifetime, so they never need replacing and the lantern runs cool so its always safe to touch, even when its been on for hours. Combining an area light with four portable lanterns in a single clever unit, the Coleman LED Quad lantern is a versatile choice for indoor or outdoor use. The Quad is equipped with four removable LED panels that hold 24 LED lights total. Users can remove any of the four panels and use them independently, such as when needing a portable light for the bathroom or tent, or they can keep all four attached to the lantern and produce 190 total lumens of light. As a result, the Quad is the perfect light for group camping trips and any other occasion where folks might splinter off into smaller groups during the night. Each of the panels is a fully functioning light, with six 5mm white LEDs, a rechargeable NiMH battery, an independent on/off switch, and a top handle for carrying or hanging. The NiMH battery--which recharges when stored in the lantern base--provides up to 1.5 hours of light on its own. The lantern base, meanwhile, requires eight D-cell batteries (not included), which provide an approximate run time of 75 hours for the entire lantern. Other features include a main on/off switch located on top of the lantern, a light range of 26 feet, and a limited five-year warranty. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: I have owned this lantern for several years now, I have used it on at least 10 camping trips and during blackouts this light works great I am thinking about buying another one as a backup. Okay I picked up a backup Lantern and I found out that even thou they look a like they are not the same My old lantern is brighter then the new one I am not happy with the new one I do not know why Colman changed the light but they are not the same and if I knew this before buying the lantern I would not have purchased it, My brother purchased one thinking he was getting the same lantern as I have and he is not happy with the amount of light it puts out and is returning his, something I am thinking of doing my self!

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Camping Tent with Built-in Closet | Tenaya Lake Cabin Tent with Fast Pitch Setup
data.description: Enjoy some comforts from home while camping with the Coleman Tenaya Lake Fast Pitch 8-Person Cabin Tent with Closet. This camping tent includes a built-in closet with shelves and a hanger bar, making it easy to keep your clothes and gear organized without having to dig through your backpack. Thanks to pre-attached, color-coded top poles and hub, fast-fit feet, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension, this cabin tent with the Fast Pitch system assembles 55 percent quicker than similar Coleman tents with a conventional setup. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you and your gear stay dry, while the included rainfly offers extra weather protection. This backpacking tent offers enough room for two queen air beds and comes with a room divider for added privacy and storage pockets to keep your items stored away. An E-Port lets you easily bring power inside the tent. A hinged door lets you enter and exit the tent effortlessly, and Illumiline reflective guy lines offer greater visibility at night, so you wont accidentally trip in the dark. And when its time to pack up, the 8-person tent fits neatly inside the included wheeled carry bag for easy storage and transportation.
data.reviewText:
The rainfly leaked in the seam though the shower was not heavy. I will probably return it.
I owned 2 previous Coleman tent (6 people) and a screen but they are OK.

data.title: Coleman One-Mantle Kerosene Lantern
data.description:
Illuminate your outdoor adventures with the Coleman One-Mantle Kerosene Lantern. This durable camping lantern features a one-mantle design that casts up to 784 lumens of high-intensity light. You can light the lantern with a quick match strike and dial in the desired brightness level with the adjustable dimmer knob. The high-temperature globe resists cracking and is protected by built-in spring clips, and the porcelain-coated ventilator helps resist corrosion and rust. A bail handle makes it easy to carry or hang the lantern, and the fuel tank provides a steady base when the lantern is placed on a table or other surface. This outdoor lantern lasts up to 5.75 hours on high with 2 pints of kerosene (sold separately). It comes with a pre-installed #11 mantle and a plastic bottle to help fill the pre-heater. Sometimes, you just can't beat a classic kerosene lantern to light up your campsite. This model is iconic lantern maker Coleman's brightest such device, offering high-intensity light output with the included #11 mantles. It provides up to 5.5 hours of burn time on one two-pint kerosene tank, and -- thanks to an easy-to-operate, single-knob control -- it's as intuitive to use as it is durable. The fuel tank is coated on the interior to hold up to repeated use, and a "porcelain-ized" ventilator resists rust. Includes an automatic tip cleaner. The Coleman Limited Warranty
All Coleman products have a warranty against defects in material and workmanship, and details are included with your purchase. About Coleman Outdoor Products
More than 100 years ago, an entrepreneurial young man named W.C. Coleman had an idea for manufacturing better lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. A century later, Coleman's current catalog features a wide-ranging array of products that make spending time outside a pleasure. The company crafts coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days, airbeds that are comfortable and won't deflate during the night, a complete line of bright and long-lasting LED lights, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and more.
data.reviewText: I love Coleman lanterns. This seems to be built with the quality of old. The mantle did char up after an hour of burning. I am using the high quality clear fuel that Lowes sells for heaters. I bought better quality mantles on eBay. Then I bought a new old stock glass chimney to replace the made in China one with the large made in China lettering. The replacement says made in USA, just like the rest of the lantern. I am now using a bit of Coleman gas mixed in, which some call the Amish mix. The color of the light is a bit yellow when compared to the white color of my Coleman gas lantern.

data.reviewText: Right out of the box this thing is defective. One of the wind shields fell off when opening it. Upon inspection, the one that's still attached is held on very poorly. You'd think they'd have a better fastener to hold on the wind shields, but they don't. I guess that's what we should expect from Coleman since they have their products made in China, which makes them pieces of junk. I received this 15 minutes ago, and I already initiated the return for a refund. What a joke!

data.reviewText: Coleman electric, especially rechargeable lanterns, are complete and total junk.  I bought one and hadn't used it.  When we went to use it, it didn't work.  My spouse "fixed" it.  Yeah, the lantern lit for a whole five minutes.  So they replaced it but the same thing.  And the bulb didn't work, either.  So I called again and they CLEARLY BLEW ME OFF.  This company used to be good but they are pure junk now, living on their long term name.  So after all of this wasted money, I still went back to them and bought a dual power gas lantern.  They are still fine, but don't buy electric.  And don't expect good service.  They are pretty worthless in all aspects now.

The NiMH battery--which recharges when stored in the lantern base--provides up to 1.5 hours of light on its own. The lantern base, meanwhile, requires eight D-cell batteries (not included), which provide an approximate run time of 75 hours for the entire lantern. Other features include a main on/off switch located on top of the lantern, a light range of 26 feet, and a limited five-year warranty. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come. REVIEW: Coleman electric, especially rechargeable lanterns, are complete and total junk. I bought one and hadn't used it. When we went to use it, it didn't work. My spouse "fixed" it. Yeah, the lantern lit for a whole five minutes. So they replaced it but the same thing. And the bulb didn't work, either. So I called again and they CLEARLY BLEW ME OFF. This company used to be good but they are pure junk now, living on their long term name. So after all of this wasted money, I still went back to them and bought a dual power gas lantern. They are still fine, but don't buy electric. And don't expect good service. They are pretty worthless in all aspects now.

data.title: Coleman Elite Perfectflow Propane Lantern
data.description: Reliable, extended-run outdoor illumination can be yours with the Coleman Deluxe Propane Lantern. The 2-mantle design of this lantern creates a steady, bright glow of up to 1000 lumens of light that reaches up to 23 meters away. A quick match strike is all you need to get your lantern started, then simply dial in the perfect brightness with the adjustable control knob. Once your lantern is lit, the footed base provides stability when placed on a table, and the bail handle allows for easy hanging. No matter where you go, the PerfectFlow pressure control technology produces a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather.
data.reviewText: Over priced for being made in china. Did not come with a case as stated in the description.

Reliable, extended-run outdoor illumination can be yours with the Coleman Deluxe Propane Lantern. data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText: Well as the title says. I wanted to love this tent but it says weather tec right on it and the pole snapped the first real storm i was in which soaked all my stuff and obviously left the tent useless. Believe me when i say I'm meticulous in setting up so it wasn't a operator error. Like i said i really wanted to love it but i left me hanging.

data.title: Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen
data.description:
Bring a cooking station with full features to your next outdoor gathering with the Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen. Equipped with a 39.4 x 19.7-inch food prep area, this portable camp kitchen offers ample space for slicing meats and chopping vegetables. A removable sink easily slots into the prep area for easy cleaning. The side stand accommodates most Coleman stoves and coolers, while hanging hooks, a paper-towel holder, and wire shelves offer additional storage space for plates, utensils, and ingredients. To facilitate late-night cooking, a lantern hook provides a convenient place to hang a light. Setting up this portable kitchen is easy: Simply expand the aluminum frame, snap on the surfaces, and make sure the kitchen's footing is steady. When it's time to pack up, the kitchen folds up and easily fits into a single, self-contained case for easy carrying and storage. With its lightweight aluminum frame and sturdy construction, this folding camp kitchen is built to withstand years of rugged use. Outdoor cooking is a ton of fun, but it's not always easy to prep your meal on coolers, picnic tables, and car tailgates. Enter the Coleman deluxe Camp Kitchen, a complete outdoor food preparation center that fits inside a compact carrying case.


The Camp Kitchen includes a removable dish tub for post-meal cleanup.
The Camp Kitchen is outfitted with a rust-resistant aluminum frame made using heavy-duty channel-extruded aluminum for added strength. More important than the construction, however, is the functionality. The kitchen's main body includes a moisture-resistant MDF tabletop food prep area with plenty of space for chopping onions and shaping hamburger patties. To the side is a wire-grid extender shelf that easily holds most Coleman and other brands of camping stoves at countertop level. No more cooking on the ground or an unstable stump. It's the extras that really make the Camp Kitchen go. The unit offers a handy bottom shelf for storing items like pots, dishware, and bread, along with chrome-plated wire hooks and a spice rack/shelves along the back for mugs, spatulas, and condiments. The Camp Kitchen even includes a removable tub for washing your dishes and a produce bin. And when you're ready to head home, the Camp Kitchen folds up into an easy-to-carry suitcase-style frame that fits easily in most car trunks. Other details include stable leg braces for added support, plastic guards to protect the corners, a lantern hook, a paper-towel holder, and a hand-towel holder. The Camp Kitchen measures 74.5 by 64 by 20 inches (W x H x D) when open and 39.5 by 7 by 20 inches when closed and weighs 24.2 pounds. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: This works awesome. Its easy to tear down and assemble. No tools required. Only drawback is its a little on the heavy side. But if u want the support and durability, I guess its expected. I would definitely recommend to purchase for your camping adventures.

data.title: Coleman 100-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels, Blue
data.description:
Food, drinksand the funlast longer, when you roll in with the Coleman 100-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels. To be exact, this cooler keeps ice up to 5 days at temps as high as 90F thanks to an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls. Large enough to hold 160 cans, this spacious cooler transports and chills enough food and drinks so the entire party remains fueled and refreshed for the duration of your adventures. Wherever youre headedcampgrounds, tailgate parking lot, or across a ball fieldthe heavy-duty wheels and large tow handle let you roll over almost any terrain with little effort. The additional 2-way side handles make it easier to lift your cooler in and out of the car or onto a picnic bench. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid ensures you have a place to sit and relax, and the cup holders molded into the lid keep your drink close and prevent spills. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain lets you empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so youre ready for your next fun adventure in no time. Carry up to 160 cans with ease--and store ice for up to five days even when it's 90 degrees outside--in the Xtreme 5 100-Quart Wheeled Cooler by Coleman. With two large wheels, two-way handles and a comfortable tow handle for one-hand operation, this cooler is easy to roll even on rough terrain. After the party, it's easy to drain thanks to a rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain for no-tilt draining. Features:
  • Keeps ice up to 5 days at 90 degrees Fahrenheit
  • Holds 160 cans
  • Hinged lid with beverage holders
  • Two-way handles for flexible easy carrying
  • Comfortable tow handle for one-hand operation
  • Rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain for no-tilt easy draining
  • Two large wheels for easy pulling, even on rough terrain
  • Made with Thermozone insulation that contains no toxic CFCs, HFCs, or HCFCs
Specifications:
  • Exterior Dimensions: 36.5 by 16.75 by 18.25 inches
  • Interior Dimensions: 23.75 by 11.75 by 13 inches
  • Weight Empty: 18 pounds
  • Made in the USA
About Coleman

In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: Dont buy this product if you plan on using it more than once. Actually, just don't waste your money on this at all. The hinges on ours broke off the first time we used it and the only way we could fix it was by putting one back on sideways making it impossible to open all the way and replacement hinges won't stay in. The handle broke off when we were moving it. We did tons of research to figure out the best cooler for us and landed on this one. BIG MISTAKE. Now we have a broken piece of junk thats barely holding together and were out over $100 dollars! We were really hoping that spending more money and buying a Coleman product would be worth it. This product is just disappointing and cheaply made.

data.title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
data.reviewText:
When I first opened this and laid it out, the directions made no sense whatsoever. Fortunately I was able to figure it out and get the tent set up in a snap... or so I thought. Then I noticed the roof of the tent was sagging. Although all of the side poles had snapped easily into place, I hadn't been able to get the top one to snap at all. After multiple additional attempts and enlisting ever-stronger people to help me (ending with a guy who's 6'5"), we FINALLY got it to snap into place. When we finally got back home and set it up to air out, the same thing happened--we needed two strong adults playing tug-of-war on the top beam to get it locked. If I had purchased it rather than getting it free for review, I would have returned it.  As it is, I will never take it camping unless I have another adult with me, and/or a second tent as backup.

In case mine was simply defective, here are some other thoughts:
* Lots tent for your money - it's very tall inside and would fit 8 sleeping mats laid out flat on the floor, so it's exceptionally roomy for 3-6 people
* Weatherproofing is OK but not great - we had a couple hours of light rain and although we had a handful of puddles in the tent they were small--a tablespoon here and there rather than drenched. We were at a beach with 90-degree days and 75-degree nights so this wasn't a problem for us, but it would be a concern in more rain, or if camping in colder weather
* Includes a divider to separate the tent into two halves, which is great for privacy while changing. I was too short (5'2") to attach the one at the top of the tent, as I simply couldn't reach that high. But we did okay leaving that one loose / it turned the divider into something more like a stall in a dressing room-- provided ventilation, but you couldn't see over it
* The zippers are mildly annoying. They would snag two to three times every single time we opened or closed the zipper over the entire week of camping **but** it was super easy to unsnag, so this was a mild annoyance rather than a big one
* The tent includes two pockets for hanging small items like car keys or a phone. I had expected zero so this was a welcome surprise, although my other tents have more pockets, and more would have been helpful here too
* Six windows provide good ventilation. They don't unzip all the way--I assume for weather proofing--but they unzip enough to get the job done
* Weighs 26 pounds so it's lighter than most toddlers and easy for one person to carry, you just wouldn't want to use it for backpacking; this is for car camping only
* The tall size means it's a good source of shade when camping in overly sunny areas

Overall, it's a decent tent once set up, but getting the roof to stay put is fiendishly difficult, and more so for a tent that purports to be easy.

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: " The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent is designed for quick and easy setup, so you can spend more time enjoying the outdoors. Great for camping in warm weather, this backpacking tent is designed with large windows and a ground vent to help push warm air up and out to keep you comfortable. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you stay dry if the weather turns, while the sturdy frame with Insta-Clip pole attachments stands up to 35+ mph winds. A rainfly with an integrated door awning is included to help keep rain from getting into the tent, and the durable Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric is designed to withstand the elements for reliable use season after season. This dome tent offers enough room for one queen size air mattress and comes with built-in storage pockets to help keep your gear organized. An E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside the tent. The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent sets up in only 10 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves and packs away compactly in the included carry bag.
data.reviewText: While the tent was cheap and affordable, it was very easily pushed around by a moderate amount of wind. Due to this, the tent was almost flattened on one side. Attaching the guyline did not do much to remedy this situation. I would not recommend this tent to others.

A rainfly with an integrated door awning is included to help keep rain from getting into the tent, and the durable Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric is designed to withstand the elements for reliable use season after season. This dome tent offers enough room for one queen size air mattress and comes with built-in storage pockets to help keep your gear organized. An E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside the tent. The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent sets up in only 10 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves and packs away compactly in the included carry bag.

REVIEW:
While the tent was cheap and affordable, it was very easily pushed around by a moderate amount of wind. Due to this, the tent was almost flattened on one side. Attaching the guyline did not do much to remedy this situation. I would not recommend this tent to others.

data.title: Coleman Weathermaster II Screened 10 Person 16 X 10 Tent with Hinged Door and Autoroll Windows, 7 Feet Tall and Fits 4 Queen Air Beds
data.reviewText: This is a HEAVY  tent to move around and really ANNOYING to setup. I'm going to return it tomorrow.  I just purchased this tent on sale with high hopes. I need a tent that's large enough for my husband, myself two boys and a dog. Thank god I did test run setup in the back yard before our camping trip. The directions are clear and easy to follow, however you really need 2 people to assemble the tent. I didn't like the fact that the tent ceiling setup is separate from the walls. I much prefer a dome style tent that can be put up quickly and taken down easily. I am also not pleased with the quality. The bag that it comes had a zipper that kept jamming and a seam that was splitting. Not a good sign for the tent itself. And a total pain since you would rely on that outer bag to store & move the tent around. The REI Base Camp 6 by comparison was a cakewalk to set up and really good quality but is not quite large enough. I ordered the Kelty Discovery 8 man tent and hope that one works.

data.title: Coleman Trailhead II Cot
data.reviewText: so it's one year later.  When in one piece, this cot is super sturdy and comfortable.  It's a great length and has no crossbar.  This cot has been used on 4 camping trips and we are both well under the 300 lb weight limit.  During our 4th trip with the cots (last weekend) the ENTIRE 6 foot side of the cot ripped away from the camouflage fabric part.  End to end.  My hubby had to sleep on the ground the rest of the trip.  Oddly, we were just praising the durability of the cot to our friends the day before.  But now it is completely unusable after just 4 camping trips.  I'm hoping Coleman will make good.

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText: we ordered both green and blue.  It is as described.  We use it daily.  Great so far.

data.title: Coleman Classic Propane Stove
data.description: Coleman Classic Propane Stove About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: This is not the old Coleman products we all knew from past camping. fishing etc.  The metal side wind shields are made cheap and one of the brads holding one side on was already broken when received.  The other side and any similar fasteners would not have lasted either.  Sorry to say cannot recommend and sorry to see a great name fall to this sort of manufacturing. Returned to Amazon.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Classic Propane Stove

DESCRIPTION:
Coleman Classic Propane Stove About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.

REVIEW:
This is not the old Coleman products we all knew from past camping. fishing etc.  The metal side wind shields are made cheap and one of the brads holding one side on was already broken when received.  The other side and any similar fasteners would not have lasted either.  Sorry to say cannot recommend and sorry to see a great name fall to this sort of manufacturing. Returned to Amazon.

data.title: Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+
data.description: The Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+ offers both a grill and a stove for versatile campsite cooking. This durable steel-crafted outdoor appliance has a stove surface that fits a 10-inch pan as well as a 130-square inch grill area, letting you saut veggies while you're grilling chicken, or get the soup going while you're cooking up burgers. Each surface has independent temperature controls to give you precise control. The InstaStart push-button ignition powers up the portable grill with a simple press of a button with no matches needed, while integrated Wind Block panels help shield the flame from wind and can be folded down on calm days to be used as side tables. This outdoor grill features PerfectFlow pressure control technology to keep the heat steady even in extreme conditions and PerfectHeat technology for more efficient cooking with less fuel. An aluminized steel cooktop offers durable use and is rust resistant, and the removable grates and grease management tray offer simple and quick cleanup. The Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+ can run for up to one hour with both burners on high setting on one 16.4-ounce propane cylinder (sold separately) and produces 20,000 total BTUs of cooking power.
data.reviewText: Unable to contol the flame. I wouldn't buy it again

data.title: Coleman Pack-Away Camping Cot
data.description: Bring along a perfect relaxation spot on your next camping trip with the Coleman Pack-Away Camping Cot. Ideal for sitting, reclining, and sleeping, this versatile camping cot features an extra-wide design that provides ample resting room. It also comes equipped with a convenient removable side table with a cup holder, where you can easily keep drinks, magazines, and other personal items. The steel frame can support up to 300 pounds and is built to withstand season after season of rugged use. Thanks to its folding, portable design and included carry bag, this camping cot fits inside most car trunks and is easy to transport. The Coleman Pack-Away Camping Cot accommodates most people up to 6 feet 6 inches tall.
data.reviewText: It's very comfortable and makes tent camping great. The reason I didn't score it higher is because folding the legs is not the easiest. The other minor issue is when completely open, the legs tend to collapse relatively easy when no wait is applied.

data.title: Coleman 3000002009 28Qt Xtreme
data.description: Take the new, stylish performance of a Coleman 28 Quart extreme 3 cooler with you to the party-at the tailgate, BBQ or campsite. While your friends revel in the sleek new look, you'll enjoy the added improvements that better fit your lifestyle. The redesigned 28-quart cooler is tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright and large enough to hold up to 36 cans. Two molded cup holders help prevent spills, and the Have-A-Seat lid supports up to 250 lbs. (113.4 kg) so you'll always have a place to sit and rest. Inside the cooler's walls, extra Thermo zone insulation and extreme technology holds the cold for three days in temperatures up to 90 F (32.22 degree C), and still performs amazingly on hotter days. The foam is also free from CFCs, HFCs and HCFCs, which deplete the ozone. When the fun is done, the textured EZ-Clean top makes washing your cooler simple. Holds 36 cans-more than a case of soda fits inside a 28-quart cooler new, stylish outer Casing. Two molded cup holders keep drinks close. Have-a-seat lid supports up to 250 lbs. (113.4 kg) for a place to sit and rest retextured EZ-Clean top for quick, easy washing. data.title: Coleman Cabin Tent with Instant Setup | Cabin Tent for Camping Sets Up in 60 Seconds
data.description: The Coleman Instant Cabin makes getting camp set up quicker and easier than ever. This instant tent has pre-attached poles that make setting up camp as simple as unfolding, extending, and securing. You can assemble the tent in about a minute and then get started on your adventure. This camping tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry if it starts to rain. An integrated vented rainfly offers added protection from the rain and improves airflow without the need for extra assembly. This backpacking tent is made from rugged Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric that stands up to the rigors of the outdoors, so you can use it reliably season after season. This 6-person tent offers enough room for two queen size air beds and comes with integrated storage pockets to help you keep small items organized. And when its time to go home, the camping tent packs away securely in the included expandable carry bag that lets you tear away the rip strip for easy packing.
data.reviewText:
Edit: this will be our 5th year of useful this tent. I still love it.

I have not tried this tent in a downpour, but it stands up to a drizzle like a boss. It does not come with a  rain fly, but doesn't seem to need one.

The is seriously easy to set up, and almost as easy to take down.  It fits back in the original bag well.

data.title: Coleman Guide Series Powerhouse Dual Fuel Stove
data.description: Cook breakfast, lunch and dinner for the entire camp in any weather on the Coleman Guide Series Powerhouse Dual Fuel Stove. The cooking surface offers plenty of room for a 12-in. and 10-in. pan to simultaneously sit above two Band-a-Blu burners that deliver a total 17,000 BTUs of cooking power. With Dual Fuel Technology, which gives you the option of using Coleman Liquid Fuel or unleaded gasoline, you can take less fuel with you to save money and packing space. WindBlock panels help shield your flame from wind and can adjust in and out to fit various pan sizes. When the meal is over, the chrome-plated grate removes for simple and quick cleanup.
data.reviewText:
I bought this stove because the design is tried and true. My grandfather own one just like it, although better built in sure. The other reason I bought this is because I can use white fuel or gasoline.... I don't like carried all the bottles.

That being said I at first had trouble getting the burners to burn a true blue. If I only had one burner going the flame would be a bright yellow. I guess the stove need a little breaking in because now it fires right up and runs like a champ.

data.title: Coleman Broadband Mesh Quad Camping Chair
data.description: Bring a comfortable seat with you to the campsite with the Coleman Broadband Mesh Quad Camping Chair. Ideal for camping, picnics, or backyard barbeques, this versatile folding chair offers premium comfort in a roomy yet portable design. It features a breathable mesh back that allows the breeze to cool your back on hot days, plus an integrated mesh cup holder that keeps a refreshing beverage within your reach. Designed to ensure your comfort, the chair features an extra-wide 21.3-inch seat that offers plenty of room to lounge and adjustable arms that let you customize the chair to suit your preferences. The strong steel frame can support up to 250 pounds and withstands seasons of heavy use. And thanks to its collapsible design and convenient carrying bag, this folding chair is easy to store and transport.
data.reviewText:
Nice solid chairs, pack relatively small, relatively light.
No thrills, but also no problems.
They do what they are supposed to do, and do it well.
And cheap.
I'm happy with them - warmly recommended.

data.title: Coleman Triton Series 2-Burner Stove
data.description: Cook breakfast, lunch and dinner on the legendary durability of a Coleman Triton Series 2-Burner Stove. Once this propane camping stove starts to heat up, PerfectHeat Technology gets the temperatures just right, WindBlock Shields keep the wind from blowing the flames around and the PerfectFlow Pressure Control System keeps the heat steady on each of the high-performance 11,000-BTU burners. The spacious cooking surface is large enough to fit a 10-in. (25.4 cm) pan and 12-in. (30.48 cm) pan at the same time, and independently-adjustable controls allow you to set your heat anywhere from simmer to boil. The two burner camp stove is also efficient, with a 1-hr. runtime on high using a single 16.4-oz. propane cylinder. When the fun is done, removable heavy-duty, nickel-chrome grates make cleaning easy, and the built-in handle will have you carrying this lightweight stove effortlessly.
data.reviewText: Works great and the burner control works great but the external pipe seems to be a little flimsy. Will have to see how long it lasts.

data.title: Coleman Sportster II Dual Fuel 1-Burner Stove
data.description: The fully-adjustable Coleman Sportster II Liquid Dual Fuel Stove is an easy to use, essential addition to your outdoor gear that cooks breakfast, lunch and dinner in any weather on the single-burner. The cooking surface is compact enough to fit inside a pack, but large enough to prepare a trail meal. It gives you the option of using Coleman liquid fuel or unleaded gasoline. This all-season stove is perfect for camping, hunting and even in cases of emergency.Built-in pot support and wind protection.Filter funnel included.1.1 pint fuel capacity.Size: 7.38" x 7.8" x 6.55".Weight: 2 lb..
data.reviewText:
Ok, not sure about all the 4 & 5 star ratings on this product. I've owned both the Coleman model 533 and the model 553, it's 'big brother', and neither are impressive. They lack fine mid-range temperature control, versus the Feather 400 model, are quite temperamental to cold-starts unless on high and kept on high during the cook. The smaller tank on the 533 model means more frequent in-meal pumping of the fuel tank, and don't dare DREAM of burning gasoline in the stove, regardless what the sales marketing says - because there are NUMEROUS COLEMAN WARNINGS inside the box of what it will do to your stove and its generator...they REALLY don't want you burning unleaded gasoline in their multi-fuel stove (hey, Coleman, don't make sure a big deal out of it in the box paperwork, if you are going to sell it as an intentional dual-fuel stove in the marketing phase, ok?)

Overall, it is dependable to fire and burn, but hey, so are a few sticks of wood - and it is about as controllable for a fire as two sticks burning. It is what it is, cheap. You can do better with an Optimus or MSR all-fuel stove. Why don't you check them out first, most of them pack smaller, burn better, and are built MUCH better...and if you get their multi-fuels, they don't warn you to death not to burn multi-fuels in it, they merely tell you how to properly service it after you have. What a concept. Coleman products have become a money grabbing, poor-quality fiasco in modern times. I do not recommend the 533 or the 553 stoves, as a result of this showing in both products.

With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.

data.title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove
data.description: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory.
data.reviewText: Got this stove for it's simmer capability. A bit pricey and has plastic parts. Seems to work alright with Coleman fuel.  I did not try the multifuel capability. It is still too big to nest in cook kit.  I prefer the older Canadian Coleman that is no longer made.

data.title: Coleman Camping First Aid Kit an All Purpose First Aid Kit for Emergencies at Home, Car, Workplace, Hiking, or Survival, 205-Pieces
data.description: The Coleman Expedition First Aid Kit is the ultimate all purpose first aid kit for larger groups or outdoor adventures. This 205 piece kit features components needed in the field, including various standard and specialty bandages, first aid tape, gauze pads, antiseptic towels, antibiotic ointment, sting relief wipes, a cold pack and common first aid tools. All the first aid supplies are organized in a zipped, durable, semi-hard case. The unique EZ Find System keeps your items organized and easy to find during an emergency. There is space in the kit for your specialized medical supplies or medications so you can fully customize this first aid kit to fit your needs. The Coleman Expedition First Aid Kit is the perfect first aid kit for your home, RV, car, bug out bag, or for groups when camping, hiking, at sporting events, workplace, and more.
data.reviewText: I was a little worried when I read some of the reviews, but it has great  variety of basic stuff, and it all fits in the case with ease. I even found it has a little room to fit a few extra things you might want to add, unlike what a other reviewer said. I'm very happy with this basic little first aid kit, and impressed with how much is  actually in it.

data.title: Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter, 13 x 13 Feet
data.description:
The Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter, 13 x 13 Feet, assembles in 3 minutes in 3 easy steps to create a shady area outdoors. This canopy cover is made with UV Guard material that offers protection from the suns harmful rays and features 2-way roof vents to keep the air circulating when the temperature rises. The lightweight preattached telescoping poles can be assembled without having to reach over your head, so anyone can set up the tent easily, while comfort grips lock the shelter into place with pinch-free adjustability. Once the canopy tent is assembled, it offers stable shelter on grass, concrete, and asphalt thanks to its welded steel feet. When its time to pack up, this shelter can be stored and transported in the included easy-pull wheeled carry bag, which fits inside most cars. The Coleman 13-by-13-foot Back Home Instant Shelter provides an easy, convenient shelter in the backyard, at a campsite, sporting event, or wherever you need reliable cover from the sun. The Shelter's sturdy, one-piece steel frame sets up quickly and easily in only three minutes, with Coleman's exclusive push-button release levers so your fingers stay safe during setup and takedown. The heavy-duty shelter canopy gives you an instant hex-shaped shelter with a generous center height of eight feet, four inches and also features SPF 50+ UV Guard protection from the sun's rays. Features:
  • Sets up in less than 3 minutes
  • Heavy-duty 150D canopy with 50+ UV Guard sun protection
  • Sturdy 28mm one-piece steel frame
  • Exclusive push-button release levers take it easy on fingers
  • Hexagon shape for a distinct shelter
  • Wheeled carry bag
  • Footed poles for easy securing and stabilization with steel tent pegs
Specifications:
  • Shaded Area: 13 by 13 feet
  • Footprint: 10 by 10 feet
About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: I get tons of compliments on this. I've had for over two years and it is still in great shape. Very easy to set up and convenient to travel because of its case. Everyone is jealous of the size, I guess 10x10 is the norm.

data.title: Coleman Stainless Steel Mess Kit
data.description: Cook up and enjoy just about any meal on the trail with a compact Coleman Stainless Steel Mess Kit. The entire 6-piece set nestles inside the stock pot. Take the stock pot apart and the bottom turns into a sauce pan and the lid turns into a separate frying pan with a locking, interchangeable handle. When the meal is done, enjoy it from the included BPA-free bowl and mug. When you're done, wash the dishes in the mesh dip bag and pack it all up for the next adventure.
data.reviewText: Used these on a Coleman camping stove, the pots were fine in size for cooking for 3 people (when cooking for all 5 of us, we'd make two batches).  The detachable handle works great, I just don't know why one is not able to purchase extra of these, for added convenience; I tried.  Burned food (hard to avoid completely on the camping stove, which is very high heat) is a pain to totally scrub off, and there was a tad of warping, but still fit together, and worked & packed up fine.  The plastic bowl & cup turned out to be handy, too.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Stainless Steel Mess Kit

DESCRIPTION:
Cook up and enjoy just about any meal on the trail with a compact Coleman Stainless Steel Mess Kit. The entire 6-piece set nestles inside the stock pot. Take the stock pot apart and the bottom turns into a sauce pan and the lid turns into a separate frying pan with a locking, interchangeable handle. When the meal is done, enjoy it from the included BPA-free bowl and mug. When you're done, wash the dishes in the mesh dip bag and pack it all up for the next adventure.

REVIEW:
Used these on a Coleman camping stove, the pots were fine in size for cooking for 3 people (when cooking for all 5 of us, we'd make two batches).  The detachable handle works great, I just don't know why one is not able to purchase extra of these, for added convenience; I tried.  Burned food (hard to avoid completely on the camping stove, which is very high heat) is a pain to totally scrub off, and there was a tad of warping, but still fit together, and worked & packed up fine.  The plastic bowl & cup turned out to be handy, too.

data.title: Coleman RoadTrip Grill Light
data.description: Shed some light on your grill with the Coleman RoadTrip Grill Light. The Grill Light-Inchs universal clamp attaches to any surface up to 1.25-Inch thick -- including all Coleman RoadTrip Grills. It features a long-lasting 1-watt LED light, so you can easily grill in the dark -- or use the light wherever you need a small, portable light source. The Grill Light features High and Low settings for convenience. It-Inchs weather-resistant, so it-Inchs great for outdoor use, and has a flexible 16-Inch chrome-plated steel arm that can be directed wherever you need some illumination. The Grill Light runs on three AAA batteries (included) and carries a 1-year warranty.
data.reviewText: So far, it is very good. Very bright. Only used it acouple times. Time will tell if it deserves a 5 star. Worry a bit about rain damage.

data.reviewText: Do not buy this pad! I agree with all the bad reviews found here. You may as well sleep on the ground with no pad...that is how useless this Coleman pad is. Do yourself a favor and go buy a hammock tent and never sleep on the ground again.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: Right out of the package this thing was dull. I spent a lot of time honing the blade against a sharpening stone. I shouldn't have do that with a brand-new axe. Once sharpened, it quickly minced chunks of 2X4's into kindling. I had a much tougher time chopping through real tree branches that were only a couple of inches thick. The blade bounces off. There's something not right about the balance of this axe. It wobbles in my hand and fails to hit the branch straight on. Also, the blade quickly becomes dull again and needs constant sharpening.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Camp Axe

DESCRIPTION:
Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.

REVIEW:
Right out of the package this thing was dull. I spent a lot of time honing the blade against a sharpening stone. I shouldn't have do that with a brand-new axe. Once sharpened, it quickly minced chunks of 2X4's into kindling. I had a much tougher time chopping through real tree branches that were only a couple of inches thick. The blade bounces off. There's something not right about the balance of this axe. It wobbles in my hand and fails to hit the branch straight on. Also, the blade quickly becomes dull again and needs constant sharpening.

data.reviewText: I ordered 2 of these, one Red, one Blue. The Red one is great, filled it up and tested the spigot - no leaks, put it on its side-no leaks, put it completely upside down over night- no leaks. The blue one leaks from the lid, not acceptable I'll be returning it.

The unique stacking design offers vertical storage and use, while its two-gallon capacity is perfect for school parties and family functions. You can even use different colored jugs for different drinks so serving and filling is fast and fun. The removable spigot is easy to clean, and the large bail handle makes carrying easy.Brand: ColemanModel: 3000000736Color: BlueMaterials: PlasticDimensions: 7 inches long x 8 inches wide x 9 inches highConvenient stackable design for multiple beveragesSecure storage and easy-to-clean spigotThis item is not able to be shipped internationally or to a freight forwarding address.

REVIEW:
I ordered 2 of these, one Red, one Blue. The Red one is great, filled it up and tested the spigot - no leaks, put it on its side-no leaks, put it completely upside down over night- no leaks. The blue one leaks from the lid, not acceptable I'll be returning it.

data.title: Coleman Palmetto Cool Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description: The Coleman Palmetto Sleeping Bag is a rectangular, cool weather sleeping bag made for camping temperatures between 30 and 50 degrees. Perfect for Family camping!.ThermoLock construction reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer.ZipperGlide tailoring allows smooth zipper operation around the corners.QuickCord for easy, no tie closure.ComfortCuff surrounds your face with softness.Commercial Machine washable.
data.reviewText: Not a good sleeping bag if you ask me. I purchased this at Walmart for about the same price as it is here and I've only used it once. After only one night a large (about 9 inches) section of the seams where it is stitched came apart. The temperature rating I would say is about right on. With the unfortunate seam opening I was able to see the filler material inside and I highly doubt it is cotton as it is described. Going to return it and look for a 100% cotton inside and out. For the life of me I don't know why anyone would want that sticky poly material against their skin in any way inside or out. It feels really gross. Spend a bit more and get a better bag.

data.title: Coleman Soft Plush Top Inflated Quickbed
data.description: Take the comfort of an indoor bed with you when you sleep outside on the Coleman Queen Airbed Folding Cot with Side Tables and 4D Battery Pump. Connect the included 4D pump to the Double Lock valve for easy, leak-free inflation. Once you find the perfect firmness, the AirTight system will help keep it that way through the night, since it's factory-tested to be leak-free. The ComfortStrong coil system provides all-night support. It is more than a place to sleep with 2 side tables with cup holders to easily place your drink, a snack, a magazine, or personal items on both sides of the bed. A sewn-in cover keeps the airbed in place on the strong steel frame. When youre ready to pack up, the Double Lock valve lets you deflate the airbed just as easily, and it folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
data.reviewText:
I just plain love airbeds, can't imagine why anybody would sleep on something else. I have bought lots of them over the years.

At the local megastore, Coleman's new "single high" airbeds are cheaper than the old version, but they suck (or blow, leak, whatever). I've tried two recently. No more Coleman for a while.

I'd like a thicker material, so I have ordered the other popular "outdoor" brand that says the plastic is "15 gauge". Any other suggestions are invited. I don't want to spend $100 per mattress since the risk of failure is high with all of these things. Get a good one and it will last for over a year. Awesome bedding. But when they leak, the awesomeness turns to serious frustration.

data.title: Coleman Men's 35mm PVC/Poly Rain Suit
data.description: Keep yourself reliably dry and protected from the elements with the Coleman Men-Inchs PVC/Poly Rain Suit. Constructed of durable, waterproof 35mm PVC-coated polyester, the Rain Suit includes a rain jacket and pants. The jacket features a drawstring hood, center front zipper with storm flap to keep out moisture, back and armhole vents for air flow, and two lower pockets. The pants feature a drawstring elasticized waist for a good fit, leg openings with zipper closure & snap adjustment, articulated knees for ease of movement, and 2 convenient slit pockets that give you access to pockets on the pants you wear underneath. The Rain Suit stores compactly in the included carry bag.
data.reviewText: UPDATE... I wore this on a 2 mile walk in a light rain. Though the suit is still new looking and has no rips or tears, I was wet down to my underwear. Not soaked mind you but damp enough that the cell phone in my pocket got a foggy screen and is now sitting in a bucket  of rice. I initially reviewed this on looks alone as I ordered it before the rainy season. I assumed that, because my 20 year old Coleman suit (from when they were still made in New Braunfels TX USA) looked very similar. But my old one is practically shredded and it still offers better protection than this sub standard Chinese suit. It is better than nothing but not by much. save some money and cut a hole in a trash bag. It will work better.

data.title: Coleman 24-Can Party Stacker Portable Cooler
data.description: You'll be the life of the party when you take along the Coleman 24 Can Party Stacker Cooler to your next event. Not limited to canned drinks, the wide base fits 13 x 9 in. serving dishes, so you can easily pack something for everyone. No matter what you bring, the molded handles will make it easy to get it there. To add even more variety, grab a few coolers in different colors and organize your fun. Use one color to chill the adult drinks, another color for the kids, and keep food warm in yet another, so everyone will know where to find their favorite treats. When the fun is done, the specially designed base fits in the lid of other Party Stacker items, making them simple to stack and store until the next glorious get together.

data.title: Coleman 2 Burner Dual Fuel Compact Liquid Fuel Stove
data.description: Stove, 2-Burner Standard Compact, Dual Fuel. Whether you're staying in a cabin, tent, or under the stars, the Coleman 424-700G Two-burner Compact Dual-fuel Stove will enhance any camping adventure. Equipped with one 7,500 BTU burner and one 6,500 BTU burner, you can whip up a meal in minutes. The Coleman stove operates on clean-burning Coleman Fuel or unleaded gas and one tank gives you two hours of burn time. This stove can boil a quart of water in four minutes. All this in an amazingly small package of 17.75-inches by 11.5-inches by 4.875-inches when closed for easy packing.
data.reviewText: Excellent stove.  Especially for making larger more complicated meals.

data.title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove
data.description: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory.
data.reviewText: I wanted to like this stove while I used it for car camping.  But it has one very serious flaw.  It won't turn off and spills unburned fuel creating a massive fire hazard.  It wouldn't turn off when brand new.  So I called Coleman support who were very helpful.  We determined which part failed and they sent out a replacement, no charge.  I installed it and it ran fine for several years but recently spilled a cup of white gas on the tailgate because, again, it wouldn't turn off.  It is too dangerous to keep so I threw it in the trash.  The off switch should be a needle valve but this design is just a thin wire running through the top tube to plug the jet.  Use this at your own risk
data.text: TITLE: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove DESCRIPTION: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory. REVIEW: I wanted to like this stove while I used it for car camping. But it has one very serious flaw. It won't turn off and spills unburned fuel creating a massive fire hazard. It wouldn't turn off when brand new. So I called Coleman support who were very helpful. We determined which part failed and they sent out a replacement, no charge. I installed it and it ran fine for several years but recently spilled a cup of white gas on the tailgate because, again, it wouldn't turn off. It is too dangerous to keep so I threw it in the trash. The off switch should be a needle valve but this design is just a thin wire running through the top tube to plug the jet. Use this at your own risk

data.title: Coleman Tent Kit
data.description: Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.
data.reviewText: I'm not entirely sure why I bought this. It seemed like a good idea at the time I was buying two tents. However, once it arrived, I thought I may have been losing it. Who wants to carry a single purpose mallet around simply to hammer in tent spikes. There are cheaper and less heavy ways to accomplish the same thing. Nevertheless, I'm too lazy to send things back so I kept the kick. I expect that it will just remain in my basement as I carry my camp axe in place of the mallet.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.

data.title: Coleman 2000014864 Mosquito Head Net
data.description: Enjoy the outdoors without the pests when you're wearing a Coleman Mosquito Head Net. The fine mesh stops insects from biting, while still allowing you to see nature's beauty. The Mosquito Head Net is designed especially for wide, all-around brim hats, but still fits other styles well. data.reviewText: This is a good quality net but it does not fit ideally around the face since it will stick to the face and neck depending on the size of your head and how it is worn.  I recomend wearing it over a hat to allow for a good enough separation between the net and the face.  For the price it is a good net.  It will aliveate insect pestering a great deal but this is not the best net available on the market.

data.title: Coleman 12 oz. Enamel Mug
data.description: Grab a refreshing drink at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with an impact-resistant Coleman 12 oz. Enamel Mug. Each lightweight, steel bowl is dishwasher-safe and coated in a durable, classic blue speckled enamel finish. They also feature rolled rims for sipping comfort.
data.reviewText: We have enjoyed Coleman products for many years. Has there been a change? Apparently so. This enamelware mug is constructed of very thin, easily bendable metal lightly clad in enamelware.  The mug arrived with a nickel size chip on the rim which was badly rusted. Not only unhygienic but easily able to cut your lip. Unacceptable.  Very disappointed.
