data.title: Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description:
Sleep comfortably--even when the temperature dips below freezing--with the Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag. Long enough for people up to 6 feet 5 inches in height, this adult sleeping bag is designed to keep you warm in temperatures as low as 0 degrees Fahrenheit. The cotton canvas cover and cozy cotton flannel liner retain heat, so you feel warm from head to toe. For additional comfort, a Thermolock draft tube helps prevent heat from escaping through the zipper, and a ComfortCuff surrounds your face in soft, warm fabric. Fiberlock construction keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A two-way patented ZipPlow zipper system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. And the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N Roll integrated packing system make this backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. The Big Game Sleeping Bag is machine washable and comes with a matching flannel pillow. Stay snugly warm as you slumber on camping and hunting excursions with the Coleman Big Game sleeping bag, which is rated to temperatures as low as -5 degrees Fahrenheit. It's filled with five pounds of Holofill 808 insulation, which offers high-loft, hollow-core fibers that prevent body heat from escaping, thus holding more air inside the bag for good warmth. This super king-sized, rectangular sleeping bag measures 40 by 85 inches, and will fit most sleepers up to 6 foot, 5 inches. Other features include a heavy duty cotton cover material, cotton flannel liner, an included pillow, and Coleman's exclusive set of ComfortSmart sleeping bag technologies, including:
  • ZipPlow: Prevents zipper snags by plowing fabric away from zipper
  • FiberLock: Prevents insulation from shifting, increasing durability
  • ThermoLock: Reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer
  • Zipper GlideTailoring: Allows smooth zipper operation around the corner
  • Comfort Cuff: Surrounds your face with softness
  • Roll Control: Locks bag in place for easier rolling
About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText:
This is actually the second Coleman Big Game sleeping bag I bought.  This one was purchased for my wife.  She wanted it after I bragged about mine for so long when we camp.  Mine is a year old and I simply love it.

I love the room this bag gives me.  Normal size bags can be difficult to stretch out or turn over in the night.  A mummy bag.... forget it.  I feel tied up and only want to break free.  I am a mover when i sleep and do not stay in one position.  This bag allows me to not only move and stretch but to also let me flex my knee up and well, sleep how I want to as if I am in bed.

Now, the claim for -5 degrees, I am sorry but I cannot see that.  I do get cold easy and am one that loves my Big Buddy heater in the tent with me if it get below 50 degrees.  I have slept without the heater and this Coleman Big Game sleeping bag in 40 degrees and I was cold.  Now, it could be I am 5'7 and about 170 lbs so there is a lot of extra room in this sleeping bag but I do try to keep the top closed around me and squish in the sides when it is cool out, but I still am a bit cold.  I can see the rating of 25 to 30 degrees maybe but again, it could be just me.  But, -5 degrees I think is a bit off...

My wife said she loves this sleeping bag.  She is 5 ft and about smaller framed, but says she sleeps warm and sleeps with it unzipped on the 40 degree nights.  She likes the ability to stretch and move in the bag too.
data.text:
TITLE: Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag DESCRIPTION: Sleep comfortably--even when the temperature dips below freezing--with the Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag. Long enough for people up to 6 feet 5 inches in height, this adult sleeping bag is designed to keep you warm in temperatures as low as 0 degrees Fahrenheit. The cotton canvas cover and cozy cotton flannel liner retain heat, so you feel warm from head to toe. For additional comfort, a Thermolock draft tube helps prevent heat from escaping through the zipper, and a ComfortCuff surrounds your face in soft, warm fabric. Fiberlock construction keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A two-way patented ZipPlow zipper system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. And the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N Roll integrated packing system make this backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. The Big Game Sleeping Bag is machine washable and comes with a matching flannel pillow. Stay snugly warm as you slumber on camping and hunting excursions with the Coleman Big Game sleeping bag, which is rated to temperatures as low as -5 degrees Fahrenheit. It's filled with five pounds of Holofill 808 insulation, which offers high-loft, hollow-core fibers that prevent body heat from escaping, thus holding more air inside the bag for good warmth. This super king-sized, rectangular sleeping bag measures 40 by 85 inches, and will fit most sleepers up to 6 foot, 5 inches. Other features include a heavy duty cotton cover material, cotton flannel liner, an included pillow, and Coleman's exclusive set of ComfortSmart sleeping bag technologies, including:
  • ZipPlow: Prevents zipper snags by plowing fabric away from zipper
  • FiberLock: Prevents insulation from shifting, increasing durability
  • ThermoLock: Reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer
  • Zipper GlideTailoring: Allows smooth zipper operation around the corner
  • Comfort Cuff: Surrounds your face with softness
  • Roll Control: Locks bag in place for easier rolling
About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture. REVIEW: This is actually the second Coleman Big Game sleeping bag I bought. This one was purchased for my wife. She wanted it after I bragged about mine for so long when we camp. Mine is a year old and I simply love it. I love the room this bag gives me. Normal size bags can be difficult to stretch out or turn over in the night. A mummy bag.... forget it. I feel tied up and only want to break free. I am a mover when i sleep and do not stay in one position. This bag allows me to not only move and stretch but to also let me flex my knee up and well, sleep how I want to as if I am in bed. Now, the claim for -5 degrees, I am sorry but I cannot see that. I do get cold easy and am one that loves my Big Buddy heater in the tent with me if it get below 50 degrees. I have slept without the heater and this Coleman Big Game sleeping bag in 40 degrees and I was cold. Now, it could be I am 5'7 and about 170 lbs so there is a lot of extra room in this sleeping bag but I do try to keep the top closed around me and squish in the sides when it is cool out, but I still am a bit cold. I can see the rating of 25 to 30 degrees maybe but again, it could be just me. But, -5 degrees I think is a bit off... My wife said she loves this sleeping bag. She is 5 ft and about smaller framed, but says she sleeps warm and sleeps with it unzipped on the 40 degree nights. She likes the ability to stretch and move in the bag too.

data.title: Coleman 50-Quart Xtreme Wheeled Cooler
data.description: Perfect for any tailgate, barbecue, beach trip or campsite, the Coleman 50-quart Xtreme Cooler has more than enough space for everything you need. With a unique design, this cooler is tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles and large enough for up to 84 cans. Have a seat lidRust proof and leak resistantHolds 2-liter bottles uprightBrand: ColemanQuantity: One (1) coolerColor options: Blue/white or grey/orangeMaterials: PlasticDimensions: 22.6 inches long x 17.6 inches wide x 17.7 inches highModel: 3000001840
data.reviewText: I hate writing negative reviews, I really do, but we are sorely disappointed in this cooler.  We have used it for a week straight and the temps have only gotten over 90 one time and we kept it in the shade all the time. Not once has the ice lasted until the next day but I will say the water stayed cold for 2 days at a time.  The first 2 days we gave it a leeway thinking that once the products were cold inside it would last longer but no, the ice melted in a few hours and left a pile of water.  We have an old junky cheap cooler that works better than this (but it's broken). Maybe we just got a bad one I don't know but I want people to be warned, if you get this make sure you're not depending on it for 5 months straight and then are disappointed once on your trip.  We travel constantly for our business and were hoping this was as good as people said but we were extremely disappointed.

TITLE:
Coleman 50-Quart Xtreme Wheeled Cooler

DESCRIPTION:
Perfect for any tailgate, barbecue, beach trip or campsite, the Coleman 50-quart Xtreme Cooler has more than enough space for everything you need. REVIEW:
I hate writing negative reviews, I really do, but we are sorely disappointed in this cooler.  We have used it for a week straight and the temps have only gotten over 90 one time and we kept it in the shade all the time. Not once has the ice lasted until the next day but I will say the water stayed cold for 2 days at a time.  The first 2 days we gave it a leeway thinking that once the products were cold inside it would last longer but no, the ice melted in a few hours and left a pile of water.  We have an old junky cheap cooler that works better than this (but it's broken). Maybe we just got a bad one I don't know but I want people to be warned, if you get this make sure you're not depending on it for 5 months straight and then are disappointed once on your trip.  We travel constantly for our business and were hoping this was as good as people said but we were extremely disappointed.

data.title: Coleman High Stand,20.25" H x 18.75" W x 28" L
data.description: Coleman 2000020280 High Stand. Camping Furniture. Made of the highest quality materials. Camping Furniture
data.reviewText: Purchased this in May 2016 to hold my Coleman camp stove. When I spread it out, there was no way to "lock" mine into place as some have noted. There is nothing on the legs to lock. They just slide up but won't stay in place. So, to keep it sturdy, I purchased small hose clamps and slid them over the legs. Spread the stand apart and tighten the clamps down. Now I have a sturdy stand but I still don't trust it to hold my stove so I have a piece of plywood over the top. Didn't bother to send it back. Not worth the effort. Impulse buy.

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: " The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent is designed for quick and easy setup, so you can spend more time enjoying the outdoors. Great for camping in warm weather, this backpacking tent is designed with large windows and a ground vent to help push warm air up and out to keep you comfortable. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you stay dry if the weather turns, while the sturdy frame with Insta-Clip pole attachments stands up to 35+ mph winds. A rainfly with an integrated door awning is included to help keep rain from getting into the tent, and the durable Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric is designed to withstand the elements for reliable use season after season. This dome tent offers enough room for one queen size air mattress and comes with built-in storage pockets to help keep your gear organized. An E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside the tent. The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent sets up in only 10 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves and packs away compactly in the included carry bag.
data.reviewText: I was able to set it up by myself. The door opens on the side so it has to be tied to keep it out of the dirt.

data.title: Coleman 8D Square Pack-Away Full Size Lantern
data.description: Designed for convenience, the Coleman 8D Pack-Away Family Size Lantern features a clever telescoping design. Packed up, the lantern creates a built-in carry case for compact and easy storage and transport. Simply pull up the top to unpack the lantern to its full size (12" tall). The lantern's 15-watt fluorescent spiral U-tube provides an extra-bright, white light, with a reflector that directs light upward and outward for more efficient illumination -- it sheds an impressive 390 lumens of light on High. The light automatically turns off when the lantern is closed, to conserve your batteries. The lantern's bail handle is convenient for comfortable carrying or for hanging. The 8D Pack-Away Lantern runs for up to 24 hours on low or 12 hours on high, with a single set of eight D batteries (sold separately). The bulb and battery are easy to access and replace. Includes a limited 5-year warranty. Lantern measures approximately 12.13" x 6.81" x 6.57" extended, and weighs 2.58 pounds without batteries.
data.reviewText: This little portable is a neat design and stores away well when traveling.  I didn't give it a higher rating because there are other lights that require 8 batteries that give off more light and cost less.  Of course, they are LED not fluorescent, which is becoming pass.  This is a very good lighting unit but it has been surpassed with more light emitting LED models.

data.title: Coleman Instant Dome Tent
data.reviewText:
We bought ours at Big 5 - it was yellow. This appears to be the same with instant set-up and 6-person capacity.

Our first mistake was not trying to assemble it at home before taking it into the wilderness. We bought it for the instant setup, as my husband was doing this solo while I arrived on a later boat.

One minute turned into THREE HOURS of setup due to poor Quality Control at the factory. Some of the pieces that were supposed to be together weren't, so he couldn't set it up in a minute or two as promised. He's an engineer, so he figured out the problem fairly quickly but not the solution. Two poles were not joined where they should have been. And when he did attempt to join them, he didn't do it right at first. (This is not a simple old-school join poles together problem.) He finally figured it out and raised the tent, but a screw did pop out and he couldn't put it back in where it came from, because it was broken and wouldn't stay.

Then, a mild gust brought the entire tent down. This is a design flaw, part of the joints that make the tent an instant-set tent. If it blows slightly the wrong way, the whole thing collapses. This happened to us twice. Our only saving grace was that the mild gusts happened in the afternoon and not late at night.

One other mentioned that the tent is hard to zip around the corners. I agree. Pretty flimsy.

I am extremely disappointed. Coleman used to make good tents.

data.reviewText: It's a nice large tent, but I wish it had better ventilation and more storage pockets. Easy to set up and take down. Held up well in moderate winds.

Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent

Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText:
As far as splitting logs, this thing just doesn't seem to cut it (no pun intended).
As the back side being a hammer and having a nail remover notch however, I really do like it, and it has proved more useful than the axe itself.
If you're trying to split logs with this, I would just hit the log as hard as you can and wedge the blade into the log. Then hit the back of the axe with something else to get it to split. Also definitely pick up a sharpening tool, as axes are sharpened to a different degree than knives.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Camp Axe

DESCRIPTION:
Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.

REVIEW:
As far as splitting logs, this thing just doesn't seem to cut it (no pun intended).
As the back side being a hammer and having a nail remover notch however, I really do like it, and it has proved more useful than the axe itself.
If you're trying to split logs with this, I would just hit the log as hard as you can and wedge the blade into the log. Then hit the back of the axe with something else to get it to split. Also definitely pick up a sharpening tool, as axes are sharpened to a different degree than knives.

data.title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
data.description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
data.reviewText: Recieved my 1-burner stove today.  The burned is very nice quality, nice and heavy.  You can't go wrong with it.

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: The Coleman Sundome 6, 10'x10', dome tent is part of the classic Sundome series of tents from Coleman. The Sundome 6 sleeps 6 happy campers and includes a large door for easy entry/exit with a hooded fly to provide ventilation even in rain. A large rear hooded window also provides airflow for cross ventilation. Mesh vents on the roof provide additional ventilation and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against a leaky floor. The coated polyester fabrics on the fly and walls are rugged and durable. Like all Coleman Sundome tents it is easy to set up or take down and features an Electrical Access Port, a ground vent and an interior mesh storage pocket.Freestanding design is easy to set up and take down.2 windows and roof vents for maximum ventilation and comfort.Mesh roof aids in ventilation and reduces condensation.Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.Polyester fly is tough and durable and won't stretch when wet.1 zippered Electrical Access Port to run AC power into the tent.1 vented Cool Air Port for increased ventilation and comfort.1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient clip and continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import.
data.reviewText: Experienced camper. Bought the tent for casual car camping in temperate weather. Set it up last night.  There was some wind.  Nothing epic.  Nothing that isn't within the norm of "peak winds for any given week."  Not part of a storm system.  Within 2 hours one of the rings that anchors the tent floor to the poles and the ground had pulled out of the tent.  Had to spend the night in the back seat of the car.  When I awoke in the morning, all four rings had pulled out.  The pegs (with the rings still in them) were still attached to the ground, but my tent was stuck in a bush 20' away.  This wasn't an old/abused tent.  This was a tent I literally pulled out of the box to set it up.  What a complete waste of money.  The tent was so far away from actually performing that I cannot believe this was just one that slipped through the QA cracks.  This was simply cheap crap.  ...And the last Coleman tent I will ever buy.

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. data.title: Coleman RoadTrip Swaptop Aluminum Grill Griddle, Full Size
data.description: Get more meals out of your Coleman RoadTrip Grill by adding a durable Coleman RoadTrip Swaptop Full Size Aluminum Griddle. Swaptop compatible, you can mix and match it with other cook tops (sold separately) for more meal options. It creates 285 sq. in. of cooking space for pancakes, bacon and more.
data.reviewText:
This is almost a guaranteed MUST HAVE for any family camping trip. I was able to make pancakes in the mornings with the kids, sausage links, among many other things. I also planned on making eggs with it, but the griddle wasn't deep enough, which wasn't a problem as I had bought the Stove Grate as well.

Typically the middle area doesn't get nearly as hot as it does around the two burner spots, so things in the middle will take longer to cook? Maybe use that for the adults as by the time the kids food will be finished cooking, the middle should be as well.

Overall a good piece and makes terrific pancakes and grilled cheese!

Typically the middle area doesn't get nearly as hot as it does around the two burner spots, so things in the middle will take longer to cook? Maybe use that for the adults as by the time the kids food will be finished cooking, the middle should be as well.

data.title: Coleman Camping Coffee Maker
data.description: Easily make drip-brewed coffee while enjoying the outdoors with the Coleman Camping Coffee Maker. Designed with a steel base that fits over most 2- and 3-burner camp stoves, this camping coffee maker features a large reservoir that enables you to brew up to 10 cups of hot coffee at once. Making coffee with this outdoor coffee maker is easy. Simply spoon grounds into the removable swing-out filter basket, fill the reservoir with water, and place the coffee maker over your stove burner. Within minutes, the 10-cup glass carafe will be filled with freshly brewed coffee ready to be enjoyed in the great outdoors. A Pause 'N Serve feature lets you grab a cup before the brew cycle is finished. The Coleman Camping Coffee Maker measures 8.5 x 11 x 14 inches. About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come. END
data.reviewText: I was so excited to get this to have normal coffee while camping. Unfortunately I couldn't ever get it to work properly. The water would back up in the top causing it to overflow and make a huge mess everytime. I even tried just running water only with no filter or coffee grounds and it did the same thing. It also took way too long to start brewing.

data.title: Coleman 40-Quart PowerChill Hot/Cold Thermoelectric Cooler
data.description: You choose: keep your food warm OR cool, and never need ice or a freezer pack, with the Coleman 40-Quart PowerChill Hot/Cold Cooler. The PowerChill uses electricity to keep your food at the right temperature. On Cool mode, it cools down to forty degrees below the surrounding temperature; on Warm, it heats to 110-140 degrees above the surrounding temperature. Just plug it into your car's 12-volt outlet, or purchase the 110-volt AC adapter (sold separately) for use in the home. The PowerChill 40 Quart can hold 44 cans, plus snacks. The interior tray helps keep everything organized, and you can use the PowerChill upright, cabinet-style, or horizontally, chest-style. The door can be converted to open from the left or the right, so it'll work well in your available space. 1-year limited warranty.
data.reviewText:
It actually just came in today. I have it connected to a 10 amp power supply that came with a 12v receptacle. So far it's dropping its internal temp 10 degrees per hour.
Note 1: It arrived in perfect shape, and appears to be very well built and finished. I particularly like this line in the Manual:
  "This cooler will not chill foods and beverages as fast as your home refrigerator. Please don't expect it to do so."
Note 2:  It does NOT come with an AC adapter of any kind, contrary to some of the reviews on Amazon. However, the Manual tells how to order
  one.
Note 3:  I had an earlier version of this cooler for 5 or 10 years, and might have killed it by putting ice and icy foods in it.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: Extremely dull, the head is very small. Had to grind it down forever to get it sharp enough to cut anything. It's extremely heavy for as small as it is. I was very disappointed all around. Spend a little more and get an axe that doesn't suck

data.title: Coleman Camper’s Toilet Paper, 3 Rolls
data.description: Take an everyday hygiene necessity on camping trips with Coleman Campers Toilet Paper. Includes 3 rolls of biodegradable, septic-safe 2-ply toilet paper that easily fits in any camping backpack. Each roll contains at least 65 squares of premium tissue and comes in a case that also doubles as a dispenser for added convenience. Compact and convenient, Coleman Campers Toilet Paper lets you take an essential indoor comfort wherever the trail leads.
data.reviewText:
Toilet paper in the woods is for sissys.

Here's what you do... squat and do your biz... wait for a squirrel to come by.  Grab said squirrel and use as anal brush. Sometimes after too many smore's the night before I have to two-squirrel it.  No biggie.

 It's quick, clean and leaves no trace (except of course on the squirrel - but it will rain soon).  I mean who needs toilet paper anyways when you have tree rats?

Right?

Orrrr... just steal a roll of that Charmin stuff from your mom's house and bend it so the core closes, place in zip lock bag (also from mom's house) and stick in pack.  Or napkins from McDonalds.  That all works too.

Or you could go blow $7.33 on this little gem of an idea.

data.title: Coleman WeatherMaster 6-Person Tent with Screen Room
data.description:
Watch nature unfold before your eyes in Coleman WeatherMaster 6-Person Tent with Screen Room. During the day, the screen room lets you enjoy the great outdoors away from bugs and can be used on warmer nights for extra sleeping space. If it rains, this tent keeps you dry thanks to WeatherTec technology, which uses a tub-like floor with patented corner welds, protected seams, and a covered zipper to help keep water out. Meanwhile, the strong frame is tested to withstand 35+ mph winds. Additionally, an angled window keeps rain out while allowing for extra air circulation. A hinged door makes for easy coming and going. Setup is simple with snag-free, continuous pole sleeves and a patented pin-and-ring system. Inside, there's enough room to stretch out on 2 queen-size airbeds, and storage pockets keep small items organized and off the floor. When it's time to head home, the tent easily packs up into an expandable carry bag with a rip strip for extra storage until you're off on your next adventure.


A patent-pending hinged door provides easy entry and exit, and a touch of at-home convenience.
The Coleman WeatherMaster 6 Tent has two rooms and plenty of space for the entire family. Up to six campers can sleep easily inside thanks to the 17-by-nine (W x D) footprint and 6 ft. 10 in. center height. Two doors�including a patent-pending hinged front door�offer easy in-and-out. An additional floor-less screened room provides extra ventilation on warm days, a great place for meals and gathering, rain or shine, and protected storage space. Coleman's Comfort System allows excellent ventilation control via Variflow adjustable airflow, a vented Cool-Air privacy Port, privacy vent window, and all-mesh ceiling. A convenient electrical access port lets you run a cord from your device in the tent to an outside power source, while an interior gear pocket helps you keep things organized. Setup is quick and simple thanks two color-coded poles and continuous pole sleeves, shock-corded fiberglass poles, an exclusive pin-and-ring design, and InstaClip attachments. Straightforward instructions are sewn into the convenient carry bag, and separate stake and pole bags are also included. Coleman's WeatherTec System
This proprietary series of interworking features keeps the weather where it belongs�outside the tent�at all times.
  • Leak-free Seams: In addition to fully-taped rainfly seams, the rainfly covers doors and windows, and incorporates easy-to-use Velcro frame attachments
  • Weather-resistant Fabric: Coated polyester fabric combined with anti-wicking thread, webbing, and zippers are designed to keep you dry
  • Protected Seams: Inverted floor seams dramatically increase weather resistance by hiding needle holes inside the tent, away from the elements
  • Waterproof Floors: Welding technology strengthens the tent floor and eliminates needle holes
  • Zipper Protection: Zipper cuff adds protection from the elements to the door
  • Wind Strong Frame: Engineered to be a stronger, more wind-responsive frame by using redesigned poles and guy-out triangles to anchor tents and increase performance
Specifications
  • 75-denier polyester taffeta fly
  • 68-denier polyester mesh inner tent
  • 1,000-denier polyethylene floor
  • 19-millimeter steel poles
The Coleman Limited Warranty
Coleman has been in business for more than 100 years, and continues to produce high-quality, affordable tents, lanterns, table-top stoves, and more. All Coleman products have a warranty against defects in material and workmanship, and the WeatherMaster 6 Tent includes a limited one-year manufacturer's warranty. About Coleman Outdoor Products
More than 100 years ago, an entrepreneurial young man named W.C. Coleman had an idea for manufacturing better lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. A century later, Coleman's current catalog features a wide-ranging array of products that make spending time outside a pleasure. The company crafts coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days, airbeds that are comfortable and won't deflate during the night, a complete line of bright and long-lasting LED lights, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and more.
data.reviewText: My wife insisted on having a huge tent that would fit a queen sized air bed and a playpen. This fit the bill, so she ordered it. it didn't take long to assemble, but exoframes went out in the 80s for a good reason, I don't know why Coleman wants to bring them back. The rigid door frame was a nice touch and made it easy to get an out of, but I think the screened area is too small to be useful. There are a lot of parts and stakes, but the instructions are fairly clear. Best of all, it fit back in the bag when I was done.

data.title: Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart
data.description: Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.
data.reviewText: This is the second cooler of this model that broke on us mid-trip. Bought in May and used it only 2 weekends (I'm in August as I write). I would think Coleman could make coolers that last longer than a few months. Highly dissatisfied. Get a better cooler.

data.title: Coleman Dunnock Cold Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description: Ideal for camping at higher altitudes or in cooler months, the Coleman Dunnock Cold-Weather Sleeping Bag helps you sleep comfortably in temperatures as low as 20F. This sleeping bag features Fiberlock construction that keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A cotton cover and ultra-soft cotton flannel liner also keep you warm, as well as a Thermolock draft tube that prevents heat loss through the zipper. The bottom of the bag unzips to provide extra ventilation on warmer nights. The 2-way patented ZipPlow system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. Plus, the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N' Roll integrated packing system make this lightweight backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. This sleeping bag has a spacious design that can accommodate most people up to 5 feet 11 inches tall and is machine washable for easy cleaning.
data.reviewText: I wish they would be a bit more descriptive in their descriptions of these.  This has the cotton feel exterior and interior.  i was hoping for the silky, smooth, polyester.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Dunnock Cold Weather Adult Sleeping Bag

DESCRIPTION:
Ideal for camping at higher altitudes or in cooler months, the Coleman Dunnock Cold-Weather Sleeping Bag helps you sleep comfortably in temperatures as low as 20F. This sleeping bag features Fiberlock construction that keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A cotton cover and ultra-soft cotton flannel liner also keep you warm, as well as a Thermolock draft tube that prevents heat loss through the zipper. The bottom of the bag unzips to provide extra ventilation on warmer nights. The 2-way patented ZipPlow system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. Plus, the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N' Roll integrated packing system make this lightweight backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. This sleeping bag has a spacious design that can accommodate most people up to 5 feet 11 inches tall and is machine washable for easy cleaning.

REVIEW:
I wish they would be a bit more descriptive in their descriptions of these.  This has the cotton feel exterior and interior.  i was hoping for the silky, smooth, polyester.

data.title: Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Grill Case for LX Series Grills
data.description: Add even more portability to your Coleman RoadTrip LX Series Grill when you pack it inside a Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Case. This heavy-duty, weather-resistant case includes easy-glide wheels for more mobility and top and side handles for better control. A durable zipper and inner straps secure the grill the entire trip, and inside pockets fit accessories and condiments for whatever meal youre planning to cook up.
data.reviewText: It's took a few tries to make it fit but it's a great case/cover.  Glad I have it.

data.title: Coleman Palmetto Cool Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
data.description: The Coleman Palmetto Sleeping Bag is a rectangular, cool weather sleeping bag made for camping temperatures between 30 and 50 degrees. Perfect for Family camping!.ThermoLock construction reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer.ZipperGlide tailoring allows smooth zipper operation around the corners.QuickCord for easy, no tie closure.ComfortCuff surrounds your face with softness.Commercial Machine washable.
data.reviewText:
This bag seemed about the right size and weight I wanted when i first unpacked the box.  Immediately the zipper on the outer bag snagged and proceed to break off.  After unrolling it and trying it out, it seems really cheaply made, The zipper on the sleeping bag seems pretty flimsy as well so I expect that to break soon too.  Haven't camped with it yet, but I don't have very high hopes that it will make for a pleasant experience in the 40 degree's it advertises.

I thought coleman was a good name in camping gear?  4 out of the 9 things I just bought for my upcoming trip were Coleman and they were all very junky looking (tent kit, lantern, mess kit, camp pillow).  The Suisse Sport stuff I purchased all seems to be way better made than the coleman stuff.

Edit:

Took it camping once.  The stitching on the bottom zipper ripped so there was a hole by my feet.  By the time I woke up the hole grew to a 10inches long because the stitching just continued to fall out along the zipper.

Coleman has become the worst brand I have had experience with, I tossed this thing in the dumpster, I will never by a Coleman product again.

data.title: Coleman 32-oz. Aluminum Bottle(Colors may vary)
data.description: The Coleman Aluminum Bottle is lightweight and durable, great for hiking, camping, and any activity when you want a beverage handy. The bottle's topper seals tightly with a heavy O-ring to prevent leaks, and the loop on top makes it easy to carry the bottle or attach to a backpack, strap or carabiner. The bottle holds up to a liter of liquid. Colors may vary (red, black, blue or silver).
data.reviewText: This product is flimsy. Cap cracked and chipped in first week of use and the container dents so easily. Can't use with broken cap so garbage in a week. Don't buy look elsewhere.

data.title: Coleman Classic Propane Stove
data.description: Coleman Classic Propane Stove About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: Classic design that can't be beat (at least at this price).

Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.

REVIEW:
Classic design that can't be beat (at least at this price).

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText:
It's a decent enough grill.  Really lacks any control over flame.  High might as well be "Volcano" and low might as well be "Afterburner"

That's fine.. my first cook on it went ok, once I got a feel for it, the second was better.

What kills me is the migraine inducing rattle when the thing is in a car being moved.  The grill plates rattle, the wheels rattle, the lid rattles..Fine..  I remove the plates, bungee the lid down, and tape the wheels in place.

Then the hinge hardware rattles too.  And it's all tightened down.  Sigh.  Call Coleman, it's normal for it to do that.

So, it's in the box.. Waiting for UPS to pick it up.

data.title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove
data.description: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory.
data.reviewText:
I purchased this a few months ago after the power at home went out for 6 hours and I could not heat up any dinner.  My thought was you always have some unleaded gas around the house (in your car or for the boat).

I forgot about it until we had the 6.1 earthquake Sunday here in Hawaii - I opened the box, found some unleaded gas - filled it up, pumped the tank up a few stokes, and lit the burner - hot coffee in no time.  This burns very hot and heated a tea kettle of water in about 5 minutes - burns clean with no soot or smoke.

I'd highly recommend one for your emergency kit - you never know who long the power might be out.

data.title: Coleman Trailhead II Cot
data.reviewText: When I got them and opened them up 1 was already damaged. This isn't a review about the seller as they are willing to help by exchanging however I need them and I need them now. Don't have time for all of that. I have to wire the broken one together and hope it holds. So far it has. The greater concern I have is the taughtness of the fabric. Pretty taught when new. A 200lb guy will have this stretched out in about 2 nights, my back is starting to hit the aluminum frame below. I will not ever again purchase any item made by Coleman PERIOD.  Been burnt on everything I've bought of there's for years and I'm done with them. I would recommend spending a little extra. It'll go a long way in the end. These are not military grade. Come to think of it pretty sure the US Military doesn't use anything made by Coleman. I could be wrong tho.

data.title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove
data.description: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory.
data.reviewText: this is the 3rd coleman stove I own. the first 2 where the cheapest model available from walmart and since they would not work for more than 4 or 5 camping escapades I decided to give this better quality version a try. Big mistake. I took it for a 4 day camping spring trip into the smokey mountains and used 100% unleaded gas (no ethanol added) and it worked beautifully the first 2 days. on the third it was already showing signs of poor combustion, it was hard to start, and would only build up the pressure and temperature needed for it to work on it's own, after 15 minutes of pumping! (in hot weather!)... I recommend you either go with propane if you're going to use it every now and then, or go with the real deal and spend a couple of hundred dollars on the most expensive ones (I hear MSR for $200-$300 is a good brand but never tried it, I'm personally going to get a britelyt stove for $100 because I have one of their lanterns and it is a superb quality product) I'm not giving it 1 star just because if I had the patience to open and clean the thing every other day of use and perhaps if I used the FAR MORE EXPENSIVE coleman gas, it would work fine, but as for me I'm returning it and I'm done with coleman liquid fuel powered products (also had 3 lanterns before buying the brytelit AKA petromax lantern).

It does not stay together, so when you go to let the legs down everything fell apart on the top and of course the paint chipped and is scrapped.  First time out, looks like a piece of junk we've abused for years, very disappointed

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText: Finally got to use my tent. I bought it then got really sick before I could use it. I was excited. Where I go on the Washington coast can be really windy. (This past trip, winds reach up near 40+MPH). It held up great, better than I expected in the wind. Where it failed was when the rain hit. It wasn't as near windy when the rain hit but it poured a solid 20+ hours straight. I was impressed till morning when the leaks started. One was on the door seam. Not the fly but on the double stitched seam. The other was in the top of the reason fly dripping down over my sleeping bag. At 37F that was cold. It did hold up to the wind with that great long rain fly but the wind would still sneak underneath and flow through. I didn't feel like the tent was going to take off with me on it like my last tent but it still leaked. I'm passed my time to return it. I'm sad that it didn't hold up. I feel like I did my research but in the end maybe I got a lemon? I would try again if I could return it but I'm past the time. Sad.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent

DESCRIPTION:
The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..

REVIEW:
Finally got to use my tent. I bought it then got really sick before I could use it. I was excited. Where I go on the Washington coast can be really windy. (This past trip, winds reach up near 40+MPH). It held up great, better than I expected in the wind. Where it failed was when the rain hit. It wasn't as near windy when the rain hit but it poured a solid 20+ hours straight. I was impressed till morning when the leaks started. One was on the door seam. Not the fly but on the double stitched seam. The other was in the top of the reason fly dripping down over my sleeping bag. At 37F that was cold. It did hold up to the wind with that great long rain fly but the wind would still sneak underneath and flow through. I didn't feel like the tent was going to take off with me on it like my last tent but it still leaked. I'm passed my time to return it. I'm sad that it didn't hold up. I feel like I did my research but in the end maybe I got a lemon? I would try again if I could return it but I'm past the time. Sad.

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText: Decent capacity, but not as insulating as I would have thought. Foam walls could be thicker.

data.title: Coleman 3-Piece 48 Quart Cooler Combo
data.description: A variety of coolers for all occasions, the Coleman 48 Quart Cooler Combo is a three piece set that's great for backyard parties, camping, day-trips, and more. A 48-quart cooler, a 5-quart cooler, and 1/3-gallon jug are included in this combo pack, each crafted from solid, lightweight plastic and featuring eco-safe ThermOZONE insulation that retains a cool atmosphere for hours. The coolers are deep enough to keep 2-liter soda bottles store upright; the 48-quart cooler has a capacity for up to 63 cans while the 5-quart cooler can hold six cans. Both coolers are outfitted with a hinged lid. A leak-resistant, rustproof channel makes draining the larger cooler simple. The smaller cooler's lid transforms into a convenient serving tray with four cup holders. The jug features a wide, easy-pour mouth and twist-on cap. Set comes in red or blue (colors subject to availability). You know the name, and you?ll love the company. Coleman is unparalleled in the world of camping and outdoor gear. What started out as an innovation in gas-powered lamps over 100 years ago has grown to an international company that designs and constructs top-quality tents, lanterns, stoves, coolers, sleeping bags, and more. No matter what your outdoor sport: hiking, camping, fishing, hunting, boating, swimming, four-wheeling, relaxing, or tailgating, Coleman has what you need to help get you out there to enjoy it. Includes 1/2-gallon jug, personal cooler, & a 48 qt. cooler. Choose from the available color options. ThermOZONE insulation. Personal cooler reverses to a serving tray. Main cooler can hold up to 68 cans.
data.reviewText: Purchased this set as a gift to a son who is always borrowing my 30 year old cooler. My old one has experienced being bucked off a horse on a horse back trip in Wyoming and is still working great.

data.title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. data.reviewText: If you leave the plastic liner in it, it can hold 7 or 8 twist top drinks but there is no room for an ice pack - you would need to use real ice.

data.title: Coleman Tent for Camping | Elite Montana Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: Stretch out and relax in style with the Coleman Elite Montana 8-Person Lighted Tent. This cutting-edge camping tent features a cabin-like design with a variety of outdoor technologies to enhance any camping trip. The large outdoor tent can fit three queen size air beds, ensuring your whole family has plenty of room. When the sun goes down, you keep the party going with the six built-in CPX LED lights that provide 100 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by four D batteries (sold separately) or the included CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge and comes with an energy pack that can power virtually anything with a USB slot. During the day, the auto-roll windows neatly roll down when you unzip them and can open at an angle to let in light but not water when it rains. This 8-person tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry. An extended door awning creates a dry space for you to store gear and enter the tent, while the patented hinged door makes it easy to come and go. The heavy-duty tent sets up easily in about 15 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves, a patented pin-and-ring system, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension. Clip on the included rainfly for added wetness protection, or gaze at the stars through the mesh ceiling.
data.reviewText:
This tent is very poorly made. The pole for the door doesn't fit. Once it's wrestled in place, it pulls the tent crooked. Either the pole is too long, or the "ports" that the pole fits into were put too close together. When the door is closed, there is a 3-4 inch gap at the bottom of the door because it doesn't lay flush. This is after much adjusting to try and make it work. When I took the door apart, the plastic cap for the pole got stuck in the port, it didn't come out with the pole.
As you can see in the picture, the rainfly does not cover the ends of the tent. We put this tent up in our yard for a weekend so we could see how it repelled rain. Inside was dry where the rainfly was. Water seeped in on the exposed ends. I wish Coleman would have made the fly a few feet longer to protect the entire tent.
As other reviewers have said, the small windows do little for ventilation. Finally, one of the seams on our tent it looks like the sewing machine got snagged and the thread is tangled and ripped.
For a large tent, it is narrow. Another foot or two front to back would make this ideal for a 4 person family that uses air mattresses.
Overall, I am very disappointed in this tent both in design and quality.
Update 6/6/18: We painstakingly waterproofed the entire tent. We have camped three times in this tent, and twice it rained heavily. The waterproofing we did held up. However, the poles for the door started to splinter during our last camping trip, and one of them tore through the fabric of the door. I am calling Coleman today to see about return options.

This cutting-edge camping tent features a cabin-like design with a variety of outdoor technologies to enhance any camping trip. Either the pole is too long, or the "ports" that the pole fits into were put too close together. When the door is closed, there is a 3-4 inch gap at the bottom of the door because it doesn't lay flush. This is after much adjusting to try and make it work. When I took the door apart, the plastic cap for the pole got stuck in the port, it didn't come out with the pole.
As you can see in the picture, the rainfly does not cover the ends of the tent. We put this tent up in our yard for a weekend so we could see how it repelled rain. As other reviewers have said, the small windows do little for ventilation. Finally, one of the seams on our tent it looks like the sewing machine got snagged and the thread is tangled and ripped.
For a large tent, it is narrow. Another foot or two front to back would make this ideal for a 4 person family that uses air mattresses.
Overall, I am very disappointed in this tent both in design and quality.
Update 6/6/18: We painstakingly waterproofed the entire tent. We have camped three times in this tent, and twice it rained heavily. The waterproofing we did held up. However, the poles for the door started to splinter during our last camping trip, and one of them tore through the fabric of the door. I am calling Coleman today to see about return options.

data.title: Coleman Portable Butane Stove with Carrying Case
data.description: On-the-go cooking just got a little easier with the Coleman Portable Butane Stove with Carrying Case. With an ultra-portable design and included carry case, this stove is ready for any camping adventure and quickly sets up on any outdoor tabletop, supporting up to a 10-inch pan. Attach an 8.8 oz. butane cylinder (sold separately), light the automatic matchless ignition with the turn of a knob, and in no time, youre cooking with up to 7,650 BTUs of power for up to 1.25 hours. Wind baffles shield your flame from the wind, and a large stable base makes stirring easy. Use your stove for years to come thanks to an easy-to-clean, durable porcelain-coated grate, rust-proof aluminum burner, and made-to-last enameled steel exterior.

DESCRIPTION:
On-the-go cooking just got a little easier with the Coleman Portable Butane Stove with Carrying Case. data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText: Makes a pretty good daily lunch bag.  I've been using it for several months and the only problems I have had it keeping the strap on my shoulder.  The shoulder pad on the strap doesn't provide much "grip" and slides off the shoulder easily.

data.title: Coleman Self-Inflating Camping Pad with Pillow
data.description: Enjoy additional support and cushioning with the Coleman Self-Inflating Camping Pad with Pillow. This camping sleeping pad features a comfy tufted design that adds an extra layer of padding between your sleeping bag and the ground. Equipped with a free-flow valve, the sleeping pad self-inflates with no air pump needed. Simply roll it out and open the valve, and the pad will begin inflating. When it's time to pack up, reopen the valve and push the air out using the compression straps. Thanks to its durable, weather-resistant polyester shell, this camping sleeping pad can endure night after night of use.
data.reviewText: A must have for all campers!

data.title: Coleman Cooler Stainless Steel Hinges and Screws
data.description: For replacement on most Coleman Coolers with plastic hinges as well as Coleman steel Coolers made after 2000.
data.reviewText: The hinges on my Coleman cooler did not fail at the flex point, but the screws pulled out of the plastic. I could have reused the hinges and simply moved them over an inch or two, but I decided to replace with the stainless steel ones instead. The problem that I see is that the screws are simply screwing into very thin plastic. It is extraordinarily easy to over tighten the screw and simply strip  it out of the plastic. The screws that pass through the body of the cooler could easily be replaced with through bolts and nuts, however you cannot do this for the ones that screw into the lid so just be very careful when screwing in that you do not over tighten and strip out the plastic. You do not need to drill holes, you can simply just press firmly with your screwdriver and the screws will bite right into the plastic. Once you get them started this way, then be very careful to apply only minimal torque and do not over tighten.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Cooler Stainless Steel Hinges and Screws

DESCRIPTION:
For replacement on most Coleman Coolers with plastic hinges as well as Coleman steel Coolers made after 2000.

REVIEW:
The hinges on my Coleman cooler did not fail at the flex point, but the screws pulled out of the plastic. I could have reused the hinges and simply moved them over an inch or two, but I decided to replace with the stainless steel ones instead. The problem that I see is that the screws are simply screwing into very thin plastic. It is extraordinarily easy to over tighten the screw and simply strip  it out of the plastic. The screws that pass through the body of the cooler could easily be replaced with through bolts and nuts, however you cannot do this for the ones that screw into the lid so just be very careful when screwing in that you do not over tighten and strip out the plastic. You do not need to drill holes, you can simply just press firmly with your screwdriver and the screws will bite right into the plastic. Once you get them started this way, then be very careful to apply only minimal torque and do not over tighten.

data.title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
data.description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
data.reviewText: The tent is easy to setup and tear down, but the stake rings attached to the tent itself are not very sturdy. A 20-ish mph wind whisked through our campsite and tore one of them completely off. The stitching used held up, but tore through the tent floor material relatively easily. I can't see this tent lasting longer than 3 or 4 camping trips, but most people ditch their tents and replace them every year or 2 anyway.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent

DESCRIPTION:
Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.

REVIEW:
The tent is easy to setup and tear down, but the stake rings attached to the tent itself are not very sturdy. A 20-ish mph wind whisked through our campsite and tore one of them completely off. The stitching used held up, but tore through the tent floor material relatively easily. I can't see this tent lasting longer than 3 or 4 camping trips, but most people ditch their tents and replace them every year or 2 anyway.

data.title: Coleman Steel Replacement Tent Pole, 8-Foot
data.description: Bring new life to an old tent or canopy with the Coleman Steel Replacement Tent Pole. Compatible with most camping tents and canopies, this durable tent pole easily adjusts from 3 to 8 feet to offer sturdy support where needed. A friction lock secures the replacement pole at the desired height and ensures the pole will not budge when holding up a canopy, tent, or other shelter. Durable steel construction gives this pole the reliable strength to withstand the rigors of outdoor use, helping bring older tents back to their full potential.
data.reviewText: I would have given it a 4 but had to fix everyone of them . After I fixed they work great, and are very strong.

data.title: Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen
data.description:
Bring a cooking station with full features to your next outdoor gathering with the Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen. Equipped with a 39.4 x 19.7-inch food prep area, this portable camp kitchen offers ample space for slicing meats and chopping vegetables. A removable sink easily slots into the prep area for easy cleaning. The side stand accommodates most Coleman stoves and coolers, while hanging hooks, a paper-towel holder, and wire shelves offer additional storage space for plates, utensils, and ingredients. To facilitate late-night cooking, a lantern hook provides a convenient place to hang a light. Setting up this portable kitchen is easy: Simply expand the aluminum frame, snap on the surfaces, and make sure the kitchen's footing is steady. When it's time to pack up, the kitchen folds up and easily fits into a single, self-contained case for easy carrying and storage. With its lightweight aluminum frame and sturdy construction, this folding camp kitchen is built to withstand years of rugged use. Outdoor cooking is a ton of fun, but it's not always easy to prep your meal on coolers, picnic tables, and car tailgates. Enter the Coleman deluxe Camp Kitchen, a complete outdoor food preparation center that fits inside a compact carrying case.


The Camp Kitchen includes a removable dish tub for post-meal cleanup.
The Camp Kitchen is outfitted with a rust-resistant aluminum frame made using heavy-duty channel-extruded aluminum for added strength. More important than the construction, however, is the functionality. The kitchen's main body includes a moisture-resistant MDF tabletop food prep area with plenty of space for chopping onions and shaping hamburger patties. To the side is a wire-grid extender shelf that easily holds most Coleman and other brands of camping stoves at countertop level. No more cooking on the ground or an unstable stump. It's the extras that really make the Camp Kitchen go. The unit offers a handy bottom shelf for storing items like pots, dishware, and bread, along with chrome-plated wire hooks and a spice rack/shelves along the back for mugs, spatulas, and condiments. The Camp Kitchen even includes a removable tub for washing your dishes and a produce bin. And when you're ready to head home, the Camp Kitchen folds up into an easy-to-carry suitcase-style frame that fits easily in most car trunks. Other details include stable leg braces for added support, plastic guards to protect the corners, a lantern hook, a paper-towel holder, and a hand-towel holder. The Camp Kitchen measures 74.5 by 64 by 20 inches (W x H x D) when open and 39.5 by 7 by 20 inches when closed and weighs 24.2 pounds. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: I love this when I'm camping. It is sturdy and moderately easy to store. Wish the stove holder was a solid material instead of mesh.

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
data.reviewText: The jug is fine but the spigot is terrible. The top part snapped off with very little use. If you decide to buy this make sure to buy 2 or 3 additional spigots. Better yet look to another brand.
