data.title: Coleman Retro Rechargeable Battery Powered Lantern (Family Size)
data.description:
Lantern, Retro Full Size, Rechargeable Tired of camping lanterns that burn through batteries like nobody's business? Turn to the Coleman rechargeable lantern, which offers up to nine hours of runtime on a single charge. The family-size lantern features an 11-watt fluorescent spiral U-tube bulb that shines brightly regardless of its locale, with a reflector that directs the light upward and outward so more people can enjoy it. Should the bulb burn out, the lantern offers a quick-release lever for easy bulb replacement (an extra tube is included). Most importantly, when the lantern starts to lose power, you can easily recharge the battery via a 120-volt AC outlet or a vehicle's 12-volt DC power port (cords included). That means you can recharge the lantern before leaving on a trip, or you can recharge it at the campsite using your car's lighter port. Other details include an "emergency on" circuit that helps overcome power failures, a built-in nightlight, and a recyclable battery. The lantern--which also runs extremely cool to protect against fires--measures 6.75 by 14 by 8 inches (W x H x D) and weighs 7.78 pounds. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
data.reviewText: This is the second one and it too has failed. Coleman was very nice in replacing the first one after it would not recharge. Now the electricity went out and it worked for 20 minutes and quit. And when it did work, there was little difference between high and low setting...if it mattered. I notice there is another Retro Lantern on Amazon which looks the same but has high ratings. I strongly advise to research this model and think twice before buying. Now I have to look for one that works!

data.title: Coleman SupportRest Elite PillowStop Double-High Airbed
data.description: Let your guests sleep as wonderfully as they do in their own bed with a Coleman SupportRest Elite Pillow Top Double High Airbed. They'll fall into absolute comfort with the luxurious pillow top. The antimicrobial treated sleep surface resists odor, mold, mildew and fungus from forming on the product, so you can offer a bed to your friends and family with confidence.
data.reviewText: This mattress inflates in less than five minutes and holds its air amazingly well.  It does lose a small amount of air each night if we use it for consecutive nights, but that is to be expected.  It takes a little over 10 minutes to deflate it and it does not have to be plugged in to do so.  It has held up to 415 pounds nicely and comfortably, and has also held three kids at once sleeping on it.  It is pretty lightweight and easy to put up and take down.  This is a very nice mattress for a very nice price!  We have only used it for indoor "sleepovers" so far to test it out, but there is an outing planned in the near future.  This is a typical trustworthy Coleman product!

data.title: Coleman Lantern Carry Case
data.description: Protect your lantern with the Coleman Lantern Carry Case so it'll be ready to go the next time you head outdoors. This tough case helps protect against dents, scratches and broken globes. The base has room for extra mantles and miscellaneous other items. The case latch locks securely and is positioned at the top for easy loading and unloading. data.title: Coleman 24-Piece Enamel Dinnerware Set, Red
data.description:
Serve up your favorite foods around the campfire or portable stove with the Coleman 24-Piece Enamel Dinnerware Set. Made of double-fired enamel and featuring a timeless red speckled design, this outdoor dinnerware set offers classic style and durability and is dishwasher safe for easy cleaning. This 24-piece set includes four 10-inch dinner plates, four 12-ounce coffee mugs with handles, and four 6-inch bowls, making it a great choice for family meals at picnics, BBQs, and campsites. It also includes a silverware kit with four plastic-handled forks, spoons, and knives stored in a convenient roll-up pouch for easy organization and storage. Ideal for camping, picnicking, and even backyard use, this set of Coleman speckled dinnerware includes everything you need to eat a meal under the stars. The 24-piece set is outfitted with four 10-inch dinner plates, four 10-ounce mugs, and four 6-inch bowls, giving you and three co-campers enough containers for a main course, a side salad, and an after-dinner hot chocolate. In addition, the set comes with four knives, four spoons, and four forks, so you don't need to rely on plastic silverware when eating. Best of all, the set is durable and dishwasher-safe, with a red-speckled enamel surface that holds up to heavy use. The silverware also includes a compact carrying case to keep the cutlery bunched together. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.

data.title: Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter with LED Lighting, 10 x 10 Feet
data.description:
The Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter with LED Lighting, 10 x 10 Feet, lets you enjoy the outdoors any time of day or night. Great for picnics, barbecues, campouts, and more, this sun shelter is made with UV Guard material that offers UPF 50+ protection from the sun's harmful rays. The lightweight telescoping poles with pinch-free Comfort Grip technology can be extended comfortably without having to reach over your head, allowing you to set up the canopy tent quickly. Once assembled, the canopy offers stable shelter on grass, concrete, and asphalt thanks to its welded steel feet. When the sun goes down, the fun can keep going thanks to the 6 built-in CPX LED lights that supply 400 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by 4 D-cell batteries or one CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge (both sold separately). When its time to pack up, the shelter folds down quickly and fits easily inside most cars. Whether you're camping, dining in the backyard, or hosting a wedding reception, the Coleman Instant Canopy makes an ideal companion. The Instant Canopy is Coleman's most innovative canopy, with a three-step setup that takes three minutes or less to pitch.


The canopy is easy to set up via a 3-step, 3-minute process.
No more fiddling with hard-to-interpret pole assemblies or poorly designed attachment systems. More importantly, the canopy comes with four built-in ultra-bright LED fixtures at the top of the shelter. With light emanating out from the ceiling, you can dine at dusk with full overhead illumination and dance after dark without bringing out multiple lanterns. The light system even comes with a convenient dimmer switch, so you can choose low-light ambience all the way up to bright task lighting. The lighting system operates on four D batteries (sold separately), but is also compatible with Coleman's CPX 6 battery power system. The canopy is sturdily constructed, with a 28mm steel frame and a tough cover that's made using UVGuard fabric, which provides a superior 50+ ultraviolet protection factor. And Coleman added pinch-free Comfort Grips to the frame, so you can protect your fingers during setup and takedown. Just a single squeeze locks or releases the frame for fast setup or teardown. The Instant Canopy offers 10 feet by 10 feet of shade and a center height of 9 feet 4 inches. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.

data.title: Coleman Portable Butane Stove with Carrying Case
data.description: On-the-go cooking just got a little easier with the Coleman Portable Butane Stove with Carrying Case. With an ultra-portable design and included carry case, this stove is ready for any camping adventure and quickly sets up on any outdoor tabletop, supporting up to a 10-inch pan. Attach an 8.8 oz. butane cylinder (sold separately), light the automatic matchless ignition with the turn of a knob, and in no time, youre cooking with up to 7,650 BTUs of power for up to 1.25 hours. Wind baffles shield your flame from the wind, and a large stable base makes stirring easy. Use your stove for years to come thanks to an easy-to-clean, durable porcelain-coated grate, rust-proof aluminum burner, and made-to-last enameled steel exterior.

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText: Really hard to regulate temperature. Have to be careful or everything burns.

data.title: Coleman Camping Stove | FyreCadet Propane Portable Stove, 2-Burner
data.description: Serve up a family feast in no time with the Coleman FyreCadet Propane Stove. Each 11,000-BTU burner utilizes our revolutionary HyperFlame technology, which provides an even heat distribution across the pan and better wind resistance to cut boiling time in half when compared to a standard burner in 3 m/sec wind. Wind Block pan supports also provide 360 of wind protection and eliminate the need for side panels so you can use up to a 12-in. and 10-in. pan at the same time. If you're looking for more meal options, the Swaptop interchangeable cooktops let you change out the pan supports with a grill grate or griddle (both sold separately). When you're ready to cook, just hit the Instastart push-button ignition and dial in your heat with the two independently adjustable burners. The PerfectFlow pressure control technology produces a steady fuel stream and consistent burner performance in all kinds of weather. When you're done cooking, the built-in handle and latching lid make for easy carrying back home until you're ready to create your next camping culinary masterpiece.
data.reviewText: Lights easily as stays lit in high winds. However it's HOT. Food cooks fast and burns quickly. The low setting is not low so it's super hard to adjust temp. We bought the grill plates as well.

data.title: Coleman Camper’s Toilet Paper, 3 Rolls
data.description: Take an everyday hygiene necessity on camping trips with Coleman Campers Toilet Paper. Includes 3 rolls of biodegradable, septic-safe 2-ply toilet paper that easily fits in any camping backpack. Each roll contains at least 65 squares of premium tissue and comes in a case that also doubles as a dispenser for added convenience. Compact and convenient, Coleman Campers Toilet Paper lets you take an essential indoor comfort wherever the trail leads.
data.reviewText:
The pictures, description, etc all clearly state/show that this product comes as three individually packaged rolls in a bag. This was important to me, as I'd planned to split them up between three bags to be kept in my cars. However, the individual rolls are NOT in individual cases/bags, and in fact are just loose in the primary bag.

Yes, they're useful as toilet paper, but only water resistant and durable if kept together in the original packaging. Not what I wanted.

data.title: Coleman Camping First Aid Kit an All Purpose First Aid Kit for Emergencies at Home, Car, Workplace, Hiking, or Survival, 205-Pieces
data.description: The Coleman Expedition First Aid Kit is the ultimate all purpose first aid kit for larger groups or outdoor adventures. This 205 piece kit features components needed in the field, including various standard and specialty bandages, first aid tape, gauze pads, antiseptic towels, antibiotic ointment, sting relief wipes, a cold pack and common first aid tools. data.title: Coleman 9 x 7 ft. Instant Canopy
data.description: Quickly create shade for sporting events, tailgates, and other outdoor occasions with the Coleman Instant Beach Canopy. This pop up canopy assembles in three minutes in just three steps to create a shady area outdoors. The canopy cover is made with 150 D UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays. The lightweight pre-attached telescoping poles can be assembled without having to reach over the head, so anyone can set up the tent easily, while comfort grips lock the shelter into place with pinch-free adjustability. Once the canopy tent is assembled, it offers stable shelter on grass, concrete, and asphalt thanks to its welded steel feet. And when its time to pack up, the Coleman Instant Beach Canopy can be stored and transported in the included easy-pull wheeled carry bag, which fits inside most cars.

data.title: Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Grill Case for LX Series Grills
data.description: Add even more portability to your Coleman RoadTrip LX Series Grill when you pack it inside a Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Case. This heavy-duty, weather-resistant case includes easy-glide wheels for more mobility and top and side handles for better control. A durable zipper and inner straps secure the grill the entire trip, and inside pockets fit accessories and condiments for whatever meal youre planning to cook up.
data.reviewText:
First...the Coleman Roadtrip grill and this bag are VERY HEAVY. That's not a bad thing but it was MUCH MUCH heavier than I imagined it would be. I've got a el-cheapo gas grill that's much larger that weighs HALF what this portable one does. NOT impressed with the weight to features.

This is a nice THICK bag. The rollers are a nice add-on to drag it around by.
This is a very big bag. I easily fit inside it and I'm not a small guy (don't ask WHY I tried).
The pockets for the burners is pretty tight and the velcro is aggravating the way it's orientated.
Well built...does the job but, again, not what I would term 'portable'. I would NOT go this route again.

data.title: Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart
data.description: Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.
data.reviewText: Nice looking cooler.  I had it latched and forgot and gently pulled up the lid, and the entire locking attachment on the inside came out.  Pretty sure thats not supposed to happen.  I was always a true beliver in Coleman products, but after this last camping trip....not so much.

data.title: Coleman RoadTrip LXX Portable Propane Grill, Black
data.description: Show off your grilling skills on camping trips or at tailgate parties with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Portable Propane Grill. This outdoor grill combines a collapsible, portable design with convenient features that facilitate grilling in tough conditions. The grill's individually adjustable dual burners each blast out 10,000 BTUs of heat, giving you enough cooking power to prepare a wide range of foods, from grilled veggies to thick slabs of steak. The Swaptop interchangeable design allows you to switch out the grill grates for a griddle top or stove grate (both sold separately), while the Instastart ignition lets you light the burners by simply pushing a button. Ideal for summer cookouts, this portable gas grill also excels in chilly conditions thanks to PerfectFlow technology, which helps ensure steady heat output. Sliding tables on both sides of the grill provide easy access to ingredients and utensils and can be tucked away for easy transport and storage. With its 285-square-inch cooktop, this grill offers a large cooking surface area without sacrificing portability. Its collapsible stand sets up in seconds and folds up for compact storage, while its large handle and wheels make the grill easy to transport. And thanks to its porcelain-coated cast iron grates and removable grease tray, this camp grill is easy to clean.
data.reviewText: Uneven heat and handle is too short.  Wish I had gotten something different.  I was really excited, as I had read so many positive reviews, and videos on how to convert this to LP from your already regulated rig line.  Sadly, after using this on multiple trips, I wish I had gotten something else.  It cooks very unevenly, and every-time I try to use the wheels, the lower table top pops out and drags/scrapes on the ground.  Just overall poorly designed and executed.  I've bought and used different swap tops for it, and it doesn't help.  All I've come up with is leaving one side on high to seer meats, and then placing them on the other side that is off (as low is still too hot) to slow cook.  Not what I wanted or was expecting.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman RoadTrip LXX Portable Propane Grill, Black

DESCRIPTION:
Show off your grilling skills on camping trips or at tailgate parties with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Portable Propane Grill. This outdoor grill combines a collapsible, portable design with convenient features that facilitate grilling in tough conditions. The grill's individually adjustable dual burners each blast out 10,000 BTUs of heat, giving you enough cooking power to prepare a wide range of foods, from grilled veggies to thick slabs of steak. The Swaptop interchangeable design allows you to switch out the grill grates for a griddle top or stove grate (both sold separately), while the Instastart ignition lets you light the burners by simply pushing a button. Ideal for summer cookouts, this portable gas grill also excels in chilly conditions thanks to PerfectFlow technology, which helps ensure steady heat output. Sliding tables on both sides of the grill provide easy access to ingredients and utensils and can be tucked away for easy transport and storage. With its 285-square-inch cooktop, this grill offers a large cooking surface area without sacrificing portability. Its collapsible stand sets up in seconds and folds up for compact storage, while its large handle and wheels make the grill easy to transport. And thanks to its porcelain-coated cast iron grates and removable grease tray, this camp grill is easy to clean.

REVIEW:
Uneven heat and handle is too short.  Wish I had gotten something different.  I was really excited, as I had read so many positive reviews, and videos on how to convert this to LP from your already regulated rig line.  Sadly, after using this on multiple trips, I wish I had gotten something else.  It cooks very unevenly, and every-time I try to use the wheels, the lower table top pops out and drags/scrapes on the ground.  Just overall poorly designed and executed.  I've bought and used different swap tops for it, and it doesn't help.  All I've come up with is leaving one side on high to seer meats, and then placing them on the other side that is off (as low is still too hot) to slow cook.  Not what I wanted or was expecting.

data.title: Coleman One-Mantle Kerosene Lantern
data.description:
Illuminate your outdoor adventures with the Coleman One-Mantle Kerosene Lantern. This durable camping lantern features a one-mantle design that casts up to 784 lumens of high-intensity light. You can light the lantern with a quick match strike and dial in the desired brightness level with the adjustable dimmer knob. The high-temperature globe resists cracking and is protected by built-in spring clips, and the porcelain-coated ventilator helps resist corrosion and rust. A bail handle makes it easy to carry or hang the lantern, and the fuel tank provides a steady base when the lantern is placed on a table or other surface. This outdoor lantern lasts up to 5.75 hours on high with 2 pints of kerosene (sold separately). It comes with a pre-installed #11 mantle and a plastic bottle to help fill the pre-heater. Sometimes, you just can't beat a classic kerosene lantern to light up your campsite. This model is iconic lantern maker Coleman's brightest such device, offering high-intensity light output with the included #11 mantles. It provides up to 5.5 hours of burn time on one two-pint kerosene tank, and -- thanks to an easy-to-operate, single-knob control -- it's as intuitive to use as it is durable. The fuel tank is coated on the interior to hold up to repeated use, and a "porcelain-ized" ventilator resists rust. Includes an automatic tip cleaner. The Coleman Limited Warranty
All Coleman products have a warranty against defects in material and workmanship, and details are included with your purchase. About Coleman Outdoor Products
More than 100 years ago, an entrepreneurial young man named W.C. Coleman had an idea for manufacturing better lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. A century later, Coleman's current catalog features a wide-ranging array of products that make spending time outside a pleasure. The company crafts coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days, airbeds that are comfortable and won't deflate during the night, a complete line of bright and long-lasting LED lights, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and more.
data.reviewText: I like the old school kerosene stuff I love Coleman equipment the only issue I have with this lantern and it would have been 5 stars is that the bail holds the top on, not a nut like the regular lanterns have thus making the globe rattle bad I have been  working on adjusting the heat shield trying to get the tabs bent properly to hold the globe securely in place the extra step to preheat the generator is no big deal I just use the yellow bottle of heat as mentioned before it is menthol and works great  and it only takes about five minutes. I used it up deer hunting a few weeks ago it was great  I would recommend it  one note  it is not as bright as a gas lantern but still gets the job done

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText:
I understood from the previous reviews that the axe would arrive dull. Dull is an understatement. It's about as sharp as a marble. It took a power-grinder and an hour of grinding and sharpening before it was barely usable. That said, it's an axe. If you swing it hard you might can split some wood, but it'd be hard to fell a small tree with it. The backside is good for hammering in tent pegs, just be careful.
The grip is hard plastic, and despite finger grooves, not very ergonomic.

In hindsight, I would rather have splurged and bought a good, pre-sharpened, reliable axe or hatchet that will last me a lifetime.
The poor man buys everything twice.

EDIT: I lowered the rating on this axe because I bought a better axe. Buying a good axe made me see how terrible this one was. Go ahead and spend the money on a better one that will last a while and will actually work.

Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.
data.reviewText:
Coleman has a long and rock solid reputation as the king of car camping.  But they've really let themselves slack.  I've owned this cooler for close to a decade now, and honestly it's not that good.  The lid doesn't stay shut.  The drain hole has a pathetic little plug and not a screw cap.  The handles are weak and bend easily.  And most importantly, it doesn't seem to keep things cold very well.

It's great for short simple things like running to the grocery store, but that's about it.  The Coleman Xtreme coolers do a better job of keeping things cold, but they aren't that great either.

data.title: Coleman Compression Sack
data.description: Whether you're packing or storing your gear, reduce your bulk with a Coleman Compression Sack. Its unique design compresses large sleeping bags or other soft items with three straps that tighten around the outside. When you're ready to go, the sturdy web handle on the bottom makes one-handed carrying simple.
data.reviewText:
Would give 1 star if it wasn't so cheap. Fell apart with minimal use. Seams especially near the straps gave way. The straps themselves are double stitched but could use reinforcement on the excess bit. The stuff sack material itself is very thin which inherently makes it very light and perfect for backpacking. Maybe one or two trips and this will fall apart. Getting all the seams double stitched with thicker thread.

Will steer clear of this. Already ordered one from REI which is about the same in cost but hopefully better in quality.

data.title: Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter with LED Lighting, 10 x 10 Feet
data.description:
The Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter with LED Lighting, 10 x 10 Feet, lets you enjoy the outdoors any time of day or night. Great for picnics, barbecues, campouts, and more, this sun shelter is made with UV Guard material that offers UPF 50+ protection from the sun's harmful rays. The lightweight telescoping poles with pinch-free Comfort Grip technology can be extended comfortably without having to reach over your head, allowing you to set up the canopy tent quickly. Once assembled, the canopy offers stable shelter on grass, concrete, and asphalt thanks to its welded steel feet. When the sun goes down, the fun can keep going thanks to the 6 built-in CPX LED lights that supply 400 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by 4 D-cell batteries or one CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge (both sold separately). When its time to pack up, the shelter folds down quickly and fits easily inside most cars. Whether you're camping, dining in the backyard, or hosting a wedding reception, the Coleman Instant Canopy makes an ideal companion. The Instant Canopy is Coleman's most innovative canopy, with a three-step setup that takes three minutes or less to pitch.


The canopy is easy to set up via a 3-step, 3-minute process.
No more fiddling with hard-to-interpret pole assemblies or poorly designed attachment systems. More importantly, the canopy comes with four built-in ultra-bright LED fixtures at the top of the shelter. With light emanating out from the ceiling, you can dine at dusk with full overhead illumination and dance after dark without bringing out multiple lanterns. The light system even comes with a convenient dimmer switch, so you can choose low-light ambience all the way up to bright task lighting. The lighting system operates on four D batteries (sold separately), but is also compatible with Coleman's CPX 6 battery power system. The canopy is sturdily constructed, with a 28mm steel frame and a tough cover that's made using UVGuard fabric, which provides a superior 50+ ultraviolet protection factor. And Coleman added pinch-free Comfort Grips to the frame, so you can protect your fingers during setup and takedown. Just a single squeeze locks or releases the frame for fast setup or teardown. The Instant Canopy offers 10 feet by 10 feet of shade and a center height of 9 feet 4 inches. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
data.reviewText: Absolute crap. The roof isn't even remotely waterproof. Combine this with the fact that the latch assemblies that hold the roof up failed the second time it was put up. Well I tell myself "at least it's still under warranty." Only to discover that in order to be repaired this thing which tips the scales at 60 lbs, or better has to be shipped prepaid & insured to Witchita, KS in order for them to make a determination. Since this will cost about half the price of the product new with no guarantee of satisfaction. My solution is to write a crap review here, set fire to the product, and never buy Coleman again.

data.title: Coleman Evanston Tent
data.description: Enjoy the fresh air without the bugs when you're camping in a Coleman Evanston Tent. It's designed for simple setup and easy carryingperfect for car campers and long camping trips. The tent even has a screened porch where you can sleep on warm, dry nights. If it rains, the tent is designed to hold up to the weather.
data.reviewText:
We have owned this tent for several years.  It has been used for family camping and girl scout camping when I want to put half a dozen girl scouts in the same tent.

The only problem we've had with it was when we went to Monahans State Park (Texas), and the wind was really high one night.  The tent couldn't handle the wind, and the high sides that make it wonderful because you can stand up in it also turn it into a big sail in high winds.  We ended up purposely collapsing the tent before a pole had a chance to break.

I've found that it's easy to put up once you get the hang of it (I can put it up by myself in about 10 minutes).  Putting the rain fly on without a helper is much more difficult just because you have to get it over the top.

For those asking about size, I'm attaching a few pictures - I was checking equipment before a girl scout campout, so I had two queen air mattresses and a twin blown up inside the tent earlier.  You can see that there's plenty of room for those plus a lot more for any gear they bring along.

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: " The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent is designed for quick and easy setup, so you can spend more time enjoying the outdoors. Great for camping in warm weather, this backpacking tent is designed with large windows and a ground vent to help push warm air up and out to keep you comfortable. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you stay dry if the weather turns, while the sturdy frame with Insta-Clip pole attachments stands up to 35+ mph winds. A rainfly with an integrated door awning is included to help keep rain from getting into the tent, and the durable Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric is designed to withstand the elements for reliable use season after season. This dome tent offers enough room for one queen size air mattress and comes with built-in storage pockets to help keep your gear organized. An E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside the tent. The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent sets up in only 10 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves and packs away compactly in the included carry bag.
data.reviewText:
It's a Coleman.  Great entry-level tent that's easy to put up, even for a 10-year old.  Love the bathtub floor (I sure wish tents had that feature when I was a kid, it would have saved me many nights of gear getting soaking wet).  I also love that the pole sleeves are as small as possible and that it relies on pole clips for most of the length of the poles.  That makes getting the poles in and up so much easier than a full-length sleeve.  I wouldn't exactly call them snag-proof but they're a notable improvement over full-length sleeves.

I like the fly on this model, it has one pole that goes front-to-back and does not require much bending.  Similar Coleman models have a smaller fly pole that you have to bend into a U-shape, putting tremendous tension on the fiberglass and subjecting it to splintering.  This model's fly pole is thicker and only has a slight bend so I expect it to last longer.

Biggest complaint is the zipper gets caught in the "zipper cover" all the time and for kids who don't know any better, they just keep pulling until it's completely jammed.  This is not a great design and needs to be revisited.

As with all tents on the market, "4-person" means 4 people sleeping right up on each other with no room for gear.  So this is really a tent for 3 people at most.  We've slept 3 in it comfortably (1 adult/2 kids, 2 adults/1 kid) although we did start to run short on room for gear at some point.

data.title: Coleman Soft Plush Top Inflated Quickbed
data.description: Take the comfort of an indoor bed with you when you sleep outside on the Coleman Queen Airbed Folding Cot with Side Tables and 4D Battery Pump. Connect the included 4D pump to the Double Lock valve for easy, leak-free inflation. Once you find the perfect firmness, the AirTight system will help keep it that way through the night, since it's factory-tested to be leak-free. The ComfortStrong coil system provides all-night support. It is more than a place to sleep with 2 side tables with cup holders to easily place your drink, a snack, a magazine, or personal items on both sides of the bed. A sewn-in cover keeps the airbed in place on the strong steel frame. When youre ready to pack up, the Double Lock valve lets you deflate the airbed just as easily, and it folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
data.reviewText: definitely sagged for me through the night...my husband liked it

data.title: Coleman Water Carrier, 5-Gallon
data.description: Take your own clean water to the campsite in a Coleman 5-Gallon Collapsible Water Carrier. Just remove the spigot to quickly fill it with ice and water, and then turn the valve to get the water going. Two molded handles, one on the top and one on the front, make carrying easy. You can even hang it from a post or tree for easy access without cracking or denting the carrier. When the fun is done, the carrier folds flat for compact storage.
data.reviewText: I used this for three days on a camping trip I just returned from and won't be using it again. The faucet only lets a dribble pass, so there was no way to get it to give me a lot of water without waiting for a long time. I also doesn't maintain its shape.

data.title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small
data.description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small. Designed to fit small Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 20.4 x 12 x 4.5 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
data.reviewText: It will do the job but i wish it was padded a little better. I expected a little more from Coleman.

data.title: Coleman Tent for Camping | Elite Montana Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: Stretch out and relax in style with the Coleman Elite Montana 8-Person Lighted Tent. This cutting-edge camping tent features a cabin-like design with a variety of outdoor technologies to enhance any camping trip. The large outdoor tent can fit three queen size air beds, ensuring your whole family has plenty of room. When the sun goes down, you keep the party going with the six built-in CPX LED lights that provide 100 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by four D batteries (sold separately) or the included CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge and comes with an energy pack that can power virtually anything with a USB slot. During the day, the auto-roll windows neatly roll down when you unzip them and can open at an angle to let in light but not water when it rains. This 8-person tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry. An extended door awning creates a dry space for you to store gear and enter the tent, while the patented hinged door makes it easy to come and go. The heavy-duty tent sets up easily in about 15 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves, a patented pin-and-ring system, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension. Clip on the included rainfly for added wetness protection, or gaze at the stars through the mesh ceiling.
data.reviewText: Its about as expected.  I fit four teenage boys in this tent and they had enough room.  They really don't want to be anywhere near each other and NEED their space.  This was fine.  But sleep 8?  No.

Its about as expected.  I fit four teenage boys in this tent and they had enough room.  They really don't want to be anywhere near each other and NEED their space.  This was fine.  But sleep 8?  No.

data.title: Coleman Camp Axe
data.description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
data.reviewText: Careful when using it.  The handle is short and takes some getting used to.  It's a hatchet, not an axe!!!

data.title: Coleman 52 qt. Xtreme Cooler
data.description: The Coleman 52 qt. Xtreme Cooler is perfect for weekend getaways, with the technology to keep ice up to five days in 90-degree weather and the capacity to hold 82 cans. It features two-way handles for easy lifting and carrying, and the rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain allows for no-tilt draining.   You know the name, and you?ll love the company. Coleman is unparalleled in the world of camping and outdoor gear. What started out as an innovation in gas-powered lamps over 100 years ago has grown to an international company that designs and constructs top-quality tents, lanterns, stoves, coolers, sleeping bags, and more. No matter what your outdoor sport: hiking, camping, fishing, hunting, boating, swimming, four-wheeling, relaxing, or tailgating, Coleman has what you need to help get you out there to enjoy it. Durable plastic construction. Features Xtreme 5 technology and Thermozone insulation. Designed to hold up to 82 cans. Includes hinged lid with 4 cup holders and 2-way handle. Dimensions: 27.75L x 14.95W x 17.25H in..
data.reviewText:
I just got back from a six day camping trip. I filled this cooler  with 20 pounds of ice cubes (leaving about 1/3rd of the space free for food) and much to my surprise, the ice was gone early today but the water was icy cold still and the remaining food was fine.

 In other words, this cooler performs phenomenally if the ratio of ice to food is what is needed.

 I did keep it in the shade, but the temperatures were in the 80s, and I am very very happy with it's excellent performance.

data.title: Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+
data.description: The Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+ offers both a grill and a stove for versatile campsite cooking. This durable steel-crafted outdoor appliance has a stove surface that fits a 10-inch pan as well as a 130-square inch grill area, letting you saut veggies while you're grilling chicken, or get the soup going while you're cooking up burgers. Each surface has independent temperature controls to give you precise control. The InstaStart push-button ignition powers up the portable grill with a simple press of a button with no matches needed, while integrated Wind Block panels help shield the flame from wind and can be folded down on calm days to be used as side tables. This outdoor grill features PerfectFlow pressure control technology to keep the heat steady even in extreme conditions and PerfectHeat technology for more efficient cooking with less fuel. An aluminized steel cooktop offers durable use and is rust resistant, and the removable grates and grease management tray offer simple and quick cleanup. The Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+ can run for up to one hour with both burners on high setting on one 16.4-ounce propane cylinder (sold separately) and produces 20,000 total BTUs of cooking power.
data.reviewText: this is the worst camp stove i have ever purchased, and i have bought a lot of stoves, so i have a great background on out door camping.  this stove slides around on table, and the cook ware also slips and slides, takes for ever to heat coffee water and goes through gas super quick. cooked one meal on 1 tank of gas, this was an easy meal without a long cooking time, next day heated up soup and made coffee and fried sausages and went through another tank of gas.  replaced this stove with a camp chef combo stove and oven. love it and purchased another one for our cabin

data.title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
data.description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
data.reviewText:
The Coleman Hooligan 3 is an excellent tent for less-than-ideal spring/summer/fall conditions, but design elements keep me from recommending this tent for all-around use. The positive features actually can be negatives under certain circumstances. I rate this tent at 3.5 stars for general purpose use, and 3.75 for those who want a tent better able to handle tough weather. If the zippers and the multitude of lightweight Coleman-issue stakes were all sturdier, I'd make that a 4.

The Hooligan is similar to a two pole Coleman Sundome tent constructed primarily of netting covered by a complete, waterproof fly with a good sized vestibule. There are none of the "windows" common to the Sundome family of tents; airflow through the tent is via the door/dome/side Ventilation openings. The design makes it impossible to close off the side vents, rendering this tent impractical for very cold weather use. With 13 stakes necessary for the fly, and 4 for the tent itself, setting it up in rocky or very sandy soils could be a bit of a challenge. Otherwise, actual set-up is straightforward (a usage tip-do NOT stake the tent out prior to setting up the main poles; before you do, check the tension across the front of the tent, as I found that a lot of strain can be placed on the tent zipper if you don't proceed in this manner, and the zipper itself is a little on the wimpy side). With the vestibule, most folks will need to do a double-dip or drop to hands and knees to enter the Hooligan through the vestibule and door combination. With a 58" height, standing up inside is out of the question for most of us, but that's to be expected in a small tent. At 8' x 7', the tent can handle a double size air bed, with some room to spare, making it adequate for 2 people (ok, 2 humans and one stinky dog), but forget about the three person designation unless you leave all of your gear outside.

The 13 stake fly covers the inner tent nearly to the ground and stays down tight,even in a strong wind. The dual shell design keeps water from wicking onto items touching the interior sides of the tent, and keeps condensation to a minimum. The now ubiquitous "bathtub" bottom really works; the waterproof poly floor stayed dry even when we recently pitched the tent in a mud puddle. You can get plenty of air going through the tent when it warms up by controlling the upper vent and front door openings. The lack of "windows" doesn't seem to matter much with this tent; the glow provided by the bright over-fly provides light even on a gloomy day (and on a recent trip to the Trinity Alps, allowed enough starlight through to be able to see in the middle of the night). The vestibule is big enough to be truly practical; a couple of day packs and pairs of shoes can be stored there leaving plenty of access room for the main tent (note, however, that the vestibule does not have a built-in floor; a piece of tarp will help keep your items dry).

Updated 6/2011:

We're now in our second season with the Hooligan, and it's proven to be a good choice for us. We've used it in Lassen
N.P., the Trinity Alps and throughout the California Redwoods. It's not the easiest tent to set up, but it handles
weather, is small enough to place in a relatively tiny area, yet the vestibule is big enough to be truly useful as a mud
room, dressing room, and storage area. Experience indicates that the aquisition of strong, steel stakes is essential to
insure successful performance of the tent. This is a relatively minimal additional investment to make in order to obtain
an inexpensive but tough shelter capable of standing up to variable weather conditions. For the reasons I stated above, I
stand by my original rating for the tent; it's just not going to be satisfactory for everyone. But for those who need or
want that next step up and are willing to invest some extra effort in set-up and tear down, a solid value at the price.

data.title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
data.description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
data.reviewText: You would think after all the complaints of a leaky tap that they would fix it.  That's what I was counting on however I was wrong.  Its ashame that the dispenser cap cost almost as much as the cooler.

data.title: Coleman 0°F Mummy Sleeping Bag for Big and Tall Adults | North Rim Cold-Weather Sleeping Bag
data.reviewText: I purchased two Coleman mummy bags for my boys Scout camping trips.  I trusted the Coleman brand and these bags seemed right for the price.  They kept the boys warm and dry all year round.  During the winter months (yes, Boy Scouts camp out during the winter too) I took them off the ground and had them sleep on a cot inside their tent.  The bags worked well even in the low teens and snow.  The only complaint I had with these bags was their compressibility.  Because of their materials and thickness, they do not compress well.  Many times I found them sticking half-out of a compression sack after a campout.  But once home, they can be rolled out and properly rolled back into the sack.  My opinion, if you want an affordable, durable sleeping bag for camping, this is a good choice.
data.text:

Coleman 0°F Mummy Sleeping Bag for Big and Tall Adults | North Rim Cold-Weather Sleeping Bag

DESCRIPTION:


REVIEW:
I purchased two Coleman mummy bags for my boys Scout camping trips.  I trusted the Coleman brand and these bags seemed right for the price.  They kept the boys warm and dry all year round. The bags worked well even in the low teens and snow.  The only complaint I had with these bags was their compressibility.  Because of their materials and thickness, they do not compress well.  Many times I found them sticking half-out of a compression sack after a campout.  But once home, they can be rolled out and properly rolled back into the sack.  My opinion, if you want an affordable, durable sleeping bag for camping, this is a good choice.

data.title: Coleman Folding Shovel
data.description: Dig a fire pit and cut small branches at the campsite with a Coleman Folding Shovel. This compact tool is made of durable steel with a serrated edge for sawing. It measures 10 in. (25.4 cm) when it's folded and fits inside a backpack. When you're ready to use it, the shovel folds out to 23 in. (58.42 cm) and easily locks into place for safety. During the winter, keep the shovel in your trunk to clear snow and ice in an emergency.

data.reviewText: I was shopping around for a mess kit for 4 BOB's. I figured Colman big name associated I'm my book with quality. I was very disappointed with the quality. Some of the edges were a bit sharp. After doing research on mess kits I learned that it's the best idea to purchase aluminum mess kits. With the heat from the fire when heating liquid or food in the cup or pan the kit will warp or not be able to handle the big heat of a fire for cooking or warming. I'd spend the extra maybe 5 or 10.00 for good solid stainless steel kits.

data.title: Coleman High Stand,20.25" H x 18.75" W x 28" L
data.description: Coleman 2000020280 High Stand. Camping Furniture. Made of the highest quality materials. Camping Furniture
data.reviewText: I've used these stands for several years now for both my camping grill and stove. When all is said and done, they do work, but you have to be very careful when using them. On the plus side, they're very lightweight, pack up small, and are adjustable. The problem is they are also a bit flimsy. With no back or tabletop, your grill or stove rests gingerly across two thin beams. With the lid open and a propane tank attached, most grills and stoves are already a far cry from balanced. Toss in a rickety stand and some uneven ground and you've got a very hot balancing act on your hands. A modest bump would be enough to send your stove or grill crashing to the ground. I've had a few close calls and I'm generally pretty careful. Had my grill fall once before it was lit. Would recommend it only for those times where portability matters and there's nothing sturdier available to work with. Have since bought a Coleman Pack-Away Kitchen. It's not perfect, but the beams are definitely more secure and stable.

data.title: Coleman Broadband Mesh Quad Camping Chair
data.description: Bring a comfortable seat with you to the campsite with the Coleman Broadband Mesh Quad Camping Chair. Ideal for camping, picnics, or backyard barbeques, this versatile folding chair offers premium comfort in a roomy yet portable design. It features a breathable mesh back that allows the breeze to cool your back on hot days, plus an integrated mesh cup holder that keeps a refreshing beverage within your reach. Designed to ensure your comfort, the chair features an extra-wide 21.3-inch seat that offers plenty of room to lounge and adjustable arms that let you customize the chair to suit your preferences. The strong steel frame can support up to 250 pounds and withstands seasons of heavy use. And thanks to its collapsible design and convenient carrying bag, this folding chair is easy to store and transport.
data.reviewText: Great economical camp chair pretty light.

data.title: Coleman 2-Person Pop-Up Tent
data.description: Great for festivals and car camping, the Coleman 2-Person Pop-Up Tent sets up in as little as 10 seconds, letting you get back to the fun. This camping tent is uniquely designed with preassembled poles, allowing the tent to pop into place in a snap. The taped floor seams keep you dry even when the ground is soaking wet, while the multi-position rainfly allows you to find the perfect balance of protection and ventilation. This instant tent offers room for two camp pads and comes with two integrated storage pockets to help you keep small items organized. This 2-person tent folds flat for simple storage and is lightweight for easy portability.
data.reviewText:
WAAAAAY too small to be rated for 2 people.  Maybe if you can stand sleeping on top of somebody all night then you're ok.  But I'm only good for that kind of thing for 15, 20 minutes TOPS.

Other than that, it's a cool tent.  Quality seems nice.  I'd recommend throwing it away from you when you pop it, otherwise you're gonna get a high velocity face full of tent followed by a bunch of fellow campers laughing at you, then bringing it up constantly for the duration of your trip.

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.

data.title: Coleman Camping Coffee Maker
data.description: Easily make drip-brewed coffee while enjoying the outdoors with the Coleman Camping Coffee Maker. Designed with a steel base that fits over most 2- and 3-burner camp stoves, this camping coffee maker features a large reservoir that enables you to brew up to 10 cups of hot coffee at once. Making coffee with this outdoor coffee maker is easy. Simply spoon grounds into the removable swing-out filter basket, fill the reservoir with water, and place the coffee maker over your stove burner. Within minutes, the 10-cup glass carafe will be filled with freshly brewed coffee ready to be enjoyed in the great outdoors. A Pause 'N Serve feature lets you grab a cup before the brew cycle is finished. The Coleman Camping Coffee Maker measures 8.5 x 11 x 14 inches. About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come. END
data.reviewText: The first time we used this the plastic filter basket melted!  We followed the instructions exactly.  It was used outside, on a Coleman stove, plenty of ventilation, and the outside temperature was even in the low 50's.  Still the whole side of the plastic filter basket melted and stuck to the lid of the coffee pot.  Good thing we were paying attention to it before it caught anything on fire.

data.title: Coleman Xtreme Cooler, 28-Quart, Dark Gray/Orange/Light Gray Overmold
data.description: Keep your food and drinks cold for an extended time, and make the fun last longer, when you bring a Coleman 28 Quart Xtreme Cooler. Xtreme technology uses an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls to keep your items cold for up to 3 days. Large enough to hold 36 cans, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for the entire party. Whether you're headed on a long weekend camping trip, a roadtrip, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip no-crush handles make getting to your destination easy and comfortable. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax, and the cup holders molded into the lid will keep your cold drink close while you enjoy the day.
data.reviewText:
I have mixed feelings about this cooler.  I have a big one and love it, but wanted a smaller one for weekend campouts so i was excited about a 28 Quart.  First the good,  I LOVE the size, and holding a 2 litter great.  I freeze 3 or 4 full of water and it keeps everything cool without getting wet all weekend. 3 days plus and I still have ice that way, and you can drink the cool water if need be. My pic is on day 3.

The bad.  The handle is CRAP. Complete crap! Someone thought a single handle with a soft grip would be nice.  To get the grip, they carve out over HALF of the plastic and destroy the structural integrity of the handle.  It flexes in the middle under weight and i'm sure it will break eventually.  This cooler deserves two side handles like the other Xtremes! Coleman fix it!!!  In the same size Marine cooler they left the handle solid and its much stronger.

data.title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
data.description: The Coleman Sundome 6, 10'x10', dome tent is part of the classic Sundome series of tents from Coleman. The Sundome 6 sleeps 6 happy campers and includes a large door for easy entry/exit with a hooded fly to provide ventilation even in rain. A large rear hooded window also provides airflow for cross ventilation. Mesh vents on the roof provide additional ventilation and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against a leaky floor. The coated polyester fabrics on the fly and walls are rugged and durable. Like all Coleman Sundome tents it is easy to set up or take down and features an Electrical Access Port, a ground vent and an interior mesh storage pocket.Freestanding design is easy to set up and take down.2 windows and roof vents for maximum ventilation and comfort.Mesh roof aids in ventilation and reduces condensation.Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.Polyester fly is tough and durable and won't stretch when wet.1 zippered Electrical Access Port to run AC power into the tent.1 vented Cool Air Port for increased ventilation and comfort.1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient clip and continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import.
data.reviewText:
I do NOT understand all of the positive reviews here. Are we looking at the same tent? How....

Let me start by saying my previous tent of 25 years was a Eureka, and maybe that has spoiled me for all other tents. I bought this thing to replace it when it finally died (word to the wise: if you love your tent, NEVER EVER lend it to other people, I don't care how much you like them. They will kill your tent. This was my fatal mistake, and my tent was put back wet and mold killed it forever. I am DEVASTATED. I LOVED that tent. The places we went!)

I digress. I need a replacement for my beloved Eureka. The reviews on this thing were spectacular, so I bit the bullet and got one from Cabela's (they were on sale for $89 there). NOT WORTH IT!!! IT arrived today, I took it out of the box, put it up, and it's like paper. This tent won't last it's whole year warranty, let alone 25 years like my beloved Eureka did. You want to know how I know? It didn't even last the duration of my putting it up. The fabric on this piece of crap is SO thin that just putting the tent poles through the sheaths on top TORE the fabric. TORE it. I'm not rough n anything - I am very careful and gentle. But my delicate flower like touch RIPPED the sheath through which the poles went, and why? Because they are essentially made of paper.

NO WAY I'd trust this tent in a rainstorm - all the seam sealer in the world wouldn't make this thing sturdy enough to handle any weather. The sun will shred this fabric in a minute.

This is like...this is a disposable tent. I want a tent that will last me, like my beloved Eureka. There is a reason they have lifetime warranties. Lesson learned, I won't waste my money on anything else again. I'm willing to spend three times as much on a tent that will last a million times longer.

What a piece of junk. If you just want a crap tent that you can take camping once, by all means throw away your hard-earned money on this embarrassment to tents. If you want something that will last you and protect you and give you thousands of happy memories over the next couple of decades, get a Eureka.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman Sundome 6, 10'x10', dome tent is part of the classic Sundome series of tents from Coleman. The Sundome 6 sleeps 6 happy campers and includes a large door for easy entry/exit with a hooded fly to provide ventilation even in rain. A large rear hooded window also provides airflow for cross ventilation. Mesh vents on the roof provide additional ventilation and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against a leaky floor. The coated polyester fabrics on the fly and walls are rugged and durable. Like all Coleman Sundome tents it is easy to set up or take down and features an Electrical Access Port, a ground vent and an interior mesh storage pocket.Freestanding design is easy to set up and take down.2 windows and roof vents for maximum ventilation and comfort.Mesh roof aids in ventilation and reduces condensation.Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.Polyester fly is tough and durable and won't stretch when wet.1 zippered Electrical Access Port to run AC power into the tent.1 vented Cool Air Port for increased ventilation and comfort.1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient clip and continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import.

REVIEW:
I do NOT understand all of the positive reviews here. Are we looking at the same tent? How....

Let me start by saying my previous tent of 25 years was a Eureka, and maybe that has spoiled me for all other tents. I bought this thing to replace it when it finally died (word to the wise: if you love your tent, NEVER EVER lend it to other people, I don't care how much you like them. They will kill your tent. This was my fatal mistake, and my tent was put back wet and mold killed it forever. I am DEVASTATED. I LOVED that tent. The places we went!)

I digress. I need a replacement for my beloved Eureka. The reviews on this thing were spectacular, so I bit the bullet and got one from Cabela's (they were on sale for $89 there). NOT WORTH IT!!! IT arrived today, I took it out of the box, put it up, and it's like paper. This tent won't last it's whole year warranty, let alone 25 years like my beloved Eureka did. You want to know how I know? It didn't even last the duration of my putting it up. The fabric on this piece of crap is SO thin that just putting the tent poles through the sheaths on top TORE the fabric. TORE it. I'm not rough n anything - I am very careful and gentle. But my delicate flower like touch RIPPED the sheath through which the poles went, and why? Because they are essentially made of paper.

NO WAY I'd trust this tent in a rainstorm - all the seam sealer in the world wouldn't make this thing sturdy enough to handle any weather. The sun will shred this fabric in a minute.

This is like...this is a disposable tent. I want a tent that will last me, like my beloved Eureka. There is a reason they have lifetime warranties. Lesson learned, I won't waste my money on anything else again. I'm willing to spend three times as much on a tent that will last a million times longer.

What a piece of junk. If you just want a crap tent that you can take camping once, by all means throw away your hard-earned money on this embarrassment to tents. If you want something that will last you and protect you and give you thousands of happy memories over the next couple of decades, get a Eureka.

data.title: Coleman Tent Kit
data.description: Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.
data.reviewText: The rubber mallet and stakes are good.  They both worked more than adequately even in the rocky ground where first used.  Remainder of the items are really not worth the price.  Broom/pan are really small and cheap plastic.  Stake puller may be OK in softer ground, but easily bent the first time it was used pulling stakes.  Should say, though, that stakes were driven in pretty rocky ground and rather hard to pull, so puller may work OK in softer ground.  Overall, item is probably overpriced for what you get.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman Tent Kit

DESCRIPTION:
Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.

REVIEW:
The rubber mallet and stakes are good.  They both worked more than adequately even in the rocky ground where first used.  Remainder of the items are really not worth the price.  Broom/pan are really small and cheap plastic.  Stake puller may be OK in softer ground, but easily bent the first time it was used pulling stakes.  Should say, though, that stakes were driven in pretty rocky ground and rather hard to pull, so puller may work OK in softer ground.  Overall, item is probably overpriced for what you get.

data.title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
data.description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
data.reviewText: Purchased for hubby's lunch tote, to replace his worn out one. It's smaller than his original tote. Too costly to return, so we will keep it for traveling in our car or trips outside for a picnic. Only large enough to carry a couple of bottles of water and a few snacks.
data.text:
TITLE:
Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner

DESCRIPTION:
Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.

REVIEW:
Purchased for hubby's lunch tote, to replace his worn out one. It's smaller than his original tote. Too costly to return, so we will keep it for traveling in our car or trips outside for a picnic. Only large enough to carry a couple of bottles of water and a few snacks.

data.title: Coleman Woodsman II Chair
data.description: The Coleman Woodsman II Chair is a lightweight folding chair perfect for camping, sporting events and more.Unfolded Dimensions: 15.5 in. x 17.5 in. 27.5 in. (39.4 cm x 44.5 cm x 69.9 cm).Folded Dimensions: 22 in. x 17.5 in. x 3 in. (55.9 cm x 44.5 cm x 7.6 cm).
data.reviewText: Hard to find a light portable and still comfortable camp chair that is small for packing.  Don't let little ones kneel on them. Ours put her knee though the seat the first time out with it.

data.title: Coleman Micro Packer LED Lantern, 100 L
data.description: Grab a lantern and a flashlight in the palm of your hand with the Coleman MicroPacker LED Lantern. The bright, 2-in-1 design snaps from area lighting to directional lighting quite literally in a snap. The bright LED lasts a lifetime, so it never needs to be replaced. The versatile light lasts up to 92 hours, and an extremely lightweight design is only 6 oz.-even with the three AA-cell batteries that power the lantern.
data.reviewText:
DOES NOT convert to a flashlight. No way, no how. Just like the old Coleman gasoline lanterns had a metal reflector that could shield one side? This has an inch-wide black (!) plastic barrier that slides up, so it doesn't shine back in your eyes. That doesn't make it into a "flashlight" in any way. It doesn't focus or concentrate the light in any way. It just blocks a narrow strip from your eyes.

Other than that, it looks decently made, and the 3xAA-or-1xD battery feature is nice. But I was expecting something like the Eveready lights, that pop up to be a lantern, and pop down to be a focused flashlight. This isn't/

data.title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
data.description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
data.reviewText:
A very handy grill.
We use it every trip in our motor home.
Seems to get dirtier than most other grills but it's not hard to clean.
Knocked off 1 star because the propane bottles do not line up correctly with the bracket that holds them.
I'm not going to use the expensive small bottles after I run out so it won't matter.
I bought a hose so I can use a regular propane tank.

DESCRIPTION:
Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up.
